Business justification: Pipeline throughput records track volumes flowing from production facilities through pipelines. The production_facility is the receipt/origin point for pipeline throughput — required for production-to']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Pipeline throughput generates transportation revenue that must be tracked by profit center for midstream segment reporting and pipeline profitability analysis.']
  api_gravity decimal(5,2) [note: 'The API gravity of the crude oil or liquid hydrocarbon product measured during this throughput period, used for volume correction and quality verification.']
  co2_content_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The carbon dioxide content in natural gas expressed as a percentage by volume, affecting heating value and pipeline corrosion considerations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this throughput record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for tariff rates and revenue calculations, typically USD for US pipeline operations.']
  ferc_tariff_code varchar [note: 'The FERC-approved tariff code or rate schedule identifier applicable to this throughput transaction, used for regulatory compliance and billing.']
  fuel_gas_used_volume decimal(18,3) [note: 'The volume of natural gas consumed as fuel to power compressor stations and other pipeline equipment during the transportation process.']
  gas_heating_value_btu_per_scf decimal(8,2) [note: 'The heating value or energy content of natural gas measured in BTU per standard cubic foot, used for energy-based billing and quality specifications.']
  gas_specific_gravity decimal(6,4) [note: 'The specific gravity of natural gas relative to air, used for volume correction and flow calculations in gas pipeline operations.']
  h2s_content_ppm decimal(8,2) [note: 'The concentration of hydrogen sulfide in natural gas measured in parts per million, critical for safety and quality specifications.']
  imbalance_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the volume imbalance for this shipper and measurement period, indicating whether the shipper is in balance, has over-delivered, or under-delivered relative to their nomination.']
  imbalance_volume decimal(18,3) [note: 'The difference between the shippers nominated volume and the actual metered volume delivered, used for imbalance management and settlement calculations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this throughput record was last updated or modified, used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  line_loss_volume decimal(18,3) [note: 'The volume lost during transportation through the pipeline segment, calculated as the difference between receipt and delivery volumes adjusted for fuel gas and shrinkage. Negative values indicate line gain.']
  measurement_period_type varchar [note: 'The time granularity of the throughput measurement: daily for operational monitoring, monthly for regulatory reporting and billing cycles.']
  metered_delivery_volume decimal(18,3) [note: 'The actual volume measured at the delivery or outlet measurement point of the pipeline segment, as recorded by custody transfer meters.']
  metered_receipt_volume decimal(18,3) [note: 'The actual volume measured at the receipt or inlet measurement point of the pipeline segment, as recorded by custody transfer meters.']
  metered_volume_uom varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the metered receipt and delivery volumes, standardized to volume units rather than rate units.']
  nominated_volume decimal(18,3) [note: 'The volume that the shipper nominated or scheduled for transportation through the pipeline segment during this period, expressed in the appropriate unit of measure.']
  nominated_volume_uom varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the nominated volume. Common units include barrels (BBL), barrels of oil per day (BOPD), thousand cubic feet (MCF), million cubic feet per day (MMCFD). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bbl|bopd|mcf|mcfd|mmcf|mmcfd|m3|gal — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  observed_pressure decimal(8,2) [note: 'The pressure of the product at the measurement point during throughput measurement, used for volume correction to standard conditions and flow calculations.']
  observed_temperature decimal(6,2) [note: 'The temperature of the product at the measurement point during throughput measurement, used for volume correction to standard conditions.']
  operator_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for pipeline operators to record comments, explanations for variances, operational issues, or other relevant information about this throughput measurement.']
  phmsa_pipeline_code varchar [note: 'The PHMSA-assigned identifier for the pipeline system, required for regulatory reporting and safety compliance tracking.']
  pressure_uom varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for pressure readings, such as pounds per square inch (PSI), bar, or kilopascals (kPa).']
  product_type varchar [note: 'The category of hydrocarbon product transported through the pipeline segment during this measurement period. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: crude_oil|natural_gas|ngl|lng|lpg|refined_products|condensate — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the throughput reconciliation process between nominated and actual volumes, indicating whether the record is pending review, reconciled, or under dispute.']
  scada_integration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this throughput record was automatically integrated from the SCADA pipeline monitoring system or manually entered. True indicates SCADA integration.']
  shrinkage_volume decimal(18,3) [note: 'The volume reduction due to extraction of natural gas liquids (NGLs) or other processing activities during transportation, typically applicable to natural gas pipelines.']
  sulfur_content_percent decimal(5,3) [note: 'The sulfur content of crude oil or refined products expressed as a percentage by weight, critical for quality specifications and environmental compliance.']
  tariff_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'The transportation rate charged to the shipper for moving product through this pipeline segment, expressed per unit of volume. Used for revenue calculation and shipper billing.']
  tariff_rate_uom varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the tariff rate, typically dollars per barrel for liquids or dollars per thousand cubic feet for gas.']
  temperature_uom varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for temperature readings, typically Fahrenheit in US operations or Celsius in international operations.']
  throughput_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the throughput volumes were measured. For monthly aggregations, this represents the last day of the reporting month.']
  throughput_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the throughput measurement record, used for external reporting and reconciliation with shippers and regulatory bodies.']
  water_content_lbs_per_mmcf decimal(8,3) [note: 'The water vapor content in natural gas measured in pounds per million cubic feet, critical for preventing hydrate formation and pipeline corrosion.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the measured daily or monthly throughput volumes on each pipeline segment, reconciling nominated versus actual volumes for each shipper. Records throughput date, pipeline segment ID, shipper ID, product type, nominated volume (BOPD or MMCFD), metered receipt volume, metered delivery volume, line loss or gain, fuel gas used, shrinkage, imbalance volume, and measurement point references. Supports FERC Form 6 reporting, shipper imbalance management, and tariff revenue calculation.'
}

  nominated_volume_mmbtu decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned energy content of the LNG cargo expressed in million British thermal units.']
  propane_content_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of propane in the LNG cargo composition.']
  quality_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the independent inspection certificate documenting the LNG cargo quality specifications.']
  regasification_rate_mmcfd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Planned rate at which the LNG cargo will be converted back to gaseous state at the receiving terminal, expressed in million cubic feet per day.']
  scheduled_discharge_date date [note: 'Planned date for the LNG cargo discharge operations to commence at the receiving terminal.']
  scheduled_loading_date date [note: 'Planned date for the LNG cargo loading operations to commence at the export terminal.']
  specific_gravity decimal(6,4) [note: 'Ratio of the density of the LNG cargo to the density of air at standard conditions, used for interchangeability calculations.']
  wobbe_index decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measure of the interchangeability of fuel gases such as natural gas, calculated as the heating value divided by the square root of the specific gravity.']

  Note: 'Master data entity representing a discrete LNG cargo from liquefaction to regasification, tracking the full LNG supply chain. Captures cargo reference, LNG train or liquefaction facility, loading terminal, receiving terminal, vessel (LNG carrier), scheduled loading date, actual loading date, cargo volume (MMBTU and CBM), boil-off gas (BOG) estimate, cargo quality (methane content, Wobbe index, GCV), heel volume, discharge terminal, regasification rate, and cargo status. Supports LNG trading, shipping, and gas quality management.'
}

Business justification: Fiscal measurement points are contractually designated in commercial term contracts as the custody transfer location. Linking measurement_point to commercial_term_contract enables contract-specific me']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commercial contract or transportation agreement governing the use of this measurement point for custody transfer and tariff calculations.']
  delivery_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_point. Business justification: Measurement points physically define or co-locate with customer delivery points in pipeline and terminal operations. Used in gas balancing, custody metering, and nomination confirmation. Domain expert']
  emission_source_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.emission_source. Business justification: Emission sources (flare stacks, vents, fugitive sources) are physically located at measurement points (meter stations, custody transfer points). EPA GHG reporting and LDAR programs require linking emi']
  exploration_well_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.exploration_well. Business justification: Fiscal measurement points are installed at exploration well test sites for DST/EWT metering. Regulatory bodies and PSA cost recovery audits require linking each measurement point to the exploration we']
  lease_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.lease. Business justification: Measurement points (LACT units, lease meters) are often installed at lease custody transfer points. Tying meters to leases enables automated production allocation, royalty calculation, and regulatory ']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Measurement points require calibration permits and custody transfer approvals. Meter records reference the permit authorizing fiscal measurement. Real-world operations link each measurement point to i']
  pipeline_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.pipeline_segment. Business justification: Fiscal meters and PHMSA-regulated measurement points are physically installed at specific pipeline segment boundaries. Linking measurement_point to pipeline_segment is required for tariff billing reco']
  terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.terminal. Business justification: A measurement point is physically located at a terminal (marine terminal, pipeline terminal, or storage terminal). Linking measurement_point to terminal establishes the physical location hierarchy: te']
  base_pressure_psi decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard reference pressure in PSI used for volume correction calculations when converting observed volumes to standard conditions, typically 14.73 psia for natural gas.']
  base_temperature_fahrenheit decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard reference temperature in degrees Fahrenheit used for volume correction calculations when converting observed volumes to standard conditions, typically 60°F for petroleum products.']
  calibration_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the official calibration certificate issued by the calibration authority or service provider for audit trail and compliance verification.']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'Next scheduled date by which the measurement point must be calibrated or proven to maintain accuracy certification and regulatory compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this measurement point record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  custody_transfer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this measurement point is used for custody transfer (change of ownership) requiring higher accuracy standards and independent verification per API MPMS standards.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date when the measurement point was permanently taken out of service and decommissioned, used for asset lifecycle tracking and historical analysis.']
  ferc_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this measurement point is subject to FERC tariff reporting requirements for interstate natural gas or oil pipeline transportation.']
  fiscal_measurement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this measurement point is used for fiscal (revenue) purposes requiring enhanced accuracy, audit trails, and regulatory compliance for royalty and tax calculations.']
  flow_direction varchar [note: 'Directional flow capability of the measurement point: forward (single direction), reverse (opposite direction), or bidirectional (both directions).']
  flow_rate_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for flow rate specifications: barrels per day (BOPD), barrels per hour, thousand cubic feet per day (MCFD), cubic meters per hour, or gallons per minute.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the measurement point was originally installed and commissioned for operational use.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent calibration or proving was performed on the measurement point, establishing the baseline for accuracy certification.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this measurement point record was most recently updated, used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Type of facility or infrastructure where the measurement point is physically installed: terminal, pipeline, wellhead, FPSO (Floating Production Storage and Offloading), refinery, or processing plant.']
  maximum_flow_rate decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum rated flow rate capacity of the meter before accuracy degradation or equipment damage, used for operational limits and safety management.']
  measurement_point_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier or code assigned to the measurement point for custody transfer documentation and regulatory reporting.']
  measurement_point_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the measurement point for operational identification and reporting purposes.']
  meter_factor decimal(10,6) [note: 'Calibration correction factor (K-factor) applied to raw meter readings to calculate accurate volume, derived from proving runs and used in custody transfer calculations.']
  meter_manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured the metering device, used for warranty management, spare parts procurement, and technical support.']
  meter_model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model or part number of the metering device for technical specifications reference and maintenance procedures.']
  meter_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number of the physical metering device for asset tracking, maintenance scheduling, and calibration history.']
  meter_size_inches decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nominal diameter of the meter in inches, indicating the flow capacity and pipeline connection size.']
  meter_type varchar [note: 'Technology type of the metering device installed at this measurement point: turbine, ultrasonic, Coriolis, orifice, positive displacement, or vortex meter.']
  minimum_flow_rate decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum measurable flow rate for the meter to maintain accuracy within specified uncertainty limits, used for operational planning and custody transfer validation.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the measurement point: active (in service), inactive (temporarily offline), maintenance (under repair/calibration), out of service (not operational), or decommissioned (permanently retired).']
  phmsa_meter_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by PHMSA for pipeline meters subject to federal safety regulations and reporting requirements under 49 CFR Part 195.']
  point_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the measurement point based on its operational purpose: fiscal meter (revenue measurement), allocation meter (production allocation), check meter (verification), tank gauge (inventory), wellhead meter (well production), or custody transfer meter (ownership transfer).']
  pressure_compensation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the measurement point applies pressure compensation to convert observed volumes to standard conditions for custody transfer calculations.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Type of petroleum product being measured at this point: crude oil, natural gas, LNG (Liquefied Natural Gas), LPG (Liquefied Petroleum Gas), NGL (Natural Gas Liquids), refined products, or condensate. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: crude_oil|natural_gas|lng|lpg|ngl|refined_products|condensate — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  proving_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calibrate and prove the accuracy of the meter: pipe prover, tank prover, master meter comparison, or volumetric prover, as defined by API MPMS Chapter 4.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, operational considerations, or historical context related to the measurement point.']
  scada_integration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this measurement point is integrated with SCADA systems for real-time monitoring, data acquisition, and operational control.']
  scada_tag_code varchar [note: 'Unique tag identifier in the SCADA or PI System historian used to retrieve real-time and historical measurement data for this point.']
  temperature_compensation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the measurement point applies temperature compensation to convert observed volumes to standard conditions for custody transfer calculations.']
  uncertainty_percentage decimal(5,3) [note: 'Statistical uncertainty or tolerance of the measurement point expressed as a percentage, representing the accuracy range for volume measurements used in custody transfer calculations.']

  Note: 'Master data entity representing a physical or fiscal measurement location where petroleum product volumes and qualities are measured for custody transfer, allocation, or regulatory reporting. Captures measurement point ID, point name, point type (fiscal meter, allocation meter, check meter, tank gauge), location (terminal, pipeline, wellhead, FPSO), product type, meter type (turbine, ultrasonic, Coriolis, orifice), meter serial number, calibration due date, last calibration date, uncertainty percentage, PHMSA meter ID, and operational status. Serves as the SSOT for measurement infrastructure.'
}

Table logistics.vessel_schedule_assignment {
  vessel_schedule_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this vessel schedule assignment record. Primary key.']
  shipping_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to logistics.shipping_schedule. Links this assignment to the parent shipping schedule.']
  vessel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to logistics.vessel. Links this assignment to the assigned vessel.']
  assigned_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the marine logistics planner or scheduler who made this vessel assignment decision.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Date when this vessel was assigned to the shipping schedule by the marine logistics planner. Tracks when the assignment decision was made.']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing specific considerations, constraints, or special instructions for this vessel assignment (e.g., cargo compatibility, port restrictions, weather considerations).']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this vessel assignment. Values: proposed (initial planning), confirmed (vessel committed), in_progress (loading underway), completed (lifting finished), cancelled (assignment cancelled), substituted (vessel replaced).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this vessel schedule assignment record was first created. Audit trail.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this vessel schedule assignment record was last updated. Audit trail for tracking changes to assignments.']
  laycan_end_date date [note: 'Latest date of the laytime cancelling date range (laycan) by which the vessel must arrive at the loading port. Failure to arrive within laycan may allow charterer to cancel.']
  laycan_start_date date [note: 'Earliest date of the laytime cancelling date range (laycan) during which the vessel must arrive at the loading port. Part of charter party terms.']
  planned_loading_date date [note: 'Planned date when this vessel is scheduled to commence loading operations for this shipping schedule. Critical for berth planning and terminal coordination.']
  planned_volume_bbl decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned cargo volume in barrels that this vessel is assigned to lift under this shipping schedule. Used for capacity planning and entitlement allocation.']
  vessel_role varchar [note: 'Role of the vessel in this shipping schedule assignment. Values: primary_lifter (main vessel for scheduled liftings), backup_vessel (contingency vessel), spot_charter (short-term charter), term_charter (long-term charter agreement).']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational assignment between a shipping schedule and a vessel. It captures the specific assignment of a vessel to execute liftings within a consolidated shipping schedule for a given planning horizon. Each record links one shipping schedule to one vessel with attributes that exist only in the context of this assignment, including assignment dates, vessel role, planned loading dates, planned volumes, and assignment status.. Existence Justification: In oil and gas marine logistics, a shipping schedule is a consolidated master plan covering multiple vessel movements across a planning horizon (monthly/quarterly), and each vessel can participate in multiple shipping schedules over time. The business actively manages vessel schedule assignments as operational records with specific assignment dates, vessel roles, planned loading dates, planned volumes, and assignment statuses. The explicit presence of number_of_vessels_assigned on shipping_schedule confirms that schedules routinely involve multiple vessels, and vessels are reused across different schedules.'
}

Table logistics.schedule_port_call {
  schedule_port_call_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this schedule port call. Primary key.']
  shipping_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the parent shipping schedule that governs this port call']
  terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the terminal where this port call will occur']
  berth_assignment varchar [note: 'Specific berth or loading position assigned at the terminal for this port call (e.g., Berth 3, North Dock)']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this schedule port call record was first created in the system']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this schedule port call record was last modified']
  planned_arrival_date timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the vessel is scheduled to arrive at this terminal for loading or discharge operations']
  planned_departure_date timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the vessel is scheduled to depart from this terminal after completing cargo operations']
  planned_volume_bbl decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned cargo volume in barrels to be loaded or discharged at this terminal during this port call']
  port_call_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of this port call within the shipping schedule (1 for first port, 2 for second, etc.)']
  port_call_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this port call indicating its execution state']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational port call event between a shipping schedule and a terminal. It captures the planned vessel arrival, departure, berth assignment, and volume for each terminal included in a multi-port shipping schedule. Each record links one shipping schedule to one terminal with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific port call within the schedule.. Existence Justification: In oil and gas marine logistics, a shipping schedule is a consolidated master plan that encompasses multiple port calls across different terminals during a planning horizon (monthly or quarterly). Each shipping schedule includes planned vessel movements to multiple terminals for cargo liftings, and each terminal hosts multiple shipping schedules over time. The business actively manages schedule port calls as operational planning entities with specific arrival/departure dates, berth assignments, and planned volumes for each terminal visit within a schedule.'
}

Table customer.counterparty {
  counterparty_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each customer or counterparty master record in the oil and gas value chain. Serves as the primary key for the SSOT of all counterparty identity data. Entity role: MASTER_PARTY.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Counterparties are registered within specific legal entities for tax jurisdiction, intercompany trading rules, and consolidation. Essential for JV partner accounting and transfer pricing in oil & gas ']
  royalty_owner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.royalty_owner. Business justification: Royalty owners who sell their production back to the operator become commercial counterparties. Links ownership interest records to commercial sales agreements and pricing terms for equity crude marke']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Master data linkage for entities that are both customers and vendors (common in oil & gas for JV partners, trading houses, integrated operators). Business process: unified KYC/sanctions screening, con']
  venture_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venture.partner. Business justification: Counterparties often hold dual roles as venture partners and commercial customers. Critical for consolidated KYC, sanctions screening, credit risk assessment, and integrated relationship management ac']
  account_group_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA Customer Account Group code that controls the field selection, number range, and partner functions for this counterparty in the SD and FI modules. Examples include domestic customers, export customers, one-time customers, and intercompany customers. Drives master data screen layout and business process routing.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the counterparty account. \'pending_kyc\' indicates Know Your Customer (KYC) verification is in progress. \'blocked\' indicates credit or compliance hold. \'suspended\' indicates temporary restriction pending review. Drives order-to-cash and procure-to-pay eligibility.']
  counterparty_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the counterparty within the oil and gas value chain. Distinguishes International Oil Companies (IOCs), National Oil Companies (NOCs), independent traders, national refiners, wholesale buyers, industrial consumers, retail distributors, and contract counterparties. Drives commercial terms, credit policies, and reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IOC|NOC|independent_trader|refiner|wholesale_buyer|industrial_consumer|retail_distributor|contract_counterparty — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this counterparty master record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX Section 404, COPAS record-keeping standards, and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  credit_classification varchar [note: 'Internal credit classification of the counterparty based on external credit ratings and internal risk assessment. Drives credit limit assignment, payment terms, collateral requirements, and exposure management in oil and gas commodity trading and supply chain. Aligns with SAP Credit Management (FI-AR-CR) classifications.']
  credit_limit_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum approved credit exposure in US Dollars (USD) for this counterparty across all outstanding transactions. Enforced in SAP Credit Management (FI-AR-CR) to control order-to-cash risk in petroleum product sales, LNG offtake, and petrochemical supply agreements.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric credit rating assigned by the designated credit rating agency (e.g., \'AAA\', \'Baa2\', \'BB+\'). Used to determine counterparty creditworthiness, set credit limits, and manage financial exposure in crude oil trading, LNG offtake agreements, and downstream supply contracts.']
  credit_rating_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the external credit rating agency that issued the counterparty\'s credit rating (e.g., Moody\'s, S&P, Fitch). \'internal\' indicates the rating is based solely on Oil Gas\'s internal credit assessment model. Used alongside credit_rating to determine credit exposure limits.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the primary transaction currency agreed with this counterparty (e.g., \'USD\' for US Dollar, \'EUR\' for Euro). Drives foreign exchange (FX) exposure management and financial reporting under IFRS/GAAP.']
  customer_classification_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA customer classification code (Nielsen classification) used for statistical analysis, pricing strategy, and market segmentation of petroleum product customers. Supports downstream retail and B2B wholesale analytics.']
  distribution_channel_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA Distribution Channel code defining how petroleum products are sold and delivered to this counterparty (e.g., wholesale, retail, direct, intercompany). Drives pricing condition determination and order management workflows in the SD module.']
  domicile_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country of legal domicile or principal place of business for the counterparty. Drives jurisdiction-specific regulatory compliance, sanctions screening, withholding tax rates, and export control requirements under OFAC and EAR.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit Data Universal Numbering System (DUNS) number assigned by Dun & Bradstreet for global business entity identification. Used for credit risk assessment, supplier qualification, and counterparty due diligence in oil and gas procurement and JV management.']
  incorporation_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction where the legal entity was incorporated or registered. May differ from domicile country for multinational IOCs and NOCs operating through holding structures. Used for legal entity hierarchy mapping and PSA compliance.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the default delivery and risk transfer conditions for petroleum product shipments with this counterparty. Critical for crude oil, LNG, LPG, and refined product logistics, insurance, and title transfer determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB|CIF|CFR|DAP|DDP|FCA|EXW|CPT|CIP|DAT — promote to reference product]']
  industry_segment varchar [note: 'Oil and gas value chain segment in which the counterparty primarily operates. Upstream covers Exploration and Production (E&P); midstream covers transportation and storage; downstream covers refining and marketing; petrochemicals covers chemical manufacturing. Used for commercial segmentation and pricing strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: upstream|midstream|downstream|petrochemicals|utilities|government|trading — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Know Your Customer (KYC) and Anti-Money Laundering (AML) due diligence process for this counterparty. \'verified\' indicates completed and approved KYC. \'expired\' indicates KYC documentation has lapsed and renewal is required. Mandatory for all counterparties under OFAC, FinCEN, and international AML regulations.']
  kyc_verified_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent KYC/AML verification was completed and approved for this counterparty. Used to track KYC renewal cycles and ensure compliance with periodic re-verification requirements under OFAC and FinCEN regulations.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this counterparty master record. Used for change data capture (CDC), data lineage, and audit compliance under SOX and COPAS record-keeping requirements.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full registered legal name of the counterparty or customer as recorded in the jurisdiction of incorporation. Used for contracts, invoices, Joint Interest Billing (JIB), and regulatory filings. Corresponds to SAP BP field \'Name 1\'.']
  lei_code varchar [note: '20-character alphanumeric Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) issued under ISO 17442 by a Local Operating Unit (LOU) of the Global LEI System (GLEIF). Mandatory for counterparties engaged in financial instrument trading and derivatives under EMIR/Dodd-Frank. Critical for IOC and NOC counterparty identification in commodity trading.']
  net_revenue_interest_pct decimal(8,6) [note: 'Default Net Revenue Interest (NRI) percentage applicable to this counterparty for revenue distribution calculations in joint venture and production sharing arrangements. Expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.750000 = 75%). Used in Quorum Land System and SAP JVA for division order processing and revenue allocation.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP payment terms key (e.g., \'NT30\', \'NT60\', \'CIA\') defining the agreed payment schedule for this counterparty. Governs invoice due dates, early payment discounts, and cash flow planning for crude oil, refined product, and petrochemical sales. Aligns with COPAS billing standards.']
  preferred_delivery_mode varchar [note: 'Counterparty\'s preferred mode of petroleum product delivery. Drives logistics planning, transportation contract selection, and SCADA/DCS integration for pipeline nominations. Relevant for crude oil, refined products, LNG, LPG, and NGL delivery scheduling.']
  price_group_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA price group code that groups counterparties for pricing condition determination in petroleum product sales. Enables differentiated pricing strategies for IOCs, NOCs, traders, and retail distributors based on volume commitments and commercial agreements.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for commercial and operational communications with the counterparty. Used for order confirmations, invoice delivery, nomination notices, and contract correspondence in oil and gas trading and supply chain operations.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the counterparty\'s commercial or operations contact. Used for urgent operational communications including cargo nominations, delivery scheduling, and emergency HSE notifications.']
  reconciliation_account_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA General Ledger (GL) reconciliation account code for accounts receivable postings related to this counterparty. Ensures sub-ledger to GL reconciliation for petroleum product sales revenue under IFRS/GAAP and SOX financial controls.']
  registered_address_city varchar [note: 'City of the counterparty\'s official registered address. Used for jurisdictional tax determination, regulatory reporting, and legal correspondence.']
  registered_address_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the counterparty\'s registered address. Used for sanctions screening, export control, and jurisdictional compliance.']
  registered_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the counterparty\'s official registered address as recorded with the relevant commercial registry. Used for legal correspondence, contract execution, and regulatory filings.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official company registration or incorporation number issued by the jurisdiction\'s commercial registry or company registrar. Used for legal entity verification, contract execution, and regulatory compliance. Distinct from tax identifiers.']
  relationship_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the commercial relationship with this counterparty was formally terminated or is scheduled to end. Null for active ongoing relationships. Used for contract expiry management, credit exposure wind-down, and regulatory reporting.']
  relationship_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the commercial relationship with this counterparty was formally established. Used for relationship tenure analysis, contract anniversary tracking, and customer lifecycle management in oil and gas marketing and sales.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Defines the primary commercial relationship role of the counterparty with Oil Gas. A counterparty may be a customer, supplier, joint venture (JV) partner, royalty owner, government entity, or affiliate. Supports dual-role counterparties common in upstream JOA and PSA structures.']
  sales_organization_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA Sales Organization code representing the organizational unit responsible for selling petroleum products to this counterparty. Determines pricing procedures, output determination, and revenue recognition rules applicable to this customer relationship.']
  sanctions_last_screened_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent sanctions screening check performed against OFAC, EU, and UN sanctions lists for this counterparty. Used to enforce periodic re-screening requirements and demonstrate compliance audit trails.']
  sanctions_screened boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this counterparty has been screened against applicable sanctions lists including OFAC SDN List, EU Consolidated Sanctions List, and UN Security Council Sanctions. Must be TRUE before any commercial transaction is executed. Critical compliance control for international oil and gas operations.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued Tax Identification Number (TIN), Employer Identification Number (EIN), or VAT registration number used for tax reporting, withholding obligations, and regulatory filings. Required for Joint Interest Billing (JIB) and revenue distribution under COPAS standards.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Operating or \'doing business as\' (DBA) name used commercially, which may differ from the registered legal name. Relevant for marketing, distribution agreements, and downstream retail channel identification.']
  vat_registration_number varchar [note: 'Value Added Tax (VAT) or Goods and Services Tax (GST) registration number for the counterparty in their operating jurisdiction. Required for cross-border petroleum product invoicing, import/export documentation, and indirect tax compliance in international oil and gas operations.']
  working_interest_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this counterparty is eligible to hold a Working Interest (WI) in oil and gas joint ventures and Production Sharing Agreements (PSAs). Drives counterparty qualification for JOA participation, AFE approval workflows, and Joint Interest Billing (JIB) in SAP Joint Venture Accounting.']

  Note: 'Master record for all counterparties and customers in the oil and gas value chain, including IOCs, NOCs, national refiners, independent traders, wholesale buyers, industrial consumers, retail distributors, and contract counterparties. Serves as the SSOT for counterparty identity, legal entity classification, registration details, domicile jurisdiction, tax identifiers, DUNS number, LEI (Legal Entity Identifier), credit classification, and relationship type. Supports both B2B wholesale upstream/midstream/downstream segments and retail distribution channels.'
}

  account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the commercial account record in the Oil Gas enterprise data platform. Primary key for the account entity in the customer domain Silver layer.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Customer accounts must be assigned to legal entities (company codes) for financial consolidation, regulatory reporting (SEC, IFRS), and tax compliance. Core master data relationship in oil & gas multi']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Customer accounts in oil & gas track operational costs (LOE, G&A allocation) to cost centers for budgeting, variance analysis, and management reporting. Essential for account-level profitability and c']
  parent_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent account in the account hierarchy, enabling multi-level corporate group structures (e.g., subsidiary accounts rolled up to a parent holding company). Supports consolidated credit exposure management and group-level reporting.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Accounts are assigned to profit centers for segment P&L reporting, revenue recognition, and business unit performance tracking. Critical for management accounting and segment reporting under IFRS/GAAP']
  royalty_owner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.royalty_owner. Business justification: Accounts established for royalty owners who purchase products/services or sell their production. Connects commercial relationship management (credit, pricing, contracts) to legal ownership records for']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: In oil & gas, the same legal entity often acts as both customer and vendor (JV partners, integrated majors, service companies). Business process: consolidated counterparty management, unified credit/s']
  venture_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venture.partner. Business justification: JV partners frequently purchase their equity crude through customer accounts. Enables integrated partner-customer management for equity offtake sales, consolidated credit exposure tracking, and partne']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Legal or trade name of the counterparty associated with this commercial account, as registered in the SAP customer master. Used for contract documents, billing, and regulatory reporting.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier assigned to the commercial account. Used in invoices, Joint Interest Billing (JIB) statements, and counterparty correspondence. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD customer account master.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the commercial account. Drives eligibility for order placement, credit extension, and billing. Supports SOX audit compliance through temporal status tracking. Values: active (fully operational), suspended (temporarily blocked), closed (permanently terminated), pending (awaiting KYC/credit approval), under_review (compliance or credit review in progress).']
  account_tier varchar [note: 'Commercial segmentation tier classifying the account by strategic importance and revenue contribution. Strategic accounts receive dedicated account managers and priority service. Preferred accounts receive enhanced terms. Standard accounts operate under default commercial terms.']
  billing_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which invoices and financial transactions are denominated for this account (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Drives foreign exchange conversion in SAP FI and financial reporting under IFRS/GAAP.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Commercial relationship type defining the nature of the offtake or supply arrangement. Spot buyer: single-transaction purchases at market price. Term offtake: long-term volume commitment contract. Tolling: processing fee arrangement where counterparty owns feedstock. Exchange: product swap arrangement between counterparties. Bunkering: marine fuel supply account.']
  close_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the commercial account was formally closed or terminated. Null for active accounts. Required for SOX temporal status tracking and revenue recognition cutoff under IFRS 15.']
  commercial_territory varchar [note: 'Geographic or organizational sales territory to which this account is assigned for commercial management, quota allocation, and revenue attribution. Aligns with SAP SD sales organization and territory hierarchy.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the counterparty\'s primary registered business address. Used for sanctions screening, regulatory jurisdiction determination, and export control compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the account record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Conforms to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and SOX compliance.']
  credit_block_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is currently blocked from new order processing due to credit limit breach, overdue invoices, or credit review. True = account is credit-blocked and orders require credit manager release in SAP SD.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding credit exposure in the billing currency approved for this account by the credit risk function. Enforced at order entry in SAP SD credit management. Reviewed periodically based on counterparty credit rating and payment history.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'External or internal credit rating assigned to the counterparty, reflecting their creditworthiness and default risk (e.g., AAA, BBB+, BB-, internal risk grades). Sourced from rating agencies (Moody\'s, S&P, Fitch) or internal credit assessment. Drives credit limit setting and collateral requirements.']
  credit_rating_agency varchar [note: 'Source of the credit rating assigned to this account\'s counterparty. Identifies whether the rating is from an external agency (Moody\'s, S&P, Fitch, DBRS) or derived from an internal credit scoring model.']
  credit_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled or last-completed date for periodic credit limit and rating review for this account. Ensures credit exposure remains aligned with counterparty financial health. Managed through SAP FI credit management workflow.']
  distribution_channel_code varchar [note: 'SAP SD distribution channel code classifying how products reach this account (e.g., wholesale, direct, retail, export). Used for pricing determination, revenue reporting, and sales analytics.']
  dunning_procedure_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI dunning procedure key assigned to this account, defining the escalation schedule and notice levels for overdue invoice collection. Governs accounts receivable collections workflow.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost allocation between Oil Gas and the counterparty for product deliveries under this account. Critical for logistics, insurance, and revenue recognition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_intercompany boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account represents an intercompany transaction counterparty (i.e., another legal entity within the Oil Gas corporate group). True = intercompany account subject to transfer pricing and intercompany elimination in consolidation.']
  is_jv_partner boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counterparty is also a Joint Venture (JV) partner under a Joint Operating Agreement (JOA) or Production Sharing Agreement (PSA). True = counterparty has working interest (WI) participation. Drives Joint Interest Billing (JIB) processing in SAP JVA.']
  kyc_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current KYC compliance documentation for this account expires and must be renewed. Null if KYC has no fixed expiry. Triggers automated renewal workflow in compliance management systems.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Know Your Customer (KYC) compliance verification for this account\'s counterparty. Approved: all KYC documentation verified and current. Pending: verification in progress. Expired: KYC documentation has lapsed and requires renewal. Rejected: counterparty failed KYC screening.']
  open_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the commercial account was formally opened and activated in the system of record. Marks the effective start of the business relationship. Used for tenure analysis and SOX audit trails.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP payment terms key defining the due date calculation, early payment discount schedule, and cash discount conditions applicable to this account (e.g., NT30, NT60, 2/10NET30). Governs accounts receivable aging and cash flow forecasting.']
  preferred_delivery_mode varchar [note: 'Primary logistics mode preferred by the counterparty for product delivery. Drives transportation planning, scheduling, and logistics cost allocation. Relevant for crude oil, refined products, LNG, and NGL deliveries.']
  price_list_type varchar [note: 'SAP SD pricing condition type or price list category applicable to this account, determining the base pricing structure (e.g., WTI-linked, Brent-linked, fixed price, formula-based). Drives pricing determination in sales order processing.']
  product_segment varchar [note: 'Primary petroleum product segment associated with this commercial account. Defines the commodity category for which the account is established. Crude oil, refined products (gasoline, diesel, jet fuel), Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG), Natural Gas Liquids (NGL), Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG), petrochemicals, or lubricants. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: crude_oil|refined_products|LNG|NGL|LPG|petrochemicals|lubricants — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code classifying the account\'s counterparty location for commercial territory management, regulatory reporting, and market analytics (e.g., USGC, NWEUR, MENA, APAC).']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Primary commercial channel through which product is sold or delivered to this account. Direct: bilateral contract. Broker: intermediary-facilitated. Exchange: commodity exchange platform. Pipeline: pipeline tariff-based delivery. Marine: tanker or vessel-based delivery. Retail: downstream retail network.']
  sales_org_code varchar [note: 'SAP SD sales organization code to which this account is assigned. Defines the legal entity responsible for the sale, the applicable pricing conditions, and the revenue booking entity for financial consolidation.']
  sanctions_screened boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counterparty associated with this account has been screened against applicable sanctions lists (OFAC SDN, EU Consolidated List, UN Sanctions). True = screened and cleared. False = not yet screened or screening pending.']
  status_change_date date [note: 'Date on which the account status was last changed (e.g., from active to suspended, or from pending to active). Critical for SOX audit compliance and temporal status tracking in the Silver layer.']
  status_change_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason code or description explaining the most recent account status change (e.g., credit limit breach, KYC expiry, counterparty request, sanctions flag, commercial closure). Supports audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  tax_classification_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification assigned to this account in SAP FI/SD, determining applicable VAT, excise duty, and withholding tax treatment for transactions. Varies by counterparty jurisdiction and product type.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the account record in the enterprise data platform. Conforms to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture, incremental processing, and audit trail.']
  vat_registration_number varchar [note: 'Value Added Tax (VAT) or equivalent indirect tax registration number of the counterparty. Required for tax invoice compliance, cross-border transaction reporting, and VAT reclaim processing.']
  volume_commitment_bopd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Contracted minimum or nominated daily volume commitment for this account expressed in Barrels of Oil Per Day (BOPD). Applicable for term offtake accounts. Used for production planning, logistics scheduling, and take-or-pay obligation tracking.']

  Note: 'Commercial account record representing an active or historical business relationship between Oil Gas and a counterparty. Captures account number, account tier (strategic, preferred, standard), account status (active, suspended, closed), assigned commercial territory, primary sales channel, account open/close dates, account manager assignment, credit limit, payment terms code, billing currency, and account classification (spot buyer, term offtake, tolling, exchange, bunkering). Links counterparty identity to commercial, billing, and logistics operations. Supports temporal status tracking for SOX audit compliance.'
}

Table customer.contact {
  contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the individual contact person record within the Oil Gas customer domain. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and relationship resolution.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent counterparty or customer account (IOC, NOC, refiner, trader, industrial consumer) to which this contact belongs. Supports account hierarchy and relationship management.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_counterparty. Business justification: Contacts can be associated directly with counterparties (not just through accounts). This supports scenarios where a contact represents the counterparty entity itself. The organization_name on contact']
  approval_limit_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary value in US Dollars that this contact is authorized to approve for transactions, AFEs (Authorization for Expenditure), purchase orders, or contract amendments on behalf of their organization. Critical for COPAS AFE management and SOX financial controls.']
  assistant_name varchar [note: 'Name of the executive assistant or administrative contact who manages scheduling and correspondence on behalf of this contact. Used for executive-level relationship management with senior IOC, NOC, and industrial customer representatives.']
  assistant_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the executive assistant or administrative contact. Used to schedule meetings and route communications for senior counterparty representatives who delegate correspondence management.']
  authority_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the contractual and operational authority level of the contact: signatory (authorized to execute contracts/AFEs), approver (can approve transactions up to defined limits), operational (day-to-day operational decisions), informational (receives communications only). Critical for AFE authorization, JIB approvals, and contract execution workflows.']
  contact_role varchar [note: 'Functional role classification of the contact in the context of the Oil Gas business relationship. Determines which communications, documents, and workflows are routed to this individual. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commercial|technical|legal|hse_liaison|financial|executive|operational|procurement|regulatory — promote to reference product]']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contact record. Active contacts are eligible for communications and workflow routing. Inactive or terminated contacts are retained for audit and historical reference but excluded from active routing. Supports KYC refresh and relationship management processes.']
  contact_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contact\'s relationship type to the account: primary (main point of contact), secondary (backup), emergency (24/7 incident response), billing (JIB and invoice recipient), technical (operations and engineering). Supports structured relationship management across IOC, NOC, and industrial customer organizations.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code representing the country where the contact is primarily based or operates. Used for jurisdictional compliance, KYC screening, sanctions screening, and regulatory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contact record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit trail creation anchor for SOX compliance and data governance.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or business unit of the contact within their counterparty organization (e.g., Trading, Procurement, Legal, HSE, Operations, Finance, Executive). Supports routing of commercial, technical, and legal communications.']
  direct_phone varchar [note: 'Direct dial telephone number for the contact in E.164 international format. Used for urgent operational communications, SIMOPS coordination, HSE incident notifications, and commercial negotiations.']
  do_not_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact has requested to be excluded from non-essential communications. True = do not contact except for legally required or contractually obligated communications. Supports GDPR opt-out and communication preference compliance.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this contact record became or becomes effective and valid for use in business processes. Supports temporal validity tracking for contacts associated with time-bound roles such as JV representatives, PSA counterparties, or project-specific liaisons.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this contact record expires or ceases to be valid. Null indicates an open-ended active record. Used for time-bound role assignments such as project representatives, interim contacts, or term-limited JV liaisons.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary business email address of the contact. Used for commercial correspondence, contract notifications, JIB distribution, AFE approvals, and regulatory communications. Serves as the primary digital identifier for this contact.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the contact in E.164 international format. Still used in oil and gas for formal document transmission including contract amendments, regulatory filings, and JIB (Joint Interest Billing) correspondence in certain jurisdictions.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the individual contact person. Used for personalized communication and formal correspondence with traders, procurement officers, operations contacts, and executive sponsors.']
  hse_certification varchar [note: 'HSE certification or qualification held by the contact relevant to oil and gas operations (e.g., BOSIET, HUET, H2S Alive, NEBOSH, IADC WellSharp). Required for HSE liaison contacts and those involved in PTW (Permit to Work) processes or offshore operations.']
  hse_certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the contact\'s primary HSE certification. Used to track certification validity and trigger renewal notifications. Contacts with expired certifications may be restricted from certain operational roles or site access.']
  is_emergency_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is designated as an emergency response contact for HSE incidents, SIMOPS notifications, or operational emergencies. True = emergency contact available 24/7. Required for offshore and high-risk operations per BSEE and OSHA requirements.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is designated as the primary point of contact for the parent account. True = primary contact; False = secondary or supplementary contact. Only one contact per account should be flagged as primary at any given time.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title or position of the contact within their organization (e.g., VP Trading, Procurement Manager, HSE Director, Operations Engineer). Used for protocol, routing of communications, and authority-level determination.']
  kyc_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact has completed Know Your Customer (KYC) identity verification as required for counterparty onboarding and compliance. True = verified; False = pending or not verified. Supports anti-money laundering (AML) and sanctions compliance workflows.']
  kyc_verified_date date [note: 'Date on which the contact\'s KYC identity verification was last completed or renewed. Used to track KYC refresh cycles and ensure compliance with periodic re-verification requirements under AML regulations.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) representing the contact\'s preferred language for communications and documents (e.g., \'en\', \'ar\', \'fr\', \'es\', \'zh-CN\'). Supports multinational IOC and NOC relationship management.']
  last_interaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent recorded business interaction with this contact (meeting, call, email exchange, contract negotiation, site visit). Used by commercial and account management teams to track relationship engagement frequency and identify dormant contacts.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name or surname of the individual contact person. Combined with first_name to form the full display name for correspondence, KYC records, and relationship management.']
  linkedin_profile varchar [note: 'LinkedIn professional profile URL for the contact. Used for relationship intelligence, executive engagement tracking, and due diligence verification of counterparty representatives in commercial and JV negotiations.']
  mobile_phone varchar [note: 'Mobile or cellular phone number for the contact in E.164 international format. Used for after-hours emergency contact, field operations coordination, and HSE incident response. Particularly important for offshore and remote operations contacts.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing relationship intelligence, communication preferences, background context, or special handling instructions for this contact. Used by commercial teams, account managers, and JV administrators to record qualitative relationship insights.']
  preferred_communication_channel varchar [note: 'The contact\'s preferred channel for receiving business communications. Drives automated notification routing for contract updates, invoice delivery, AFE approvals, and operational alerts.']
  salutation varchar [note: 'Formal salutation or honorific for the contact (e.g., Mr, Ms, Dr, Eng). Used in formal correspondence, contract documents, and executive communications with senior counterparty representatives.']
  sanctions_screened boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact has been screened against applicable sanctions lists (OFAC SDN, EU Consolidated List, UN Sanctions). True = screened and cleared; False = not yet screened or screening pending. Mandatory for counterparty onboarding in oil and gas cross-border transactions.']
  sanctions_screened_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent sanctions screening was performed for this contact. Used to enforce periodic re-screening intervals and demonstrate compliance with OFAC and international sanctions programs.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The original identifier for this contact record in the upstream operational system of record (e.g., SAP S/4HANA Business Partner number, CRM contact ID). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and cross-system deduplication.']
  source_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this contact record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and master data management reconciliation across Oil Gas enterprise systems.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier (e.g., \'America/Houston\', \'Europe/London\', \'Asia/Riyadh\') for the contact\'s primary work location. Used to schedule meetings, set communication windows, and timestamp operational notifications correctly across global operations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this contact record, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change detection, data synchronization, and audit trail maintenance.']

  Note: 'Individual contact person associated with a counterparty or account, including name, title, role (commercial, technical, legal, HSE liaison), department, direct phone, email, preferred communication channel, language preference, authority level (signatory, approver, operational), and active status. Supports relationship management for traders, procurement officers, operations contacts, and executive sponsors across IOC, NOC, and industrial customer organizations.'
}

Table customer.delivery_point {
  delivery_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the delivery point record. Primary key for the delivery_point data product. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a physical or logical delivery location managed by the business.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Delivery points (terminals, loading facilities, custody transfer points) incur operating costs (maintenance, personnel, utilities) tracked to cost centers for facility-level cost management and AFE tr']
  operating_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.operating_license. Business justification: Delivery points such as marine terminals, pipeline interconnects, and loading facilities require operating licenses from regulatory authorities. Operations teams must verify valid licenses before sche']
  operator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.operator. Business justification: Delivery points (terminals, custody transfer points, loading facilities) are operated by specific entities responsible for facility operations, safety, and custody transfer. Critical for logistics coo']
  operator_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Delivery points (terminals, pipelines) are typically operated by third-party vendors. Business process: tariff management, custody transfer agreements, logistics service procurement, operator performa']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Delivery points generate revenue (throughput fees, tariffs) and costs tracked to profit centers for asset-level profitability analysis and segment reporting. Essential for midstream operations financi']
  address_line varchar [note: 'Street address or physical location description of the delivery point facility. Classified as confidential organizational contact data. Used for logistics dispatch, trucking directions, and regulatory permit filings.']
  berth_number varchar [note: 'Specific berth or jetty number at a port or marine terminal delivery point. Used for vessel scheduling, port authority coordination, and cargo nomination documentation. Applicable to tanker, LNG, and LPG delivery modes.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the delivery point is located. Used for address resolution, logistics routing, and regulatory permit identification.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the delivery point is physically located (e.g., USA, GBR, NLD, SAU). Used for regulatory jurisdiction determination, export/import compliance, and geographic reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delivery point record was first created in the system of record. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and SOX compliance requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  custody_transfer_point boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this delivery point is a designated custody transfer point where title and measurement responsibility passes between parties. Custody transfer points require API MPMS-compliant metering, sampling, and quality certification. Critical for revenue accounting and joint interest billing (JIB).']
  customs_port_code varchar [note: 'Official customs authority port or entry point code assigned to this delivery location for import/export declaration purposes. Required for international crude oil, LNG, and refined product shipments. Aligns with CBP (U.S. Customs and Border Protection) or equivalent national customs authority codes.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date on which this delivery point was or is scheduled to be permanently decommissioned and removed from service. Nullable for active locations. Used for asset lifecycle management, contract termination planning, and regulatory P&A (Plug and Abandonment) reporting.']
  delivery_mode varchar [note: 'Primary transportation mode used to deliver product at this delivery point (pipeline, tanker, truck, rail, or barge). Determines applicable logistics, tariff, and scheduling rules. Supports multi-modal delivery planning for crude oil, LNG, LPG, NGL, and refined products.']
  delivery_point_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique business identifier for the delivery point (e.g., terminal code, rack code, pipeline interconnect ID). Used in nominations, scheduling, and logistics communications with counterparties and operators. Aligns with SAP SD delivery location codes and Avocet Production Operations location identifiers.']
  delivery_point_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the delivery point (e.g., \'Houston Ship Channel Terminal\', \'Cushing Pipeline Interconnect\', \'Rotterdam LNG Discharge Berth 3\'). Used in commercial documents, logistics scheduling, and customer-facing communications.']
  delivery_point_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the delivery point by facility type. Drives logistics scheduling rules, product compatibility checks, and nomination workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: terminal|pipeline_interconnect|rack|refinery_gate|storage_facility|port_berth|fpso_offloading|lng_regasification|rail_siding|truck_loading — promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this delivery point became or becomes commercially and operationally active. Used for contract validity checks, nomination eligibility, and historical reporting. Aligns with SAP SD validity period management.']
  h2s_service boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this delivery point handles Hydrogen Sulfide (H2S)-bearing streams. H2S service locations require specialized safety equipment, personnel training, and HSE protocols per OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 Process Safety Management and API RP 55.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory or internal safety inspection conducted at this delivery point. Used for HSE compliance tracking, maintenance scheduling in Maximo Asset Management, and regulatory audit readiness.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the delivery point in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for GIS mapping, offshore/onshore classification, proximity analysis, and logistics route optimization. Supports integration with GIS systems.']
  loading_arm_count varchar [note: 'Number of loading or discharge arms available at the delivery point. Determines simultaneous loading capacity and influences scheduling of concurrent cargo operations (SIMOPS). Applicable to marine terminals, rack locations, and pipeline interconnects.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the delivery point in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for GIS mapping, offshore/onshore classification, proximity analysis, and logistics route optimization.']
  max_vessel_dwt decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum vessel size in Deadweight Tonnes (DWT) that can be accommodated at this marine delivery point. Used for vessel compatibility screening during cargo scheduling and nomination management for crude oil and LNG tanker deliveries.']
  metering_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fiscal or allocation metering system installed at this delivery point, as registered in the OSIsoft PI System or SCADA/DCS. Used to link delivery volumes to measurement data for custody transfer, allocation, and revenue accounting.']
  min_vessel_dwt decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum vessel size in Deadweight Tonnes (DWT) required to safely operate at this marine delivery point. Used alongside max_vessel_dwt for vessel compatibility screening during cargo scheduling.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory regulatory or internal safety inspection at this delivery point. Drives preventive maintenance scheduling in Maximo Asset Management and HSE compliance calendar management.']
  nomination_lead_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of calendar days in advance that a delivery nomination must be submitted for this delivery point. Drives nomination management workflows in Avocet Production Operations and SAP SD scheduling. Ensures operational readiness and regulatory compliance.']
  norm_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this delivery point has identified Naturally Occurring Radioactive Material (NORM) risk associated with produced water, scale, or sludge handling. NORM-flagged locations require specialized waste management, personnel monitoring, and regulatory reporting per EPA and state radiation control programs.']
  offshore_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery point is located offshore (True) or onshore (False). Offshore delivery points are subject to BSEE/BOEM regulatory oversight, additional HSE requirements, and FPSO/marine logistics protocols.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Description of the delivery point\'s operational hours (e.g., \'24/7\', \'Mon-Fri 06:00-22:00 Local\', \'Daylight hours only\'). Used for logistics scheduling, nomination window management, and customer delivery planning.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle state of the delivery point. Controls whether the location is available for nomination and scheduling. \'suspended\' indicates temporary unavailability; \'decommissioned\' indicates permanent retirement from service.']
  pipeline_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the pipeline system or network to which this delivery point is connected (e.g., \'Colonial Pipeline\', \'Trans-Alaska Pipeline System\'). Applicable for pipeline_interconnect and terminal delivery point types. Used for tariff determination and FERC/PHMSA regulatory reporting.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the delivery point location. Used for logistics routing, tax jurisdiction determination, and regulatory compliance filings.']
  region varchar [note: 'Business-defined geographic region or operating area in which the delivery point is located (e.g., \'Gulf Coast\', \'North Sea\', \'Middle East\', \'Asia Pacific\'). Used for regional logistics planning, commercial reporting, and supply chain segmentation.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory jurisdiction governing this delivery point (e.g., \'FERC\', \'BSEE\', \'EPA Region 6\', \'PHMSA\', \'UK HSE\'). Determines applicable safety, environmental, and operational compliance requirements. Used for regulatory reporting and permit management.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative subdivision where the delivery point is located. Required for regulatory jurisdiction mapping (e.g., state environmental permits, PHMSA state pipeline safety programs) and tax/royalty reporting.']
  storage_capacity_bbl decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum storage capacity of the delivery point facility expressed in barrels (BBL). Principal quantitative fact for storage facilities, terminals, and FPSOs. Used for inventory planning, nomination management, and capacity allocation. Applicable to crude oil, NGL, LPG, and refined product locations.']
  tariff_code varchar [note: 'Applicable pipeline or terminal tariff code governing the transportation or throughput charges at this delivery point. Used for freight cost calculation, FERC tariff compliance, and SAP SD pricing condition determination.']
  throughput_capacity_bopd decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum throughput or loading/discharge rate of the delivery point expressed in Barrels of Oil Per Day (BOPD). Used for logistics scheduling, nomination management, and capacity constraint modeling. Applicable to terminals, racks, pipeline interconnects, and refinery gates.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the delivery point location (e.g., \'America/Chicago\', \'Europe/Amsterdam\', \'Asia/Riyadh\'). Used to correctly interpret operating hours, nomination deadlines, and delivery timestamps in logistics and scheduling systems.']
  unlocode varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE five-character code identifying the port, terminal, or logistics location (e.g., USGLS for Galveston, NLRTM for Rotterdam). Mandatory for international trade documentation, LNG/LPG cargo nominations, and customs declarations. Aligns with UNECE Trade Facilitation standards.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delivery point record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  water_depth_m decimal(10,2) [note: 'Water depth in metres at the offshore delivery point location. Applicable only to offshore berths, FPSO offloading points, and subsea pipeline interconnects. Used for vessel compatibility assessment and marine logistics planning.']

  Note: 'Physical or logical delivery location associated with a customer account, including terminal name, port of loading/discharge, pipeline interconnect point, rack location, refinery gate, storage facility, geographic coordinates, country, region, delivery mode (pipeline, tanker, truck, rail), UNLOCODE, and operational status. Supports logistics scheduling, nomination management, and product delivery execution for crude oil, LNG, LPG, NGL, and refined product customers.'
}

Table customer.credit_profile {
