  credit_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the credit risk profile record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commercial account associated with this credit profile, supporting account hierarchy structures for multi-entity counterparties such as parent-subsidiary trading groups.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the counterparty or customer entity for whom this credit profile is maintained. Links to the customer master record covering IOCs, NOCs, refiners, traders, wholesale buyers, and industrial consumers.']
  credit_limit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.credit_limit. Business justification: Links customer credit profiles to commercial credit limits for integrated credit risk management. Required for credit utilization monitoring, exposure reporting, and credit committee reviews that span']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Credit provisions and allowances for doubtful accounts must post to specific GL accounts (bad debt expense, allowance accounts). Required for financial statement preparation and credit risk reporting.']
  venture_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venture.partner. Business justification: Credit assessment of venture partners for commercial exposure (equity crude sales, product supply). Required for partner credit risk management, trade receivables exposure tracking, credit limit setti']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the credit limit and terms were formally approved by the credit analyst or credit committee. Establishes the effective start of the current credit terms for this counterparty.']
  collateral_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value in US Dollars of the collateral or credit support instrument currently held against this counterparty\'s credit exposure. For letters of credit, this is the LC face value; for prepayments, the amount held on account.']
  collateral_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current collateral instrument (e.g., letter of credit, parent guarantee) expires. Critical for proactive renewal management to avoid unsecured exposure gaps in crude oil and LNG trading operations.']
  collateral_type varchar [note: 'Type of credit support or collateral arrangement securing the counterparty\'s credit exposure. \'letter_of_credit\' = bank-issued LC; \'parent_guarantee\' = corporate guarantee from parent entity; \'prepayment\' = advance payment required; \'cash_deposit\' = cash margin held; \'surety_bond\' = third-party bond; \'none\' = unsecured. Common in crude oil trading and LNG offtake agreements.']
  counterparty_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the counterparty by business role in the oil and gas value chain. IOC (International Oil Company), NOC (National Oil Company), refiner, trader, wholesale buyer, industrial consumer, or retail distributor. Drives credit policy application and risk tier assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IOC|NOC|refiner|trader|wholesale_buyer|industrial_consumer|retail_distributor — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this credit profile record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for SOX compliance and data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  credit_analyst varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the credit analyst responsible for maintaining and reviewing this credit profile. Provides accountability and audit trail for credit decisions on crude oil trading, LNG offtake, and refined product wholesale accounts.']
  credit_committee_approval_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the current credit limit and terms have been formally approved by the credit committee, as required for counterparties above the credit analyst\'s delegated authority threshold. True = committee-approved; False = within analyst authority or pending approval.']
  credit_exposure_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current total outstanding credit exposure in US Dollars, representing the sum of open invoices, unconfirmed deliveries, and mark-to-market positions for crude oil, LNG, and refined product transactions with this counterparty.']
  credit_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counterparty is currently on credit hold, blocking new crude oil, LNG, or refined product shipments and transactions until the hold is resolved. True = hold active; False = no hold. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA SD order management to block delivery creation.']
  credit_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the active credit hold. \'overdue_payment\' = outstanding invoices past due; \'limit_exceeded\' = exposure exceeds approved limit; \'rating_downgrade\' = credit rating fell below threshold; \'kyc_failure\' = KYC compliance issue; \'sanctions_flag\' = OFAC/sanctions screening hit; \'manual_hold\' = credit analyst discretionary hold. Null when credit_hold_flag is False.']
  credit_limit_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum approved credit exposure in US Dollars for this counterparty across all crude oil trading, LNG offtake, and refined product wholesale transactions. Set by the credit committee and reviewed periodically. Denominated in USD as the functional currency for international petroleum transactions.']
  credit_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for credit analyst commentary, special conditions, exceptions, or contextual information relevant to the credit profile. May include notes on collateral negotiations, payment dispute history, or market intelligence affecting creditworthiness.']
  credit_review_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which this counterparty\'s credit profile is formally reviewed and reaffirmed by the credit risk team. \'event_triggered\' indicates review is initiated by a specific event (e.g., rating downgrade, payment default, material contract change) rather than a fixed schedule.']
  credit_utilization_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Ratio of current credit exposure to approved credit limit, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 75.5000 = 75.5%). Used to monitor proximity to credit limit thresholds and trigger review or hold actions in crude oil and refined product trading operations.']
  credit_watch_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the counterparty is on credit watch and the directional outlook. \'none\' = no watch; \'positive\' = potential upgrade; \'negative\' = potential downgrade; \'developing\' = uncertain direction. Mirrors external agency watch terminology and is applied to both internal and external ratings.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the credit limit and exposure amounts (typically USD for international petroleum transactions, but may be EUR, GBP, or local currency for specific regional contracts).']
  dso_days decimal(7,2) [note: 'Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) — the average number of days this counterparty takes to pay invoices, calculated over the trailing review period. A key credit risk indicator for crude oil, LNG, and refined product wholesale transactions. Higher DSO indicates slower payment behavior.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the current credit terms (limit, rating, collateral) are binding and applicable to transactions with this counterparty. Supports temporal validity tracking for credit agreements.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which the current credit terms are valid. Null for open-ended credit arrangements. When populated, triggers renewal workflow before expiry to avoid lapse in credit coverage for active crude oil and LNG trading counterparties.']
  external_rating_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent external credit rating (S&P, Moody\'s, or Fitch) was issued or last confirmed. Used to assess rating currency and trigger re-evaluation when ratings are stale relative to credit review policy.']
  fitch_rating varchar [note: 'External credit rating assigned by Fitch Ratings for the counterparty (e.g., AAA, AA+, BBB-, BB, CCC). Provides a third-agency perspective for credit risk triangulation on significant crude oil trading and LNG offtake counterparties.']
  internal_credit_rating varchar [note: 'Proprietary internal credit rating assigned by the company\'s credit risk team, reflecting the counterparty\'s creditworthiness based on financial analysis, payment history, and industry position. Uses a letter-grade scale (e.g., AAA, AA+, BB-) aligned with internal credit policy.']
  internal_rating_date date [note: 'Date on which the internal credit rating was last assigned or reaffirmed by the credit risk team. Drives the credit review cycle and next review date calculation.']
  kyc_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current KYC verification expires and renewal is required. Drives compliance workflow alerts to ensure continuous KYC coverage for active crude oil, LNG, and refined product trading counterparties.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Know Your Customer (KYC) compliance status for this counterparty. \'verified\' = KYC checks passed and current; \'pending\' = KYC in progress; \'expired\' = KYC verification has lapsed and renewal is required; \'failed\' = KYC checks failed; \'not_required\' = exempt per policy. Mandatory for all trading counterparties under FATF and OFAC compliance frameworks.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent formal credit review was completed and the credit profile was last reaffirmed or updated by the credit analyst or credit committee.']
  moodys_rating varchar [note: 'External credit rating assigned by Moody\'s Investors Service for the counterparty (e.g., Aaa, Aa1, Baa2, Ba3, Caa1). Used alongside S&P and Fitch ratings for comprehensive external credit assessment of major trading counterparties.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal credit review of this counterparty. Calculated from last_review_date and credit_review_frequency. Used to drive workflow alerts in SAP S/4HANA and credit management dashboards.']
  payment_history_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score (typically 0–100) reflecting the counterparty\'s historical payment behavior, incorporating on-time payment rate, frequency of late payments, and dispute history across crude oil, LNG, and refined product transactions. Higher score indicates better payment performance.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP payment terms code defining the credit period and discount structure for this counterparty (e.g., NT30 = net 30 days, NT60 = net 60 days). Governs invoice due date calculation for crude oil liftings, LNG cargo invoices, and refined product deliveries.']
  profile_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the credit profile, used in credit review correspondence, JIB (Joint Interest Billing) documentation, and counterparty communications. Format: CP- followed by 6–12 alphanumeric characters.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the credit profile record. \'active\' indicates the profile is in force; \'suspended\' indicates a temporary hold; \'under_review\' indicates a scheduled or triggered reassessment; \'closed\' indicates the counterparty relationship has ended; \'pending_approval\' indicates awaiting credit committee sign-off.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Internal risk tier classification for the counterparty, used to apply differentiated credit policies, collateral requirements, and monitoring intensity. Tier 1 = lowest risk (investment grade, strong payment history); Tier 4 = highest risk (sub-investment grade, poor payment history or limited financial information).']
  sanctions_screening_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent sanctions screening was performed against OFAC SDN, EU, and UN sanctions lists. Used to ensure screening currency and compliance with regulatory requirements for international petroleum trading.']
  sanctions_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent OFAC and international sanctions screening for this counterparty. \'clear\' = no matches; \'flagged\' = potential match requiring review; \'under_review\' = compliance team investigating; \'blocked\' = confirmed sanctions match, all transactions prohibited.']
  sp_rating varchar [note: 'External credit rating assigned by S&P Global Ratings for the counterparty (e.g., AAA, AA+, BBB-, BB, CCC). Used to supplement internal ratings for large IOC, NOC, and refiner counterparties in crude oil and LNG offtake agreements. Null if no S&P rating exists.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this credit profile record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Credit risk profile for a counterparty or account, capturing credit limit (USD), credit rating (internal and external — S&P, Moody\'s, Fitch), credit rating date, credit watch status, collateral type (letter of credit, parent guarantee, prepayment), collateral amount, credit utilization, days sales outstanding (DSO), payment history score, credit review frequency, next review date, credit analyst, and credit hold flag. Supports credit risk management for crude oil trading, LNG offtake, and refined product wholesale transactions.'
}

Table customer.credit_event {
  credit_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for each credit event record. Primary key for the credit_event data product in the customer domain.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the counterparty or customer whose credit profile is affected by this event. Links to the master customer record for IOCs, NOCs, refiners, traders, wholesale buyers, or industrial consumers.']
  credit_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific credit account or credit facility associated with this event. A customer may have multiple credit accounts across different business lines (e.g., crude trading, LNG, refined products).']
  credit_limit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to commercial.credit_limit. Business justification: Credit events (limit increases, decreases, breaches, rating changes) must reference the specific credit limit being modified. Required for audit trail, credit risk reporting, regulatory compliance (Ba']
  credit_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.credit_profile. Business justification: credit_event records transactional credit-related events (limit changes, rating changes, defaults) that affect a counterparty\'s credit profile. credit_profile is the governing parent entity. credit_ev']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Credit limit changes, payment defaults, and collateral postings trigger journal entries to specific GL accounts. Audit trail requirement for credit risk accounting and SOX controls.']
  reversed_event_credit_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original credit event that this record reverses. Populated only when reversal_flag is True. Enables bidirectional audit trail linking between original and reversal events for SOX compliance and credit history reconstruction.']
  venture_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venture.partner. Business justification: Credit events involving venture partners as customers require integrated monitoring. Essential for partner credit event tracking, default risk assessment, cross-default provisions, and coordinated cre']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the credit event was formally approved by the designated approving authority. Distinct from event_timestamp (when the event occurred) and created_timestamp (when the record was created). Critical for SOX audit trail and segregation of duties compliance.']
  approving_authority varchar [note: 'The name or role of the individual, committee, or system that authorized this credit event. Examples: \'Credit Risk Committee\', \'VP Credit Risk\', \'Automated Credit Engine\', \'CFO\'. Required for SOX compliance to establish accountability for credit decisions affecting financial exposure.']
  automated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credit event was generated automatically by the credit management system (True) or was manually initiated by a credit analyst or committee (False). Supports audit trail analysis and system performance monitoring for automated credit controls in SAP FI Credit Management.']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'The internal business unit or division of Oil Gas that owns the credit relationship and is responsible for managing this credit event (e.g., \'Crude Trading\', \'LNG Marketing\', \'Refined Products\', \'Petrochemicals\', \'E&P Joint Ventures\'). Used for credit risk reporting by segment.']
  collateral_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of collateral demanded, posted, or released in connection with this credit event. Applicable for COLLATERAL_CALL and COLLATERAL_RELEASE event types. Includes letters of credit, bank guarantees, cash deposits, or parent company guarantees used to secure credit exposure in oil and gas trading.']
  collateral_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the collateral instrument associated with this credit event. LETTER_OF_CREDIT: documentary credit from a bank; BANK_GUARANTEE: unconditional bank guarantee; CASH_DEPOSIT: cash held as security; PARENT_GUARANTEE: guarantee from parent company of counterparty; PREPAYMENT: advance payment securing future deliveries. Null for non-collateral events.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'The reference number of the underlying commercial contract (e.g., crude oil sale agreement, LNG SPA, refined products supply contract, PSA, JOA) associated with this credit event. Provides traceability from the credit event back to the originating commercial agreement.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the counterparty\'s domicile or the jurisdiction governing the credit relationship. Used for country risk assessment, regulatory reporting, and sanctions screening in cross-border oil and gas transactions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this credit event record was first created in the system. Serves as the record audit creation timestamp for data lineage, SOX compliance, and lakehouse silver layer audit trail. Distinct from event_timestamp (when the business event occurred).']
  credit_limit_new decimal(18,2) [note: 'The numeric credit limit in the designated currency that becomes effective after this event. Populated specifically for CREDIT_LIMIT_CHANGE events. Null for non-limit events.']
  credit_limit_previous decimal(18,2) [note: 'The numeric credit limit in the designated currency that was in place before this event. Populated specifically for CREDIT_LIMIT_CHANGE events to enable quantitative analysis of limit movements. Null for non-limit events.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to monetary amounts in this credit event (e.g., USD for US Dollar, EUR for Euro, GBP for British Pound). Supports multi-currency credit management for international counterparties in crude oil, LNG, and refined products trading.']
  days_past_due varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days the overdue payment has exceeded its contractual due date at the time of this credit event. Used for aging analysis, IFRS 9 expected credit loss staging (Stage 1/2/3), and credit risk reporting. Null for non-payment events.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The business date on which the credit event takes effect and the new credit terms or conditions become operative. May differ from event_timestamp when events are backdated or scheduled for future activation (e.g., a credit limit change approved today but effective at month-end).']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable business identifier for the credit event, formatted as CE-YYYY-NNNNNN. Used in correspondence, audit trails, and SOX compliance documentation to uniquely reference a specific credit action.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the credit event record. PENDING: event initiated but awaiting approval; ACTIVE: event is in effect; COMPLETED: event has been fully processed and closed; REVERSED: event was reversed due to error or dispute; CANCELLED: event was cancelled before taking effect.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the credit event occurred in the real world (e.g., when the credit hold was placed, when the rating downgrade was confirmed, when the payment default was declared). This is the principal business event time, distinct from record audit timestamps.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the credit-related event that occurred. Drives downstream workflow and reporting. CREDIT_LIMIT_CHANGE: adjustment to approved credit limit; RATING_CHANGE: upgrade or downgrade of internal/external credit rating; COLLATERAL_CALL: demand for additional collateral; CREDIT_HOLD: suspension of credit facility; CREDIT_RELEASE: reinstatement of credit facility; PAYMENT_DEFAULT: counterparty failure to pay within contractual terms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CREDIT_LIMIT_CHANGE|RATING_CHANGE|COLLATERAL_CALL|CREDIT_HOLD|CREDIT_RELEASE|PAYMENT_DEFAULT|CREDIT_REVIEW_COMPLETED|CREDIT_WATCH|COVENANT_BREACH|MARGIN_CALL — promote to reference product]']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the credit event condition expires or is scheduled for review. Applicable for temporary credit holds, collateral calls with deadlines, or time-limited credit limit changes. Null for permanent events.']
  exposure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total outstanding credit exposure to the counterparty at the time of this credit event, expressed in the event currency. Represents the sum of open invoices, in-transit shipments, and mark-to-market derivatives exposure. Critical for credit risk management and IFRS 9 expected credit loss calculations.']
  kyc_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counterparty\'s Know Your Customer (KYC) verification was current and valid at the time of this credit event. A value of True confirms KYC compliance; False indicates KYC was expired or incomplete, which may have contributed to a credit hold or review.']
  new_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The updated value of the credit attribute after this event is applied. Stores the after-state for audit trail purposes. For CREDIT_LIMIT_CHANGE, this is the new limit amount as a string; for RATING_CHANGE, this is the new rating code (e.g., \'BB-\'); for CREDIT_HOLD, this is the new status. Stored as STRING to accommodate heterogeneous event types.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional commentary, context, or instructions related to this credit event. May include analyst observations, counterparty communications summary, escalation notes, or remediation actions. Supports credit review documentation and audit trail completeness.']
  payment_default_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the overdue or defaulted payment that triggered a PAYMENT_DEFAULT credit event. Represents the outstanding invoice amount not paid within contractual terms. Null for non-default events.']
  previous_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The prior value of the credit attribute before this event was applied. Stores the before-state for audit trail purposes. For CREDIT_LIMIT_CHANGE, this is the prior limit amount as a string; for RATING_CHANGE, this is the prior rating code (e.g., \'BBB+\'); for CREDIT_HOLD, this is the prior status. Stored as STRING to accommodate heterogeneous event types.']
  rating_agency varchar [note: 'The source of the credit rating referenced in this event. INTERNAL: proprietary internal credit scoring model; SP: S&P Global Ratings; MOODYS: Moody\'s Investors Service; FITCH: Fitch Ratings; DBRS: DBRS Morningstar. Applicable for RATING_CHANGE events. Null for non-rating events.']
  rating_new varchar [note: 'The credit rating code assigned to the counterparty after this rating change event (e.g., \'BB+\', \'Ba1\', \'BBB-\'). Follows the rating scale of the designated rating_agency. Null for non-rating events.']
  rating_previous varchar [note: 'The credit rating code assigned to the counterparty before this rating change event (e.g., \'BBB+\', \'Baa1\', \'A-\'). Follows the rating scale of the designated rating_agency. Null for non-rating events.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credit event has been reversed (True) due to error, dispute resolution, or system correction. When True, the reversed_event_id field identifies the original event being reversed. Supports SOX audit trail integrity.']
  review_due_date date [note: 'The scheduled date by which the next credit review must be completed following this event. Drives credit review workflow and ensures periodic reassessment of counterparty creditworthiness in accordance with credit policy. Particularly important after CREDIT_HOLD or RATING_CHANGE events.']
  sanctions_screened boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counterparty was screened against applicable sanctions lists (OFAC SDN, EU Consolidated List, UN Sanctions) at the time of this credit event. Required for regulatory compliance in international oil and gas trading operations.']
  source_document_number varchar [note: 'The document number in the originating source system (e.g., SAP FI document number, trading system transaction ID) that corresponds to this credit event. Enables cross-system traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and operational systems of record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating system that generated or recorded this credit event. SAP_FI: SAP S/4HANA Financial Accounting Credit Management module; MANUAL: manually entered by credit analyst; TRADING_SYSTEM: generated by commodity trading and risk management system; EXTERNAL_FEED: received from external credit bureau or rating agency data feed.']
  trigger_category varchar [note: 'Structured classification of the root cause category that triggered this credit event. Enables trend analysis and portfolio-level credit risk reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PAYMENT_BEHAVIOR|EXTERNAL_RATING|FINANCIAL_DETERIORATION|MARKET_CONDITIONS|PERIODIC_REVIEW|COVENANT_BREACH|REGULATORY|COUNTERPARTY_REQUEST|COLLATERAL_SHORTFALL — promote to reference product]']
  trigger_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the business reason or triggering condition that caused this credit event. Examples include: \'Counterparty missed payment on crude oil invoice #INV-2024-001234\', \'External rating downgrade by S&P from BBB to BB+\', \'Commodity price volatility increased mark-to-market exposure beyond threshold\', \'Annual credit review completed\'. Required for SOX audit trail.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this credit event record was last modified in the system. Tracks subsequent updates such as status changes, approval completions, or reversal processing. Required for data lineage and SOX audit trail in the Databricks Lakehouse silver layer.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of credit-related events affecting a counterparty\'s credit profile, including credit limit changes, credit rating downgrades/upgrades, collateral calls, credit holds, credit releases, payment defaults, and credit review completions. Captures event type, event date, previous value, new value, triggering reason, approving authority, and associated exposure amount. Provides full audit trail for credit risk management and SOX compliance.'
}

Table customer.kyc_record {
  kyc_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the KYC compliance record. Primary key for the kyc_record data product in the customer domain Silver layer.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_counterparty. Business justification: kyc_record is explicitly described as a KYC compliance record for each counterparty. It currently has no FK to customer_counterparty despite storing counterparty_legal_name and counterparty_type as de']
  venture_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venture.partner. Business justification: Venture partners acting as customers require KYC verification for commercial transactions. Essential for consolidated compliance management, regulatory screening of partner-customers, AML/sanctions co']
  adverse_media_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether adverse media screening has identified negative news, regulatory actions, legal proceedings, or reputational risk associated with the counterparty or its beneficial owners. Adverse media findings may trigger enhanced due diligence or relationship termination.']
  adverse_media_review_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which adverse media screening was conducted for this counterparty. Adverse media screening must be refreshed periodically and upon any material risk event or sanctions alert.']
  aml_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall Anti-Money Laundering (AML) risk rating assigned to the counterparty based on a composite assessment of jurisdiction risk, business type, transaction patterns, PEP status, and sanctions exposure. Determines monitoring frequency and trading limits.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the KYC record received final compliance approval, authorizing the counterparty for trading and commercial engagement. Distinct from verification_date which marks completion of the verification process.']
  beneficial_ownership_disclosed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counterparty has fully disclosed its ultimate beneficial owners (UBOs) holding 25% or more ownership interest, as required under FATF Recommendation 24 and FinCEN Customer Due Diligence Rule. Mandatory for all trading counterparties.']
  country_of_incorporation varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction where the counterparty is legally incorporated or registered. Used for sanctions screening, OFAC/EU/UN list matching, and jurisdictional risk assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this KYC record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit trail anchor for the record\'s lifecycle and supports SOX compliance audit requirements.']
  credit_risk_linked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this KYC record has been linked to an active credit risk assessment and credit limit in the credit management system. KYC approval is a prerequisite for credit limit assignment for trading counterparties.']
  document_collection_status varchar [note: 'Status of the collection of required KYC documentation from the counterparty, including certificate of incorporation, articles of association, beneficial ownership declarations, financial statements, and authorized signatory lists. Incomplete documentation blocks trading approval.']
  document_expiry_date date [note: 'Earliest expiry date among the key KYC documents collected for this counterparty (e.g., certificate of good standing, trade license). When any document expires, the document_collection_status must be updated and renewal initiated.']
  eu_sanctions_screening_result varchar [note: 'Result of screening the counterparty against the European Union consolidated sanctions list. Mandatory for all EU-jurisdiction trading activities and European counterparties. A confirmed match prohibits transactions under EU law.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current KYC record expires and a renewal review is required. Determined by KYC tier: Enhanced Due Diligence records typically expire annually, Standard every two years, Simplified every three years per internal compliance policy.']
  fatf_framework_version varchar [note: 'Version of the FATF Recommendations framework under which this KYC record was prepared and assessed (e.g., FATF 2012 Revised Recommendations, FATF 2023 Update). Ensures traceability of compliance assessments to the applicable regulatory standard version.']
  jurisdiction_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk classification of the counterparty\'s country of incorporation based on FATF grey/black lists, OFAC sanctioned country lists, and internal country risk policy. Drives KYC tier assignment and enhanced due diligence requirements.']
  kyc_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to this KYC compliance record, used for audit trails, regulatory submissions, and compliance officer tracking. Format: KYC- followed by alphanumeric characters.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the KYC compliance record indicating whether the counterparty has passed, is under review, or has failed the KYC process. Drives trading eligibility and credit decisions.']
  kyc_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the level of due diligence applied to this counterparty. Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) applies to high-risk counterparties such as state-owned NOCs, PEPs, or entities in high-risk jurisdictions. Standard applies to typical IOC/trading counterparties. Simplified applies to low-risk, regulated entities.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent periodic KYC review was completed. Distinct from verification_date (initial verification) and next_review_date (scheduled future review). Used to track review cadence compliance and identify overdue reviews.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic KYC review. May differ from expiry_date if an interim review is triggered by a risk event, sanctions alert, or change in counterparty ownership structure.']
  ofac_screening_result varchar [note: 'Result of screening the counterparty against the U.S. Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) and Blocked Persons List. A confirmed match prohibits all transactions. Potential matches require compliance officer review.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date on which the counterparty was first onboarded into the KYC compliance program. Represents the start of the commercial relationship from a compliance perspective and anchors the KYC lifecycle timeline.']
  pep_category varchar [note: 'Specific category of PEP classification when pep_flag is true. Distinguishes between domestic PEPs (e.g., government ministers), foreign PEPs (e.g., heads of state), international organization PEPs, close associates, and family members. Relevant for NOC counterparties with government-affiliated executives.']
  pep_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counterparty or any of its beneficial owners is classified as a Politically Exposed Person (PEP) — a current or former senior government official, state-owned enterprise executive, or close associate. PEP status mandates Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) per FATF Recommendation 12.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for KYC rejection when kyc_status is \'rejected\'. Documents the specific compliance deficiency, sanctions match, or documentation failure that resulted in rejection. Required for audit trail and regulatory reporting.']
  review_trigger varchar [note: 'Reason that initiated the most recent KYC review. Distinguishes between scheduled periodic reviews and event-driven reviews triggered by sanctions alerts, changes in beneficial ownership, adverse media findings, risk escalations, or regulatory requests.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric composite risk score (0.00–100.00) calculated from the weighted combination of jurisdiction risk, AML risk rating, PEP status, sanctions exposure, adverse media findings, and beneficial ownership complexity. Higher scores indicate greater compliance risk. Used to prioritize enhanced due diligence and monitoring resources.']
  sanctions_screening_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which the counterparty was screened against OFAC, EU, and UN sanctions lists. Sanctions screening must be refreshed at defined intervals and upon any material change in counterparty ownership or jurisdiction.']
  source_system_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of this KYC record in the originating compliance management system (e.g., Enablon HSE Management or dedicated KYC platform). Enables traceability from the Silver layer back to the operational system of record for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  trading_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counterparty is currently eligible for crude oil, natural gas, LNG, NGL, or refined product trading based on the current KYC status, sanctions screening results, AML risk rating, and document collection completeness. Set to false when KYC is expired, rejected, or sanctions match is confirmed.']
  ubo_count varchar [note: 'Number of ultimate beneficial owners (UBOs) identified and disclosed for this counterparty. A UBO is any natural person holding 25% or more direct or indirect ownership or control. Used to assess ownership complexity and completeness of beneficial ownership disclosure.']
  un_sanctions_screening_result varchar [note: 'Result of screening the counterparty against the United Nations Security Council consolidated sanctions list. Applies globally to all counterparties regardless of jurisdiction. A confirmed match prohibits all transactions under international law.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this KYC record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Updated on every status change, review completion, sanctions screening refresh, or document update. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the KYC verification process was completed and the counterparty\'s identity and documentation were confirmed by the compliance team. Marks the start of the KYC validity period.']
  waiver_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the compliance waiver expires when waiver_flag is true. After this date, trading eligibility is automatically suspended until full KYC compliance is restored. Null when no waiver is in effect.']
  waiver_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal compliance waiver has been granted for this KYC record, allowing temporary trading eligibility despite incomplete documentation or pending review. Waivers require senior compliance authority approval and are time-limited.']

  Note: 'Know Your Customer (KYC) compliance record for each counterparty, capturing KYC status, verification date, KYC tier (enhanced due diligence, standard, simplified), beneficial ownership disclosure, sanctions screening result (OFAC, EU, UN), politically exposed person (PEP) flag, anti-money laundering (AML) risk rating, document collection status, KYC expiry date, compliance officer, and next review date. Mandatory for all trading counterparties under FATF, OFAC, and internal compliance frameworks.'
}

Table customer.nomination {
  nomination_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for the customer delivery nomination record. Primary key for the nomination entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: A nomination is a formal customer delivery request — it must be tied to the commercial account making the request. nomination currently has delivery_point_id, petroleum_product_id, and operational fie']
  block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.block. Business justification: In PSA and concession regimes, crude lifting nominations must reference the producing block for production allocation, government reporting, and royalty calculation. Nomination scheduling and entitlem']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Nomination processing, scheduling coordination, and pipeline/terminal operations costs are allocated to logistics/operations cost centers for operational cost tracking and efficiency analysis.']
  delivery_point_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the nominated delivery point (terminal, pipeline custody transfer point, or discharge port) where the product is to be received by the customer.']
  joa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venture.joa. Business justification: JOA partners nominate equity crude liftings per JOA lifting procedures and nomination cycles. Required for JOA-compliant nomination workflow, operator approval process, partner lifting coordination, a']
  lease_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.lease. Business justification: Crude oil nominations are tied to specific lease production entitlements based on working interest and NRI. Essential for offtake scheduling, entitlement verification, and ensuring liftings match owne']
  nominating_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venture.partner. Business justification: Nominations represent partner equity holders requesting their entitlement share under PSA/JOA. Business requires tracking which venture partner is nominating (distinct from the commercial account buye']
  parent_nomination_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original nomination record from which this amended nomination was derived. Null for original nominations; populated for all amendment versions to maintain the amendment history chain.']
  petroleum_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.petroleum_product. Business justification: Nominations specify which petroleum product (crude grade, diesel, gasoline, propane, LNG) is being nominated for lifting or delivery. Core supply chain operation requiring product master data for spec']
  psa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venture.psa. Business justification: PSA contractors nominate cost recovery and profit oil liftings per PSA terms. Essential for PSA entitlement lifting execution, contractor nomination procedures, government approval workflow, and fisca']
  supply_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.contract. Business justification: Crude oil nominations frequently reference master supply or framework contracts that establish pricing formulas, quality specifications, and delivery terms. Essential for contract compliance verificat']
  actual_volume_lifted decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual quantity of product loaded or delivered as measured at the custody transfer point, expressed in the same unit of measure as nominated_volume. Populated post-lifting from terminal or metering records.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'The total number of amendments applied to this nomination since original submission. A value of zero indicates the nomination has not been amended. Used to track nomination stability and customer behavior patterns.']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Categorized reason code for the most recent amendment to this nomination. Supports analysis of nomination instability drivers and commercial risk patterns. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: volume_change|vessel_substitution|laycan_change|product_change|delivery_point_change|customer_request — promote to reference product]']
  bill_of_lading_date date [note: 'The date on the bill of lading issued at the load port upon completion of cargo loading. Serves as the pricing date for Dated Brent and other spot-referenced cargoes and triggers revenue recognition.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for nomination cancellation when nomination_status is \'cancelled\'. Captures commercial, operational, or force majeure circumstances leading to cancellation.']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the nomination was formally confirmed by the Oil Gas commercial/trading desk. Marks the transition from submitted to confirmed status and triggers downstream logistics scheduling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the nomination record was first created in the system. Audit trail field capturing the initial record creation event for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  delivery_terms varchar [note: 'The INCOTERMS 2020 delivery basis governing risk transfer and cost allocation between Oil Gas and the customer. Determines whether Oil Gas arranges freight (CIF/CFR) or the customer nominates the vessel (FOB). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB|CIF|CFR|DES|DAP|FCA|EXW — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  destination_country varchar [note: 'The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the final destination country for the cargo. Required for export licensing, OFAC/sanctions compliance screening, and country-of-destination reporting to regulatory bodies.']
  discharge_port varchar [note: 'The UN/LOCODE five-character code identifying the port or terminal where the cargo is to be discharged and delivered to the customer. Applicable for CIF, DES, and DAP delivery terms.']
  imo_number varchar [note: 'The International Maritime Organization (IMO) unique seven-digit vessel identification number prefixed with \'IMO\'. Permanent vessel identifier used for vetting, sanctions screening, and port state control compliance.']
  kyc_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer\'s KYC documentation has been verified and is current at the time of nomination submission. Supports AML compliance and counterparty due diligence requirements.']
  laycan_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the laycan window — the latest date by which the vessel must arrive and tender NOR. If the vessel arrives after this date, the charterer has the right to cancel the charter party.']
  laycan_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the laycan (laydays cancelling) window — the earliest date the vessel may arrive at the loading or discharge port and tender Notice of Readiness (NOR) under the charter party terms.']
  lifting_program_month varchar [note: 'The calendar month (YYYY-MM format) of the monthly lifting program to which this nomination belongs. Used to reconcile nominations against the monthly crude lifting schedule and production allocation.']
  loading_port varchar [note: 'The UN/LOCODE five-character code identifying the port or terminal from which the cargo is to be loaded onto the vessel. Applicable for FOB and similar delivery terms where Oil Gas is the seller at the load port.']
  nominated_volume decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of product formally requested by the customer in the nomination. Expressed in the unit of measure specified by the volume_uom field (BBL, MT, or MMBTU depending on product type and contract terms).']
  nomination_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the customer formally submitted the nomination request. Represents the principal business event date for the nomination lifecycle.']
  nomination_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle state of the nomination. Tracks progression from initial customer submission through commercial confirmation, amendment, cancellation, or expiry without fulfillment.']
  nomination_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nomination by commercial basis and delivery mode. Distinguishes between term contract liftings, spot sale cargoes, pipeline nominations, and product exchange arrangements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: term_contract|spot_sale|pipeline|cargo|exchange — promote to reference product]']
  pipeline_nomination_cycle varchar [note: 'For pipeline gas nominations, the NAESB/FERC nomination cycle in which this nomination was submitted (Timely, Evening, Intraday 1, 2, or 3). Null for marine cargo nominations.']
  price_currency varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the price differential and any fixed price components are denominated (typically USD for international crude and LNG trades).']
  price_differential decimal(10,4) [note: 'The contractual premium or discount (in USD per BBL or USD per MMBTU) applied to the benchmark pricing basis for this specific nomination. Reflects quality, location, and timing adjustments negotiated in the contract.']
  pricing_basis varchar [note: 'The benchmark price index against which the nominated cargo will be priced per the contract terms (e.g., Dated Brent, WTI Cushing, Platts Dubai, NYMEX Natural Gas). Drives price calculation at bill of lading date or delivery. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dated_brent|wti_cushing|platts_dubai|argus_sour|nymex_ng|ice_brent — promote to reference product]']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to the nomination, used in communications with the customer, terminal operators, and logistics teams. Follows the NOM- prefix convention.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The specific delivery date requested by the customer within the laycan window, or the target pipeline delivery date for non-vessel nominations. Used for scheduling and logistics planning.']
  sanctions_screened boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer, vessel, and destination country have been screened against OFAC, UN, EU, and UK sanctions lists prior to nomination confirmation. Must be True before confirmation_timestamp is populated.']
  shipper_code varchar [note: 'The FERC-assigned or pipeline operator-assigned code identifying the shipper of record for pipeline nominations. Required for FERC-regulated interstate pipeline capacity scheduling and balancing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this nomination record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer lakehouse. SAP_SD for SAP Sales and Distribution, AVOCET for Avocet Production Operations, ETRM for Energy Trading and Risk Management system.']
  tolerance_max_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum allowable percentage above the nominated volume that may be delivered without requiring a separate nomination amendment. Expressed as a positive percentage (e.g., 5.00 for 5% over).']
  tolerance_min_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum allowable percentage below the nominated volume that the customer may accept at delivery without triggering a contract shortfall or take-or-pay obligation. Expressed as a negative percentage (e.g., -5.00 for 5% under).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the nomination record was most recently modified. Updated on every status change, amendment, or data correction to support audit trail and change tracking requirements.']
  vessel_name varchar [note: 'The name of the tanker or LNG carrier nominated by the customer or charterer to lift the cargo. Populated for marine cargo nominations; null for pipeline nominations.']
  vessel_vetting_status varchar [note: 'The outcome of the vessel vetting process conducted against OCIMF SIRE or CDI inspection records. Approved vessels are cleared for loading; rejected vessels must be substituted; conditional approvals require additional review.']
  volume_boe decimal(18,4) [note: 'Nominated volume converted to Barrels of Oil Equivalent (BOE) for cross-commodity comparison, portfolio reporting, and SEC reserves disclosure alignment. Conversion factors applied per API/SPE standards.']
  volume_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure in which the nominated volume is expressed. BBL (barrels) for crude and liquid products, MT (metric tonnes) for LNG and bulk cargoes, MMBTU for natural gas, MCF/MMCF for pipeline gas volumes, M3 for metric volume reporting.']

  Note: 'Customer delivery nomination record capturing the formal request by a customer to lift or receive a cargo or pipeline volume under a term contract or spot sale. Includes nomination reference, nomination date, requested delivery window (laycan), delivery point, product grade, nominated volume (BBL, MT, MMBTU), vessel name and IMO number (if applicable), loading/discharge port, nomination status (submitted, confirmed, amended, cancelled, expired), confirming trader, and amendment history reference. Core operational record linking customer demand to logistics scheduling, vessel chartering, and terminal operations.'
}

Table customer.customer_lifting_schedule {
  customer_lifting_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each lifting schedule record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the lifting_schedule data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete planned/actual cargo lifting event with a full lifecycle.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: customer_lifting_schedule tracks planned and actual liftings for a customer account against a term offtake agreement. Despite being explicitly described as a customer-facing schedule, it has no direct']
  block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.block. Business justification: Lifting schedules are organized by producing block for production allocation, overlift/underlift tracking, and government reporting. Block-level lifting data is required for JOA cash calls and PSA cos']
  certificate_of_quality_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.certificate_of_quality. Business justification: Each actual cargo lifting requires a certificate of quality issued at loading for custody transfer. Essential for contractual compliance verification, customs clearance documentation, payment settleme']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Lifting operations (terminal handling, vessel coordination, demurrage) incur direct costs allocated to operational cost centers for crude marketing cost control and margin analysis.']
  joa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venture.joa. Business justification: Equity crude liftings are governed by JOA terms including lifting procedures, nomination deadlines, and entitlement calculations. Essential for JOA-compliant lifting program execution, partner entitle']
  lease_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.lease. Business justification: Lifting schedules execute lease-specific production entitlements for equity crude offtake. Core to revenue realization, entitlement balancing, and ensuring each partner lifts their entitled share per ']
  lifting_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venture.partner. Business justification: Lifting schedules track actual cargo liftings by entitled venture partners. Business requires partner-level lifting records for overlift/underlift reconciliation, imbalance settlement, and JIB reporti']
  loading_delivery_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_point. Business justification: Loading port should be a FK to delivery_point (which represents terminals/ports). This replaces the loading_port_name and loading_country_code strings with a proper relational link. Note: labeled as l']
  nomination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.nomination. Business justification: A lifting schedule is typically created FROM a customer nomination. This parent-child relationship links the planned schedule to the originating nomination request. The FK may be NULL initially if the']
  offtake_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.offtake_entitlement. Business justification: A customer lifting schedule is executed against a specific offtake entitlement — the entitlement defines the volume, product, and terms that the lifting schedule fulfills. The description explicitly s']
  primary_customer_crude_grade_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hydrocarbon product being lifted (e.g., crude oil grade, LNG, NGL, condensate). Links to the product master for grade specifications, quality parameters, and pricing basis.']
  production_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservoir.production_forecast. Business justification: Customer lifting schedules are operationally derived from reservoir production forecasts — schedulers allocate cargo liftings against forecast production volumes. This link supports the monthly liftin']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Crude liftings generate revenue recognized at field/asset level profit centers for upstream P&L reporting, production entitlement accounting, and field-level economics (netback analysis).']
  psa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venture.psa. Business justification: PSA contracts govern cost recovery oil and profit oil lifting entitlements and schedules. Required for PSA entitlement calculation, contractor share determination, government take allocation, and fisc']
  supply_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.contract. Business justification: Lifting schedules execute under supply or processing contracts that govern crude grades, delivery windows, and pricing mechanisms. Required for contract performance tracking, demurrage calculations, a']
  terminal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the terminal or loading port facility from which the cargo is lifted. Determines applicable measurement procedures, custody transfer protocols, and port authority requirements.']
  vessel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the nominated vessel (tanker, LNG carrier, or FPSO shuttle tanker) assigned to perform the lifting. Links to vessel master for IMO number, capacity, and flag state details.']
  actual_lifted_volume decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual volume of hydrocarbon product lifted at the terminal as measured by the custody transfer metering system, expressed in the unit defined by volume_unit. Populated upon completion of loading and issuance of the bill of lading. Null until lifting is completed.']
  actual_lifting_date date [note: 'Actual calendar date on which the cargo lifting commenced at the terminal or loading port. Compared against scheduled_lifting_date to compute lifting variance and assess operational performance. Null until lifting is in-progress or completed.']
  api_gravity decimal(6,2) [note: 'API gravity of the crude oil or condensate cargo as measured at the loading terminal, expressed in degrees API. Determines crude quality classification (light, medium, heavy), pricing differential, and refinery yield optimization. Not applicable for LNG/gas liftings.']
  bill_of_lading_date date [note: 'Date on which the bill of lading was issued, representing the official date of title transfer and the revenue recognition date under IFRS 15 / US GAAP for FOB cargo sales. Null until lifting is completed and B/L is issued.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Official bill of lading number issued by the vessel master or terminal operator upon completion of cargo loading. Serves as the primary legal document for title transfer, revenue recognition, and customs/export documentation. Critical for IFRS 15 revenue recognition trigger.']
  bs_w_percentage decimal(6,4) [note: 'Basic sediment and water content of the crude oil cargo expressed as a weight or volume percentage, as determined by the custody transfer measurement at the loading terminal. Deducted from gross standard volume to arrive at net standard volume for invoicing.']
  cargo_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique cargo reference number assigned by the seller or trading desk for internal tracking of this specific cargo parcel. Used for cross-referencing with trading systems, back-office settlement, and cargo documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lifting schedule record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  deferral_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for deferring a scheduled lifting (e.g., vessel unavailability, force majeure, terminal maintenance, buyer request, weather conditions). Populated only when lifting_status is \'deferred\'. Used for operational analysis and contractual force majeure documentation.']
  demurrage_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether demurrage charges are applicable for this lifting due to vessel detention beyond the agreed laytime at the loading terminal. True when actual time on berth exceeds the allowed laytime per the charter party or sale agreement.']
  demurrage_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of hours of demurrage incurred by the vessel at the loading terminal beyond the agreed laytime, as calculated from the Notice of Readiness (NOR) and Statement of Facts. Used to calculate demurrage charges payable under the charter party.']
  entitlement_volume decimal(18,4) [note: 'Contractual volume entitlement for this lifting as specified in the term offtake agreement or spot sale nomination, expressed in the unit defined by volume_unit (BBL or MT). Represents the buyer\'s right to lift under the agreement for this lifting window. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category.']
  gross_observed_volume decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total volume of the cargo as measured at observed temperature and pressure conditions at the loading terminal, before correction to standard conditions. Expressed in the unit defined by volume_unit. Used in custody transfer calculations per API MPMS.']
  gross_standard_volume decimal(18,4) [note: 'Volume of the cargo corrected to standard conditions (60°F / 15°C at 1 atm) using volume correction factors per API MPMS Chapter 11. The primary billing and revenue recognition volume for crude oil custody transfers.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the point of title transfer, risk transfer, and cost allocation between seller and buyer for this cargo lifting. FOB (Free on Board) is most common for crude oil liftings; CIF/CFR for delivered cargoes.']
  laycan_end_date date [note: 'End date of the laycan window — the latest date by which the nominated vessel must arrive at the loading port. If the vessel arrives after this date, the seller may have the right to cancel the cargo. Critical for demurrage and laytime calculations.']
  laycan_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the laycan (laydays cancelling) window — the earliest date the nominated vessel may arrive at the loading port to commence lifting operations. Defines the contractual arrival window for the vessel under the cargo sale terms.']
  lifting_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the lifting event, typically assigned by the terminal operator or production operator. Used for cross-referencing with counterparty documents, bill of lading, and cargo tracking systems. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  lifting_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of this lifting within the contract year or agreement period (e.g., 1st lifting, 2nd lifting). Used for tracking cumulative entitlement consumption, scheduling periodic liftings under annual offtake agreements, and partner allocation reporting.']
  lifting_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the lifting event in its operational lifecycle. Drives production allocation, revenue recognition triggers, and inventory management. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  lifting_tolerance_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Contractual tolerance percentage (e.g., ±5%) applied to the entitlement volume within which the actual lifted volume is considered compliant with the offtake agreement. Volumes outside this tolerance may trigger over/under-lift balancing obligations.']
  net_standard_volume decimal(18,4) [note: 'Gross standard volume minus the volume of sediment, water, and free water (BS&W deductions) at standard conditions. Represents the net merchantable hydrocarbon volume for invoicing, revenue recognition, and production allocation reporting.']
  nomination_deadline date [note: 'Contractual deadline by which the buyer must nominate the lifting vessel to the seller or terminal operator. Failure to nominate by this date may result in forfeiture of the lifting entitlement or deferral under the offtake agreement terms.']
  over_under_lift_status varchar [note: 'Classification of the lifting outcome relative to entitlement: over_lift (actual > entitlement + tolerance), under_lift (actual < entitlement - tolerance), balanced (within tolerance), or pending (lifting not yet completed). Drives JOA partner balancing and make-up lifting obligations.']
  price_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the cargo price and invoice are denominated (e.g., USD for US Dollar, EUR for Euro). Most international crude oil and LNG trades are denominated in USD.']
  price_differential decimal(10,4) [note: 'Contractual premium or discount applied to the reference pricing basis for this cargo, expressed in USD per BBL or USD per MT. Reflects crude quality differentials, location adjustments, and commercial negotiation outcomes. Confidential commercial term.']
  pricing_basis varchar [note: 'Reference price benchmark against which the cargo price is calculated (e.g., Dated Brent, WTI, Dubai/Oman, Platts Oman, JKM for LNG). Determines the pricing formula applied for revenue recognition and invoice generation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dated_brent|wti|dubai_oman|platts_oman|argus_sour|lng_jkm|lng_ttf — promote to reference product]']
  scheduled_lifting_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date on which the cargo lifting is scheduled to commence at the terminal or loading port, as agreed in the term offtake agreement or spot sale nomination. Used for production planning and inventory management. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category (day-level planning precision).']
  sulfur_content_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Sulfur content of the crude oil cargo expressed as a weight percentage (wt%). Classifies crude as sweet (<0.5% sulfur) or sour (>0.5% sulfur), directly impacting pricing differential, refinery processing requirements, and environmental compliance under IMO 2020 sulfur regulations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lifting schedule record was last modified. Tracks schedule revisions, status changes, and volume adjustments for audit and data lineage purposes. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.']
  vessel_imo_number varchar [note: 'International Maritime Organization (IMO) unique vessel identification number in the format IMO followed by 7 digits. Provides a globally unique, permanent vessel identifier for regulatory reporting, sanctions screening, and cargo tracking.']
  volume_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for all volume fields on this lifting record. BBL (barrels) is standard for crude oil and condensate; MT (metric tonnes) is standard for LNG and some crude trades; MMBTU for gas energy content; MCF for gas volume. Ensures consistent interpretation of entitlement and actual volume fields.']
  volume_variance decimal(18,4) [note: 'Difference between actual_lifted_volume and entitlement_volume, expressed in the unit defined by volume_unit. Positive value indicates over-lift; negative value indicates under-lift. Used for production allocation reconciliation, over/under-lift accounting, and partner balancing under JOA terms.']

  Note: 'Planned and actual lifting schedule for a customer account against a term offtake agreement or spot sale, capturing lifting number, scheduled lifting date, actual lifting date, product, volume entitlement (BBL or MT), actual lifted volume, terminal or loading port, vessel, bill of lading number, lifting status (scheduled, in-progress, completed, deferred), and variance from plan. Supports production allocation, inventory management, and revenue recognition for crude oil and LNG liftings.'
}

Table customer.offtake_entitlement {
  offtake_entitlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the offtake entitlement record. Primary key for this entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Entity role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — represents a contractual entitlement container defining a counterparty\'s right to lift volumes under a term offtake agreement or PSA lifting schedule.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: offtake_entitlement defines a customer\'s entitlement under a term offtake agreement. It already has customer_counterparty_id (→ customer_counterparty) but lacks a direct FK to the commercial account. ']
  block_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.block. Business justification: Production entitlement allocation by block is a standard E&P operational and regulatory requirement. Blocks are the primary unit for production sharing and government reporting. An E&P production acco']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Entitlement administration, overlift/underlift reconciliation, and JV partner coordination costs are allocated to commercial/JV administration cost centers for overhead tracking and partner billing.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or counterparty (IOC, NOC, refiner, trader, or wholesale buyer) holding this offtake entitlement. Identifies the lifting party in the JOA or PSA partner lifting schedule.']
  crude_grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.crude_grade. Business justification: PSA/JOA equity crude entitlements specify field-specific crude grades for cost recovery and profit oil calculations. Grade-specific pricing differentials, quality specs, and blending constraints are e']
  delivery_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_point. Business justification: Offtake entitlement delivery_point string should be a FK reference to the delivery_point master table. This enables proper referential integrity and allows joining to get full delivery point details. ']
  discovery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.discovery. Business justification: PSA/JOA entitlement allocation reports and commerciality declarations require linking offtake entitlements to the originating discovery. Entitled volumes, equity share, and profit-oil calculations are']
  joa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venture.joa. Business justification: Offtake entitlements derive from JOA working interest and net revenue interest allocations. Critical for JOA-based entitlement calculation, equity production allocation, partner balancing, and overlif']
  license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.license. Business justification: Offtake entitlements are legally grounded in the exploration/production license. License terms define royalty rates, net revenue interest, and working interest percentages that directly determine enti']
  offtake_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent term offtake agreement, Joint Operating Agreement (JOA), or Production Sharing Agreement (PSA) under which this entitlement is granted. Links the entitlement to its governing commercial contract.']
  operator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.operator. Business justification: Offtake entitlements specify the operator managing production and lifting operations for the entitled volumes. Critical for production accounting, JIB reconciliation, and operational coordination betw']
  petroleum_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.petroleum_product. Business justification: Offtake entitlements specify which petroleum product the customer is entitled to lift under PSC/JOA agreements. Essential for equity crude allocation, gas balancing, and revenue distribution calculati']
  processing_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.contract. Business justification: Offtake entitlements often governed by processing, transportation, or handling service contracts (e.g., tolling agreements, pipeline capacity contracts). Essential for cost allocation, tariff reconcil']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Equity production entitlements (working interest, net revenue interest) are allocated to field-level profit centers for upstream accounting, reserve booking, and field economics. Core upstream financi']
  psa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to venture.psa. Business justification: PSA profit oil and cost recovery volumes establish contractor offtake entitlements. Essential for PSA entitlement determination, cost recovery ceiling tracking, profit oil split calculation, and contr']
  reserves_estimate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservoir.reserves_estimate. Business justification: Offtake entitlements in PSA/JOA structures must be traceable to certified reserves estimates for SEC/PRMS regulatory reporting. Auditors and reserves certifiers require that entitled volumes are bound']
  reservoir_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reservoir.reservoir. Business justification: In PSC/JOA arrangements with reservoir-specific cost recovery pools (e.g., separate accounting for deepwater vs shallow reservoirs in same field), offtake entitlements are calculated and tracked at re']
  afe_number varchar [note: 'Authorization for Expenditure (AFE) number associated with the capital or operating program linked to this entitlement period. Used for JIB cost allocation and partner billing reconciliation in SAP Joint Venture Accounting.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the governing commercial agreement type under which this entitlement is established. Determines the applicable accounting treatment, partner balancing rules, and regulatory reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PSA|JOA|term_offtake|spot|GSA|LNG_SPA|FPSO_offtake — promote to reference product]']
  balancing_method varchar [note: 'Method used to resolve overlift/underlift imbalances between JOA partners at period end. Cash settlement involves monetary payment; in-kind involves physical volume make-up; carry-forward defers the imbalance to the next period; deemed-sold treats the imbalance as a notional sale.']
  collateral_type varchar [note: 'Type of financial security instrument required from the customer to support credit exposure under this entitlement. Determined by credit rating and exposure level. Letter of credit and bank guarantees are standard instruments in international crude oil and LNG trading.']
  cost_recovery_volume decimal(18,4) [note: 'Volume of cost oil or cost gas allocated to the contractor party for recovery of capital and operating expenditures under a Production Sharing Agreement (PSA). Expressed in the entitlement unit of measure. Applicable only to PSA-governed entitlements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this offtake entitlement record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit trail creation anchor for SOX compliance and data lineage tracking.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum credit exposure approved for this customer under this offtake entitlement, expressed in the transaction currency. Enforced during cargo nomination and lifting authorization workflows to manage counterparty credit risk.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'External or internal credit rating assigned to the customer counterparty (e.g., S&P: AAA, AA+, BBB-; Moody\'s: Aaa, Baa1; or internal rating code). Used in credit risk assessment and determination of collateral or letter of credit requirements for lifting authorization.']
  cumulative_lifted_volume decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total volume actually lifted by the customer against this entitlement from the period start date through the most recent reconciliation date. Expressed in the entitlement unit of measure. Used to compute remaining entitlement and overlift/underlift position.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which monetary values (entitlement value, overlift/underlift value, settlement amounts) are denominated. Typically USD for international crude and LNG transactions.']
  delivery_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used to deliver lifted volumes from the production source to the delivery point. Determines applicable logistics, demurrage, and PHMSA/FERC regulatory requirements.']
  entitled_volume decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total contractual volume the customer is entitled to lift during the entitlement period, expressed in the designated unit of measure (BBL, MT, or MMBTU). Represents the gross entitlement before any adjustments for overlift/underlift carry-forward.']
  entitlement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this offtake entitlement record, typically assigned by the operator or JV administrator. Used in JIB statements, lifting schedules, and partner correspondence. Aligns with SAP JVA document numbering conventions.']
  entitlement_period_end date [note: 'The last calendar date of the entitlement period. Nullable for open-ended or evergreen offtake arrangements. Defines the closing boundary of the lifting window. Used in overlift/underlift position calculations and period-end reconciliation.']
  entitlement_period_start date [note: 'The first calendar date of the entitlement period during which the customer is contractually authorized to lift volumes. Defines the opening boundary of the lifting window under the term offtake agreement or PSA schedule.']
  entitlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the offtake entitlement. Drives operational eligibility for scheduling lifts and triggers credit/compliance checks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|suspended|terminated|pending|expired|cancelled — promote to reference product if additional states are required]']
  equity_share_pct decimal(8,6) [note: 'The customer\'s equity entitlement percentage, which may differ from Working Interest (WI) due to PSA cost recovery provisions, carried interest arrangements, or government back-in rights. Used in PSA lifting schedule calculations to determine the contractor\'s equity oil/gas share.']
  imbalance_tolerance_volume decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum allowable overlift or underlift volume before formal balancing or cash settlement is triggered. Expressed in the entitlement unit of measure. Contractually defined in the JOA or offtake agreement to accommodate operational variability in cargo sizes.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the allocation of costs, risks, and responsibilities between seller and buyer for the delivery of lifted volumes. Governs title transfer, insurance obligations, and freight cost allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB|CIF|CFR|DES|DAP|FCA|EXW|DDP|CPT|CIP — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  jib_code varchar [note: 'Joint Interest Billing (JIB) code used to allocate costs and revenues to the correct partner account in the joint venture accounting system. Enables accurate partner billing and cost recovery tracking in SAP Joint Venture Accounting per COPAS standards.']
  kyc_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has completed Know Your Customer (KYC) due diligence verification as required for counterparty onboarding under anti-money laundering (AML) and sanctions compliance programs. Must be True before entitlement activation.']
  last_reconciliation_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which the cumulative lifted volume, remaining entitlement, and overlift/underlift position were formally reconciled against production allocation records. Used to determine the currency of the entitlement position data.']
