  project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.project. Business justification: Emission sources on projects tracked for cost allocation, asset management, and project environmental compliance.']
  rig_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to drilling.rig. Business justification: Rig engines, mud pits, and flare systems are registered emission sources under EPA NSPS/NESHAP subparts. Linking emission_source to the rig enables rig-level GHG inventory, air permit compliance track']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Emission sources on projects tracked to WBS for cost allocation, capitalization, and project asset management.']
  well_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.well_asset. Business justification: Well venting, flaring, and fugitive emissions are tracked as emission sources for EPA Subpart W reporting, state GHG programs, and ESG disclosure. Well FK required for production-normalized emissions ']
  cems_installed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Continuous Emission Monitoring System is installed on this emission source for real-time measurement of pollutant concentrations and flow rates.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the emission source was placed into service and began operations. Used to determine applicability of regulatory requirements and vintage-based emission standards.']
  control_device_installed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an air pollution control device is installed on this emission source (e.g., catalytic converter, scrubber, vapor recovery unit, flare, thermal oxidizer).']
  control_device_type varchar [note: 'Type of air pollution control device installed. Examples: catalytic converter, selective catalytic reduction (SCR), vapor recovery unit (VRU), thermal oxidizer, flare, scrubber, baghouse, electrostatic precipitator. Null if no control device.']
  control_efficiency_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Design or tested control efficiency of the air pollution control device as a percentage (0-100). Represents the fraction of pollutant removed or destroyed. Used to calculate controlled emissions from uncontrolled emission rates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this emission source record was first created in the system. ISO 8601 format with timezone.']
  data_quality_tier varchar [note: 'EPA GHG Mandatory Reporting Rule data quality tier for emission calculations. Tier 4 (highest): CEMS or direct measurement. Tier 3: fuel sampling and analysis. Tier 2: default higher heating values with metered fuel. Tier 1 (lowest): default emission factors. Higher tiers provide greater accuracy.']
  decommissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the emission source was permanently retired from service. Null if still in service. Used for historical emissions inventory and asset retirement obligations.']
  emission_factor_source varchar [note: 'Reference to the emission factor database or methodology used for this source. Examples: EPA AP-42 Table 1.4-1, EPA Part 98 Table C-1, API Compendium, IPCC Guidelines, site-specific testing. Used for audit trail and methodology transparency.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of equipment or unit operation that constitutes the emission source. Examples: reciprocating engine, gas turbine, glycol dehydrator, condensate tank, pneumatic controller, compressor seal, wellhead, separator.']
  esg_reporting_scope varchar [note: 'GHG Protocol scope classification for ESG and sustainability reporting. Scope 1: direct emissions from owned/controlled sources. Scope 2: indirect emissions from purchased electricity/steam. Scope 3: value chain emissions. Used for CDP, TCFD, and investor disclosures.']
  fuel_heating_value decimal(12,3) [note: 'Higher heating value of the fuel in million British thermal units per unit volume or mass (MMBtu/scf, MMBtu/bbl, MMBtu/ton). Used for energy-based emission calculations per EPA Mandatory Reporting Rule.']
  fuel_heating_value_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the fuel heating value. Must align with fuel consumption reporting units.']
  geographic_coordinates varchar [note: 'Latitude and longitude of the emission source location in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Format: latitude,longitude. Used for spatial analysis, dispersion modeling, and regulatory reporting (EPA Facility Registry Service).']
  ghg_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this emission source is subject to EPA GHG Mandatory Reporting Rule (40 CFR Part 98). True if source emits 25,000 metric tons CO2e or more per year.']
  h2s_concentration_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Concentration of hydrogen sulfide in the gas stream or emissions from this source, measured in parts per million by volume. Critical for worker safety (OSHA PEL 10 ppm), sour gas handling procedures, and sulfur dioxide emissions from flaring or combustion.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this emission source record was last updated. ISO 8601 format with timezone. Used for change detection and data freshness assessment.']
  ldar_component_count varchar [note: 'Number of fugitive emission components (valves, flanges, connectors, pumps, compressor seals) associated with this emission source that are monitored under the LDAR program. Used for leak rate estimation and inspection scheduling.']
  ldar_program_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this emission source (typically fugitive equipment components) is subject to a Leak Detection and Repair program under EPA NSPS, NESHAP, or state regulations.']
  monitoring_methodology varchar [note: 'Method used to quantify emissions from this source. CEMS: Continuous Emission Monitoring System. PEMS: Predictive Emission Monitoring System. Fuel analysis: lab testing of fuel composition. Engineering calculation: equipment-specific algorithms. Emission factor: EPA AP-42 or industry factors. Mass balance: material input-output accounting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CEMS|PEMS|fuel_analysis|engineering_calculation|emission_factor|mass_balance|direct_measurement — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  neshap_subpart_applicable varchar [note: 'EPA NESHAP subpart(s) applicable to this emission source for hazardous air pollutant (HAP) control. Examples: Subpart HH (oil and natural gas production), Subpart ZZZZ (stationary reciprocating internal combustion engines). Null if no NESHAP applies.']
  norm_present boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Naturally Occurring Radioactive Material (radium-226, radium-228) is present in scale, sludge, or waste streams from this emission source. Triggers special handling, disposal, and worker protection requirements.']
  nsps_subpart_applicable varchar [note: 'EPA NSPS subpart(s) applicable to this emission source. Examples: Subpart OOOO (oil and gas well completions), Subpart OOOOa (new/modified sources), Subpart Dc (steam generating units). Null if no NSPS applies.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the emission source. Active: in regular operation. Standby: available but not operating. Idle: temporarily shut down. Maintenance: undergoing repair or inspection. Decommissioned: permanently retired. Planned: not yet commissioned.']
  permit_emission_limit decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum allowable emission rate or annual emission quantity specified in the air permit for this source. Used for compliance tracking and exceedance detection. Unit of measure specified in permit_emission_limit_unit.']
  permit_emission_limit_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the permit emission limit. Must match the permit condition language.']
  rated_capacity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum design capacity or nameplate rating of the emission source. For combustion equipment: maximum heat input (MMBtu/hr) or power output (MW). For storage tanks: working capacity (bbl). For compressors: horsepower (HP).']
  rated_capacity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the rated capacity of the emission source. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MMBtu/hr|MW|HP|bbl|m3|scf/day|BOPD|MCFD — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_threshold_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this emission source is subject to regulatory reporting thresholds (EPA Title V, GHG Mandatory Reporting Rule, state air permits). True if source meets or exceeds applicability thresholds.']
  source_category varchar [note: 'GHG Protocol source category for emissions inventory and ESG reporting. Aligns with Scope 1 direct emissions classification under ISO 14064-1.']
  source_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the emission source for identification and reporting purposes. Examples: Flare Stack 1A, Compressor Engine 3B, Storage Tank 402, Glycol Dehydrator Unit 5.']
  source_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the emission source, typically assigned by facility HSE team. Used in regulatory filings, permits, and internal tracking systems. Must be unique within the facility.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the emission source by emission mechanism. Combustion: engines, turbines, heaters, boilers. Venting: controlled release of gas. Flaring: combustion of waste gas. Fugitive: leaks from equipment. Process: chemical reactions. Storage: tank breathing losses. Loading: transfer operations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: combustion|venting|flaring|fugitive|process|storage|loading|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  stack_diameter_meters decimal(6,3) [note: 'Inside diameter of the emission stack at the exit point in meters. Used for exit velocity calculation and dispersion modeling.']
  stack_height_meters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical height of the emission stack or release point above ground level in meters. Used for air dispersion modeling and good engineering practice (GEP) stack height determination.']
  title_v_major_source boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this emission source contributes to the facility being classified as a Title V major source under the Clean Air Act (potential to emit 100 tons/year of any criteria pollutant or 10/25 tons/year of HAPs).']

  Note: 'Master record for stationary and fugitive emission sources at oil and gas facilities subject to GHG and criteria pollutant reporting. Captures source type (combustion, venting, flaring, fugitive, process), emission source ID, facility, process unit, fuel or feedstock type, applicable regulatory thresholds (EPA Title V, GHG MRR), monitoring methodology, and operational status. Supports EPA GHG Mandatory Reporting Rule (40 CFR Part 98) and ESG emissions inventory.'
}

Table hse.ghg_emission {
  ghg_emission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the greenhouse gas emission record.']
  asset_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility or site where the emission occurred.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: GHG emissions reported by legal entity for EPA GHGRP, state reporting, SEC climate disclosure, and carbon tax compliance.']
  crude_grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.crude_grade. Business justification: Crude grade carbon intensity calculations require grade-specific API gravity and sulfur content for Scope 3 upstream emissions, OPGEE modeling, and ESG disclosure under TCFD and CDP frameworks.']
  emission_source_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific emission source unit or equipment (e.g., flare, combustion unit, fugitive source).']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Carbon costs (offsets, taxes, compliance) posted to GL accounts for financial reporting, ESG cost tracking, and P&L management.']
  ldar_survey_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.ldar_survey. Business justification: GHG emission records can be calculated from LDAR survey results — ldar_survey captures total_estimated_emissions_co2e_metric_tons and total_estimated_emissions_mcf. Linking ghg_emission to the origina']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: GHG emissions reporting fulfills specific regulatory obligations (EPA GHGRP, state programs). Linking emissions records to obligations supports compliance verification, deadline tracking, and regulato']
  petroleum_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.petroleum_product. Business justification: GHG emission calculations require product-specific emission factors and carbon intensity values. Product identification is mandatory for EPA Subpart C/W reporting, Scope 3 emissions tracking, and ESG ']
  refined_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.refined_product. Business justification: Refined product emissions tracking requires product-specific heating values, renewable content percentage, and carbon intensity for EPA GHGRP Subpart MM reporting, LCFS credit generation, and Scope 3 ']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_regulatory_filing. Business justification: GHG emissions data populates regulatory filings (annual GHG reports, ESG disclosures). Linking emissions to filings supports filing preparation, audit trail, data traceability, and regulatory verifica']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.regulatory_submission. Business justification: GHG emissions are reported via mandatory regulatory submissions (EPA Subpart W, GHGRP). Linking ghg_emission to the regulatory_submission it was included in enables GHG inventory-to-filing traceabilit']
  seismic_survey_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to exploration.seismic_survey. Business justification: GHG emissions from seismic vessel engines, helicopter support, and explosive detonations are tracked per survey for ESG reporting and carbon accounting. Operators must allocate Scope 1/2 emissions to ']
  activity_data_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the activity data (e.g., MMBTU, MCF, barrels, tonnes).']
  activity_data_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantitative activity data used in the emission calculation (e.g., fuel consumed, gas flared, production volume).']
  calculation_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments regarding the emission calculation, methodology, or data quality.']
  carbon_offset_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a carbon offset or credit has been applied to this emission.']
  carbon_offset_tonnes decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of carbon offset credits applied to this emission, measured in CO2e tonnes.']
  co2e_tonnes decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total greenhouse gas emission expressed in carbon dioxide equivalent (CO2e) metric tonnes, calculated by multiplying GHG quantity by its global warming potential.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the emission record was first created in the system.']
  data_quality_tier varchar [note: 'Data quality tier classification per EPA reporting requirements: tier 1 (highest accuracy), tier 2, tier 3, tier 4 (lowest accuracy/default factors).']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system or application from which the emission data was captured (e.g., Enablon, OSIsoft PI, manual entry).']
  emission_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the emission event occurred or was measured, if available at event-level granularity.']
  emission_factor_source varchar [note: 'Source or reference for the emission factor used (e.g., EPA default, IPCC, site-specific test).']
  emission_factor_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the emission factor (e.g., kg CO2e per MMBTU, tonnes CO2 per barrel).']
  emission_factor_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Emission factor applied in the calculation, representing the quantity of GHG emitted per unit of activity.']
  emission_source_category varchar [note: 'Category of the emission source: combustion, flaring, venting, fugitive, process, or other.']
  epa_subpart_code varchar [note: 'EPA 40 CFR Part 98 subpart code applicable to this emission source (e.g., Subpart W for petroleum and natural gas systems).']
  esg_disclosure_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this emission is included in ESG or TCFD sustainability disclosures.']
  ghg_gas_type varchar [note: 'Type of greenhouse gas emitted: CO2 (carbon dioxide), CH4 (methane), N2O (nitrous oxide), HFC (hydrofluorocarbon), PFC (perfluorocarbon), SF6 (sulfur hexafluoride).']
  ghg_quantity_tonnes decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the specific greenhouse gas emitted, measured in metric tonnes.']
  global_warming_potential decimal(10,2) [note: 'Global warming potential factor for the gas type, used to convert to CO2 equivalent (e.g., CH4 = 25, N2O = 298).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the emission record was last modified.']
  measurement_uncertainty_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated uncertainty of the emission measurement or calculation, expressed as a percentage.']
  net_emission_co2e_tonnes decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net greenhouse gas emission after applying carbon offsets, measured in CO2e tonnes.']
  quantification_methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology used to quantify the emission: direct measurement, emission factor, mass balance, engineering estimate, default factor, or continuous monitoring.']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this emission record is subject to mandatory regulatory reporting (e.g., EPA GHGRP).']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the reporting period for which the emission is calculated.']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the reporting period for which the emission is calculated.']
  scope_classification varchar [note: 'GHG Protocol scope classification: Scope 1 (direct emissions), Scope 2 (indirect emissions from purchased energy), Scope 3 (other indirect emissions).']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the emission data was verified.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Verification status of the emission data: unverified, verified, third-party verified, or pending verification.']
  verifier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party verifier or internal auditor who verified the emission data.']

  Note: 'Greenhouse gas (GHG) emission measurement and calculation record capturing CO2, CH4, N2O, and other GHG emissions by source, facility, and reporting period. Captures emission source, gas type, quantification methodology (direct measurement, emission factor, mass balance), activity data, emission factor applied, calculated CO2e tonnes, data quality tier, and verification status. Supports EPA 40 CFR Part 98 annual GHG reporting and ESG/TCFD disclosure.'
}

  well_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.well_asset. Business justification: Well spills (flowback, produced water, wellhead equipment leak) require well-specific linkage for state regulatory reporting, reserves impact assessment (lost production), well integrity investigation']
  bsee_notification_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the spill occurred on offshore facilities under BSEE jurisdiction and meets incident reporting thresholds, triggering mandatory notification to BSEE.']
  bsee_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the offshore spill was reported to BSEE, demonstrating compliance with immediate notification requirements for offshore incidents.']
  cleanup_completion_date date [note: 'Date when active cleanup operations were completed and the site was transitioned to monitoring or remediation phase, or declared clean by regulatory authorities.']
  cleanup_method varchar [note: 'Primary cleanup or remediation method employed to remove or treat the released substance from the environment. Selection depends on substance type, volume, receiving environment, and regulatory approval. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mechanical_recovery|chemical_dispersant|in_situ_burning|bioremediation|excavation|natural_attenuation|combination — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cleanup_start_date date [note: 'Date when active cleanup and recovery operations commenced following containment activities. Used for tracking response timeline and regulatory compliance.']
  confirmed_volume_barrels decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final confirmed volume of the spill in barrels (BBL) after investigation, measurement, and reconciliation. This is the official volume used for regulatory reporting, financial liability, and environmental impact assessment.']
  containment_actions_taken varchar [note: 'Narrative description of immediate containment measures deployed to prevent further spread of the released substance, including booms, berms, dikes, absorbent materials, or emergency shutdown procedures.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the spill event record was first created in the HSE management system. Part of audit trail for regulatory compliance and data governance.']
  environmental_impact_assessment_completed boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a formal environmental impact assessment or natural resource damage assessment has been completed for the spill event, as required for significant releases.']
  estimated_cleanup_cost_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost in United States Dollars for spill response, containment, cleanup, remediation, and regulatory compliance activities. Used for financial planning, insurance claims, and liability assessment.']
  estimated_volume_barrels decimal(15,2) [note: 'Initial estimated volume of the spill in barrels (BBL), typically provided during immediate notification before confirmed measurements are available. Used for rapid response planning and regulatory notification.']
  impacted_area_extent_acres decimal(12,2) [note: 'The geographic extent of the area affected by the spill measured in acres, including soil contamination, water body surface area, or shoreline length. Used for environmental impact assessment and remediation planning.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the spill event record was most recently updated. Part of audit trail for regulatory compliance and data governance.']
  nrc_notification_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the spill meets EPA National Response Center reporting thresholds based on substance type, volume, and receiving environment. Determines immediate federal notification obligations.']
  nrc_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the spill was reported to the EPA National Response Center, demonstrating compliance with immediate notification requirements (typically within 24 hours of discovery).']
  nrc_report_number varchar [note: 'Official report number assigned by the EPA National Response Center upon notification, used for tracking federal regulatory follow-up and cross-referencing with other agency reports.']
  onshore_offshore_indicator varchar [note: 'Classification indicating whether the spill occurred in an onshore facility, offshore platform or vessel, coastal zone, or inland waterway. Determines applicable regulatory jurisdiction and response protocols.']
  receiving_environment varchar [note: 'The environmental medium or compartment into which the released substance was discharged. Critical for determining environmental impact severity, cleanup methods, and regulatory notification requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: soil|surface_water|groundwater|open_ocean|coastal_water|wetland|containment_system|paved_surface — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_closure_date date [note: 'Date when the spill event was officially closed by regulatory authorities (EPA, BSEE, state agency) following completion of cleanup, remediation, and all required reporting and documentation.']
  release_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the root cause or contributing factor that led to the unplanned release. Used for trend analysis, preventive action planning, and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equipment_failure|corrosion|human_error|operational_upset|natural_event|third_party_damage|unknown|under_investigation — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  release_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the specific cause, failure mode, or sequence of events that resulted in the spill. Includes equipment identifiers, failure mechanisms, and contributing factors identified during investigation.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of long-term remediation activities for soil, groundwater, or ecological restoration following initial cleanup. Tracks progress toward final site closure and regulatory approval.']
  spill_discovery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the spill was discovered or detected by personnel, monitoring systems, or third parties. May differ from occurrence time for slow leaks or delayed detection.']
  spill_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate (decimal degrees) of the spill location for mapping, spatial analysis, and regulatory reporting. Critical for offshore spills and environmental impact assessment.']
  spill_location_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the specific location within the facility or operational area where the spill occurred, including equipment identifiers, unit names, or geographic landmarks.']
  spill_longitude decimal(11,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate (decimal degrees) of the spill location for mapping, spatial analysis, and regulatory reporting. Critical for offshore spills and environmental impact assessment.']
  spill_occurrence_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the unplanned release or spill event occurred or was first observed. This is the principal business event timestamp for the spill.']
  spill_reference_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier or case number assigned to the spill event for regulatory reporting, insurance claims, and cross-system tracking. May include EPA NRC report number, BSEE incident number, or internal case identifier.']
  spill_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the spill event indicating the stage of response, investigation, cleanup, remediation, and regulatory closure activities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reported|under_investigation|containment_in_progress|cleanup_in_progress|remediation_in_progress|monitoring|closed|regulatory_review_pending — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  state_agency_notification_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether state environmental or emergency management agency notification is required based on spill location, volume, and state-specific reporting thresholds.']
  state_agency_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the spill was reported to the applicable state environmental or emergency management agency, demonstrating compliance with state-level notification requirements.']
  uscg_notification_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether United States Coast Guard notification is required for spills in navigable waters, coastal zones, or offshore areas under USCG jurisdiction per OPA 90 requirements.']
  uscg_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the spill was reported to the United States Coast Guard for spills affecting navigable waters or coastal zones.']
  volume_recovered_barrels decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total volume of released substance successfully recovered during cleanup operations, measured in barrels. Used to calculate net environmental loss and cleanup effectiveness.']
  volume_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used for reporting spill volume, accommodating different regulatory requirements and substance types. Barrels for crude oil, gallons for refined products, pounds for chemicals.']
  wildlife_impact_reported boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the spill resulted in documented impacts to wildlife, including fish, birds, marine mammals, or protected species. Triggers additional regulatory reporting and remediation requirements.']

  Note: 'Environmental spill and release event record capturing unplanned releases of crude oil, refined products, chemicals, produced water, drilling fluids, or other hazardous materials from oil and gas operations. Captures spill date/time, location coordinates (onshore/offshore, GPS), substance released, estimated and confirmed volume (barrels, gallons, or pounds), release cause (equipment failure, corrosion, human error, natural event), receiving environment (soil, water body, groundwater, open water), impacted area extent, regulatory notification status and timestamps (BSEE, EPA NRC, state agency, USCG), containment actions taken, cleanup method, remediation status, and final closure date. Links to parent incident record for unified HSE event tracking. Supports BSEE, EPA National Response Center, OPA 90, and state spill reporting requirements.'
}

  calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the detector or instrument was last calibrated prior to this measurement.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional comments, observations, or notes related to the monitoring event, sample collection, or measurement conditions.']
  compliance_program varchar [note: 'Name of the compliance program or regulatory framework this monitoring supports (e.g., LDAR, Title V Air Permit, SPCC, ISO 14001 EMS).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this environmental monitoring record was first created in the system, in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  detector_instrument_type varchar [note: 'Type or model of detector, sensor, or analytical instrument used for the measurement (e.g., fixed H2S detector, portable gas monitor, water quality probe, noise dosimeter).']
  evacuation_event_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether an evacuation event was triggered as a result of this measurement or alarm (True if evacuation occurred, False otherwise).']
  exceedance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the measured value exceeded the applicable regulatory limit or alarm threshold (True if exceeded, False otherwise).']
  laboratory_analysis_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for laboratory analysis if the sample was sent to a laboratory for detailed analysis.']
  laboratory_certification_number varchar [note: 'Certification or accreditation number of the laboratory that performed the analysis.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this environmental monitoring record was last modified in the system, in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric value of the measurement result.']
  monitoring_frequency_required varchar [note: 'Required monitoring frequency per regulatory permit or internal HSE program (e.g., continuous, hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, annual).']
  monitoring_type varchar [note: 'Type of environmental or occupational monitoring conducted (ambient air quality, groundwater, surface water, soil, noise, H2S fixed detector, H2S portable detector, toxic gas, personal exposure, area monitoring). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ambient_air_quality|groundwater|surface_water|soil|noise|h2s_fixed|h2s_portable|toxic_gas|personal_exposure|area_monitoring — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  muster_station_activation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether muster stations were activated as a result of this measurement or alarm (True if activated, False otherwise).']
  parameter_measured varchar [note: 'Specific environmental or occupational parameter measured (e.g., VOCs, benzene, H2S ppm, TSS, pH, TDS, noise dBA, particulate matter PM2.5, CO, LEL).']
  pressure_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for barometric pressure (millibars, inches of mercury, kilopascals, pounds per square inch).']
  quality_control_status varchar [note: 'Quality control status of the measurement or analysis (passed, failed, pending, not applicable).']
  regulatory_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Applicable regulatory limit, threshold, or alarm level for the measured parameter (e.g., OSHA PEL, IDLH, STEL, EPA permit limit).']
  regulatory_limit_type varchar [note: 'Type of regulatory limit or threshold (PEL - Permissible Exposure Limit, STEL - Short-Term Exposure Limit, IDLH - Immediately Dangerous to Life or Health, Ceiling, TWA - Time-Weighted Average, Permit Limit, Action Level, Alarm Threshold). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pel|stel|idlh|ceiling|twa|permit_limit|action_level|alarm_threshold — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  relative_humidity decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity percentage at the time of measurement.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the monitoring result was reviewed and validated by HSE personnel.']
  reviewed_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the HSE professional or supervisor who reviewed and validated the monitoring result.']
  sample_collection_method varchar [note: 'Method or protocol used for sample collection (e.g., grab sample, composite sample, continuous monitoring, personal air sampling).']
  sample_date date [note: 'Date when the environmental or occupational sample was collected or measurement was taken.']
  sample_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the sample was collected or measurement was recorded, in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  sampled_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the employee or contractor who collected the sample or performed the measurement.']
  temperature_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for temperature (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the measured value (e.g., ppm, mg/L, dBA, µg/m³, percent LEL, pH units).']
  wind_direction varchar [note: 'Wind direction at the time of measurement, typically in compass degrees (0-360) or cardinal direction (N, NE, E, SE, S, SW, W, NW).']
  wind_speed decimal(10,2) [note: 'Wind speed at the time of measurement, typically in meters per second or miles per hour.']
  wind_speed_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for wind speed (meters per second, miles per hour, kilometers per hour, knots).']

  Note: 'Environmental and occupational exposure monitoring record capturing ambient air quality, water quality, soil, noise, and hazardous gas measurements at or near oil and gas facilities. Covers both environmental compliance monitoring and occupational hygiene monitoring including H2S fixed/portable detector readings, personal exposure monitoring, and area monitoring. Captures monitoring type (air quality, groundwater, surface water, soil, noise, H2S, toxic gas), monitoring station or sample point, detector/instrument type, sample date and time, parameter measured (VOCs, benzene, H2S ppm, TSS, pH, TDS), measured value and units, applicable regulatory limit or alarm threshold (OSHA PEL, IDLH, STEL), exceedance/alarm flag, wind direction and speed, muster station activation flag, evacuation event flag, and laboratory analysis reference. Supports EPA permit compliance, OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1000 exposure limits, and ISO 14001 environmental monitoring programs.'
}

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|acknowledged|accepted|rejected|under_review|resubmission_required|closed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'Type of regulatory submission being filed. Identifies the specific regulatory report or notification category.']

  Note: 'Regulatory submission and reporting record tracking mandatory HSE reports submitted to government agencies. Table hse.process_safety_event {
  process_safety_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the process safety event record.']

  crude_grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.crude_grade. Business justification: PSE investigations for crude processing require grade-specific H2S, TAN, and vapor pressure data for root cause analysis, safety instrumented system design, and API RP 754 Tier 1/2 reporting.']
  drilling_well_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to drilling.drilling_well. Business justification: Well control events, blowouts, and H2S releases are process safety events tied to specific drilling wells. BSEE and API RP 754 reporting require PSE traceability to the operational well record for reg']
  emergency_response_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hse.emergency_response_plan. Linking process_safety_event to the emergency_response_plan that was activated enables ERP effectiveness evaluation, ']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment. Business justification: PSEs (loss of primary containment) involve specific equipment failures (vessel rupture, piping leak, relief valve lift). Equipment FK required for barrier analysis, bow-tie diagram updates, equipment ']
  finance_afe_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_afe. Business justification: PSE investigations and remediation on capital projects charged to AFE for cost tracking, insurance claims, and SEC materiality assessment.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: PSE involving fixed assets tracked for impairment assessment, asset retirement planning, and insurance claims.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: PSE costs posted to GL accounts for financial reporting, insurance claims, and process safety cost tracking.']
  petroleum_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.petroleum_product. Linking PSEs to violation records supports enforcement response, corrective action tracking, and regulatory rep']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: PSE on projects tracked to WBS for cost allocation, schedule impact analysis, and project risk management.']

  consequence_category varchar [note: 'Primary consequence category resulting from the process safety event, used for severity classification and trending.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this process safety event record was first created in the system.']
  discovery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the process safety event was first discovered or detected by personnel or monitoring systems.']
  environmental_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the process safety event resulted in environmental impact requiring regulatory notification or remediation.']
  equipment_involved varchar [note: 'Specific equipment, vessel, piping, or component involved in the process safety event (e.g., reactor, heat exchanger, pump, valve).']
  esg_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the process safety event should be included in ESG sustainability reporting and disclosures.']
  event_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the process safety event occurred.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the process safety event record in the investigation and reporting workflow.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the process safety event occurred, used for timeline analysis and SCADA correlation.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of process safety event as defined by API RP 754. LOPC = Loss of Primary Containment.']
  fatality_count varchar [note: 'Number of fatalities resulting from the process safety event.']
  ignition_source varchar [note: 'Identified or suspected ignition source for fire or explosion events (e.g., hot work, static electricity, electrical equipment, vehicle).']
  immediate_cause_description varchar [note: 'Brief description of the immediate or proximate cause of the process safety event, as determined during initial assessment.']
  injury_count varchar [note: 'Number of personnel injuries resulting from the process safety event.']
  investigation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the process safety event requires formal root cause investigation per company policy or regulatory requirement.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this process safety event record was last updated, used for audit trail and data quality monitoring.']
  lopc_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event involved a loss of primary containment as defined by API RP 754.']
  process_unit varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the process unit, plant section, or equipment train where the process safety event occurred (e.g., CDU, FCC, HDS, compressor station).']
  production_loss_boe decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated production loss resulting from the process safety event, expressed in barrels of oil equivalent.']
  property_damage_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual cost of property damage resulting from the process safety event, in US dollars.']
  pse_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the process safety event, used for external reporting and tracking.']
  pse_tier_classification varchar [note: 'API RP 754 tier classification of the process safety event. Tier 1 represents the most severe events with actual consequences; Tier 2 represents events with lesser consequences or near misses.']
  regulatory_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the process safety event meets regulatory thresholds requiring notification to OSHA, EPA, BSEE, or other authorities.']
  regulatory_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when regulatory authorities were notified of the process safety event.']
  release_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Duration of the material release in minutes, from initiation to isolation or cessation.']
  release_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Measured or estimated quantity of material released during the process safety event.']
  release_rate decimal(18,3) [note: 'Peak or average rate of material release during the event, used for consequence modeling.']
  release_rate_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the release rate (e.g., barrels per hour, pounds per minute, kilograms per second).']
  release_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the release quantity (BBL = Barrels, BOE = Barrel of Oil Equivalent, MSCF = Thousand Standard Cubic Feet, LBS = Pounds, KG = Kilograms). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BBL|BOE|MSCF|LBS|KG|GAL|LITERS — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  safety_system_demand_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the process safety event created a demand on a safety instrumented system or other safety barrier.']
  safety_system_involved varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the safety instrumented system (SIS), pressure relief system, or other safety barrier that was challenged or activated during the event.']
  safety_system_response varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the safety system responded as designed when challenged during the process safety event.']
  sec_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the process safety event meets materiality thresholds for SEC disclosure in financial filings.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Weather conditions at the time of the process safety event, relevant for dispersion modeling and consequence analysis.']
  wind_speed_mph decimal(5,2) [note: 'Wind speed in miles per hour at the time of the event, used for vapor cloud dispersion modeling.']

  Note: 'Process Safety Event (PSE) record capturing Tier 1 and Tier 2 process safety incidents as defined by API RP 754. Captures PSE tier classification, event type (loss of primary containment, fire, explosion, toxic release), process unit involved, material released, release quantity, consequence category, process demand on safety system, and whether safety system responded as designed. Supports API RP 754 process safety performance indicator benchmarking and SEC/ESG reporting.'
}

  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the emergency response plan expires and requires renewal or revision.']

  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the emergency response plan.']
  plan_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the emergency response plan document.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the emergency response plan based on the type of emergency scenario it addresses. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oil spill response plan|facility emergency response plan|well control emergency plan|offshore emergency evacuation plan|fire emergency response plan|H2S emergency response plan|blowout emergency plan|man overboard response plan|hurricane evacuation plan — promote to reference product]']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version number or identifier of the emergency response plan document.']
  regulatory_approval_number varchar [note: 'Reference number or approval identifier issued by the regulatory authority for the emergency response plan.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory authority to which the emergency response plan was submitted or that governs compliance.']
  regulatory_submission_date date [note: 'Date when the emergency response plan was submitted to regulatory authorities such as BSEE or EPA.']
  response_time_objective_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target time in hours for initial emergency response mobilization as defined in the plan.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent revision to the emergency response plan.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number tracking changes to the emergency response plan over time.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for the most recent revision, such as regulatory change, incident lessons learned, or facility modification.']
  worst_case_discharge_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum potential volume of oil or hazardous material that could be discharged in a worst-case scenario, used for oil spill response planning.']

  Note: 'Emergency response plan (ERP) master record and drill execution history for oil and gas facilities and operations. Captures plan definition: plan type (oil spill response plan, facility emergency response plan, well control emergency plan, offshore emergency evacuation plan), facility or operation covered, plan version, approval date, regulatory submission date (BSEE, EPA SPCC), and plan owner. Also captures drill and exercise records: drill type (fire drill, H2S evacuation, oil spill response, well control, man overboard, muster drill), drill date, participants count, scenario description, drill duration, performance evaluation results, deficiencies identified, and regulatory requirement fulfilled (BSEE, USCG, OSHA). Supports BSEE 30 CFR Part 254, EPA SPCC, OPA 90 emergency planning requirements, and emergency preparedness compliance verification.'
}

May differ from submission_timestamp if the authority processes submissions on a batch or business-day basis. Used to confirm the filing entered the authority\'s system of record.']

Serves as the authoritative proof of submission for audit and legal purposes.']
  confidentiality_treatment varchar [note: 'Indicates the confidentiality treatment status of this regulatory filing. Some filings contain commercially sensitive data (e.g., proprietary seismic data in BOEM permits, trade secret process information in EPA permits) for which the company may request confidential treatment from the regulatory authority.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory filing record was first created in the compliance data system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for audit trail purposes. Distinct from submission_timestamp which records the actual regulatory submission event.']
  document_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the regulatory filing document submitted (e.g., \'1.0\' for initial submission, \'2.0\' for amendment, \'3.0\' for second amendment). Supports document versioning and amendment tracking across the filing lifecycle.']
  due_date date [note: 'The regulatory deadline by which this filing must be submitted to the governing authority. Used for compliance calendar management, deadline monitoring, and on-time filing performance tracking. Compared against submission_timestamp to determine if the filing was timely.']
  enablon_submission_code varchar [note: 'The source system submission identifier from Enablon HSE Management platform, used for HSE-related regulatory filings (incident reports, permit to work submissions, environmental compliance filings, audit reports). Enables traceability back to the originating Enablon record for data lineage.']
  esg_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this regulatory filing contributes to or is part of the company\'s Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) reporting obligations. True for GHG emissions reports, environmental compliance filings, and sustainability disclosures. Supports ESG portfolio tracking and investor reporting.']
  filing_category varchar [note: 'High-level domain category of the regulatory filing, used for portfolio-level compliance tracking and reporting. Distinguishes between reserves disclosure (SEC), environmental compliance (EPA), offshore safety (BSEE), pipeline safety (PHMSA), tariff regulation (FERC), offshore permitting (BOEM), production quota (OPEC), and HSE reporting (OSHA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reserves_disclosure|environmental|offshore_safety|pipeline_safety|tariff|permit|quota|hse|financial|operational|land|joint_venture — promote to reference product]']
  filing_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the regulatory filing, including its purpose, scope, key data reported, and any special circumstances or notes relevant to compliance officers and auditors. Provides context beyond the structured classification fields.']
  filing_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary fee paid to the regulatory authority for processing this filing, where applicable (e.g., SEC registration fees, BOEM permit application fees, state commission filing fees). Expressed in the currency specified by filing_fee_currency. Zero or null for filings with no associated fee.']
  filing_fee_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the filing fee amount (e.g., \'USD\' for US dollar, \'GBP\' for British pound, \'EUR\' for euro). Required when filing_fee_amount is non-zero to support multi-currency international regulatory filings.']
  filing_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to this regulatory submission, as issued by the submitting entity or the regulatory authority (e.g., SEC accession number, BSEE incident report number, EPA facility ID submission reference, FERC docket number). Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this transaction.']
  filing_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the reporting period covered by this regulatory filing. For SEC 10-K reserves disclosures, this is the fiscal year-end date (typically December 31). For BSEE production reports, this is the month-end date. Used to validate that the filing covers the required reporting window.']
  filing_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the reporting period covered by this regulatory filing. For annual SEC reserves disclosures, this is the fiscal year start. For quarterly EPA reports, this is the quarter start date. Essential for compliance calendar management and period-over-period regulatory reporting.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the regulatory filing submission. Tracks progression from internal draft through submission to final regulatory authority disposition. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this transaction. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|acknowledged|accepted|rejected|withdrawn|under_review|superseded — promote to reference product if additional states are needed]']
  filing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory filing by its primary purpose and reporting category. Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this transaction. Examples include annual reserves disclosure (SEC 10-K/20-F), quarterly environmental report (EPA), offshore incident report (BSEE), pipeline tariff filing (FERC), safety report (PHMSA 49 CFR 191/195), offshore permit (BOEM), OPEC quota compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: annual|quarterly|monthly|incident|permit|tariff|quota|safety|emergency|amendment|withdrawal — promote to reference product]']
  ghg_emissions_scope varchar [note: 'For environmental and GHG-related regulatory filings, identifies the GHG Protocol emissions scope covered: Scope 1 (direct emissions from owned/controlled sources), Scope 2 (indirect emissions from purchased energy), Scope 3 (all other indirect emissions in the value chain). Null or \'not_applicable\' for non-environmental filings.']
  is_amendment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this filing is an amendment to a previously submitted regulatory filing. True if this is an amended submission (e.g., SEC 10-K/A, BSEE amended production report); False for original submissions. Used to distinguish original filings from corrective amendments in compliance reporting.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The geographic or political jurisdiction under which this regulatory filing is made (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'NGA\', \'SAU\', or a US state code such as \'TX\', \'LA\', \'AK\'). Supports multi-national and multi-state compliance portfolio management for an international oil and gas operator.']
  opec_quota_reference varchar [note: 'The OPEC production quota reference or allocation identifier applicable to this filing, used specifically for OPEC quota compliance reports submitted by NOC (National Oil Company) entities. Tracks the production quota period, allocated volume, and compliance status against OPEC ministerial decisions.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary penalty or fine assessed by the regulatory authority in connection with this filing or associated violation, expressed in the filing_fee_currency. Applicable for enforcement actions, late filings, or non-compliance findings. Zero or null for filings with no associated penalty.']
  psa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Production Sharing Agreement (PSA) or Joint Operating Agreement (JOA) under which this regulatory filing is made, applicable for international upstream filings where the company operates under a host government PSA. Links the filing to the relevant contractual framework.']
  regulatory_program_code varchar [note: 'The specific regulatory program, rule, or regulation code under which this filing is made (e.g., \'10-K\' for SEC annual report, \'20-F\' for foreign private issuer, \'Form 2\' for PHMSA gas distribution, \'Form 7000-1\' for BSEE production, \'SPCC\' for EPA spill prevention, \'Form 501-G\' for FERC). Enables precise regulatory program tracking.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'The reason provided by the regulatory authority for rejecting or returning this filing, where filing_status is \'rejected\'. Captures the authority\'s stated deficiency or non-compliance finding to support corrective action and resubmission. Null for accepted or pending filings.']
  reserves_category varchar [note: 'The SPE Petroleum Resources Management System (PRMS) reserves classification category applicable to this filing, used specifically for SEC reserves disclosure filings (10-K/20-F). Values: 1P (Proved), 2P (Proved plus Probable), 3P (Proved plus Probable plus Possible), PDP (Proved Developed Producing), PUD (Proved Undeveloped), PDNP (Proved Developed Non-Producing). Null for non-reserves filings.']
  resubmission_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which a corrected or amended filing must be resubmitted to the regulatory authority following a rejection or deficiency notice. Null for filings that have not been rejected. Used for compliance calendar management and escalation tracking.']
  sap_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA document number associated with this regulatory filing, particularly for filings that involve financial disclosures (SEC 10-K), cost recovery under joint venture agreements (JIB), or CAPEX/OPEX authorization (AFE). Enables cross-reference between the compliance record and the ERP financial system.']
  sox_control_reference varchar [note: 'The SOX internal control identifier or control framework reference associated with this regulatory filing, linking the filing to the company\'s SOX Section 302/404 internal control documentation. Supports SOX compliance audit trails for SEC-filed documents including 10-K reserves disclosures and financial statements.']
  submission_channel varchar [note: 'The method or channel through which the regulatory filing was submitted to the governing authority. Examples include electronic filing via SEC EDGAR, EPA CDX portal, BSEE TIMS, FERC eFiling, PHMSA portal, paper submission, or API-based automated submission.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the regulatory filing was officially submitted to the governing authority. This is the principal real-world business event timestamp (BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP) and is the legally binding submission time used for compliance deadline tracking and audit purposes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory filing record was last modified in the compliance data system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field for audit trail and data lineage purposes.']
  violation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this filing is associated with a regulatory violation, notice of violation (NOV), or enforcement action. True if the filing was triggered by or resulted in a violation finding. Supports violation tracking and enforcement action management within the compliance domain.']

  Note: 'Master record of all regulatory submissions made to governing bodies including SEC reserves disclosures (10-K/20-F proved reserves), EPA environmental filings, BSEE offshore safety reports, FERC pipeline tariff filings, PHMSA safety reports (49 CFR 191/195), BOEM offshore permits, OPEC quota compliance reports, and state commission filings. Tracks filing type, regulatory authority, submission date, filing period, status, official acknowledgment references, submission channel, and document versioning. Serves as the single audit trail for all outbound regulatory communications.'
}

Business justification: Permits (drilling, environmental, operating) are issued to specific legal entities tracked as accounts.   authorized_activity varchar [note: 'Specific operational activity or activities authorized under this permit (e.g., exploratory drilling, production operations, crude oil discharge, natural gas flaring, pipeline construction, water injection). Full conditions are maintained in the permit document repository.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the permitted activity is located (e.g., USA, GBR, NOR). Supports international permit portfolio management for multinational operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this permit record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements, SOX internal controls, and data lineage tracking in the compliance domain.']

  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the permit becomes legally effective and operations authorized by the permit may commence. This is the binding start date for compliance purposes and is distinct from the application date or approval date.']
  emission_limit_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the emission or discharge limit specified in the permit (e.g., tons per year, kg per day, lb per hour, MCFD, BOPD). Required for accurate compliance monitoring against permit thresholds.']
  emission_limit_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantitative emission limit authorized under the permit (e.g., tons per year of NOx, SO2, VOC, GHG). Applicable for EPA air emission permits (Title V, PSD, NSR). Used for environmental compliance monitoring and ESG reporting.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the permit expires and authorized operations must cease unless renewed. Drives the compliance calendar for renewal tracking and regulatory deadline management. Null for permits with no fixed expiration.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee paid to the issuing regulatory authority for the permit application, issuance, or renewal. Expressed in the bond currency. Used for CAPEX/OPEX tracking and regulatory cost management.']
  geographic_area varchar [note: 'Geographic area or region covered by the permit (e.g., OCS Gulf of Mexico, OCS Pacific, Permian Basin, Eagle Ford Shale, North Slope Alaska). Used for geographic compliance segmentation and BOEM/BSEE offshore area reporting.']
  hse_classification varchar [note: 'HSE risk classification of the permit based on the nature and severity of the authorized activity and associated environmental or safety risks. Critical permits (e.g., offshore drilling, H2S operations) require heightened monitoring and compliance controls per OSHA and EPA standards.']
  is_reportable_to_sec boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this permit or its status (e.g., revocation, material violation) must be disclosed in SEC filings under reserves disclosure or material event reporting requirements. Supports SOX compliance and SEC reporting obligations.']
  is_transferable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the permit can be transferred to another operator or entity (True) or is non-transferable (False). Relevant for merger, acquisition, and asset divestiture transactions where permit transfer approvals are required from the issuing authority.']
  issuing_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Jurisdiction under which the permit was issued, including federal, state, or local designation and the specific state or offshore region (e.g., Federal-OCS Gulf of Mexico, Texas, Louisiana, Alaska). Supports multi-jurisdictional compliance management.']
  jurisdiction_level varchar [note: 'Level of government jurisdiction under which the permit was granted. Distinguishes between federal (BOEM, BSEE, EPA, PHMSA, FERC), state, local, tribal, or international regulatory authority for compliance segmentation and reporting.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory inspection or audit conducted by the issuing authority related to this permit. Used to track inspection frequency, compliance history, and preparation for upcoming inspections.']
  lease_number varchar [note: 'Lease or land parcel number associated with the permitted activity. Links the permit to the land and lease management system (Quorum Land System) for integrated land, lease, and compliance tracking. Applicable for onshore and offshore lease-based permits.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the next scheduled regulatory inspection or compliance audit for this permit. Drives compliance calendar planning and preparation activities. May be set by the regulatory authority or estimated based on inspection frequency requirements.']
  permit_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the activities, operations, or discharges authorized by the permit. Captures the scope of authorization including geographic boundaries, operational limits, and specific activities covered.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Official permit number assigned by the issuing regulatory authority (e.g., BOEM permit number, EPA permit number, BSEE well permit number). This is the externally-known identifier used in all regulatory correspondence and submissions.']
  permit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the permit. Tracks the permit from initial application through approval, active operations, suspension, expiration, or revocation. Critical for compliance calendar management and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: applied|approved|active|suspended|expired|revoked|withdrawn — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  permit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the permit by regulatory category. Covers BOEM offshore drilling permits, EPA air and water discharge permits, BSEE well permits, state environmental permits, and pipeline right-of-way permits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: drilling|environmental|pipeline_row|air_emission|water_discharge|offshore_operations|land_use|other — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person at the issuing regulatory authority responsible for this permit. Used for regulatory correspondence, permit inquiries, and compliance communications.']
  regulatory_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact person at the issuing regulatory authority. Used for direct regulatory communications and permit-related inquiries.']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the permit renewal application must be submitted to the issuing authority to maintain continuous authorization. Typically set ahead of the expiration date per regulatory requirements. Drives compliance calendar alerts and renewal workflow initiation.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the permit renewal process. Tracks whether a renewal is not required (e.g., one-time permits), pending initiation, submitted to the authority, approved, or denied. Supports proactive compliance management and regulatory deadline tracking.']
  responsible_business_unit varchar [note: 'Name of the internal business unit, division, or operating company responsible for maintaining compliance with this permit (e.g., Gulf of Mexico Offshore Operations, Permian Basin E&P, Refining and Marketing). Used for organizational accountability and compliance reporting.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'Date on which the permit was formally revoked by the issuing regulatory authority. Populated only when permit_status is \'revoked\'. Critical for violation tracking, regulatory reporting, and HSE compliance records.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code for the state in which the permitted activity is located (e.g., TX, LA, AK, CA). Used for state-level regulatory compliance tracking and multi-state permit portfolio management. Null for federal offshore permits.']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Further classification within the permit type (e.g., for drilling: exploratory, development, workover; for environmental: NPDES, Title V air, stormwater; for pipeline: federal ROW, state ROW). Provides granular categorization for compliance tracking and reporting.']
  suspension_date date [note: 'Date on which the permit was suspended by the issuing authority or voluntarily by the company. Populated only when permit_status is \'suspended\'. Used for compliance incident tracking and regulatory reporting.']
  title varchar [note: 'Official descriptive title or name of the permit as assigned by the issuing authority (e.g., \'Application for Permit to Drill\', \'Title V Operating Permit\', \'NPDES General Permit for Stormwater Discharges\'). Provides human-readable identification for the permit register.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this permit record was most recently updated. Supports audit trail requirements, change tracking, and SOX internal controls for the compliance domain.']
  violation_count varchar [note: 'Total number of regulatory violations or notices of non-compliance recorded against this permit since issuance. Used for compliance risk scoring, regulatory relationship management, and ESG reporting. Incremented when violations are logged in the violation tracking system.']

  Note: 'Master register of all permits held or applied for by the company including BOEM offshore drilling permits, EPA air and water discharge permits, BSEE well permits, state environmental permits, and pipeline right-of-way permits. Tracks permit number, issuing authority, permit type, facility or asset covered, effective date, expiration date, renewal status, and associated conditions.'
}
