Defines the operational scope boundary.']
  commodity_type varchar [note: 'Primary hydrocarbon commodity or product category covered by the operating license. Distinguishes licenses for crude oil (BOPD), natural gas (MCFD/MMCFD), NGL (Natural Gas Liquids), LPG (Liquefied Petroleum Gas), LNG (Liquefied Natural Gas), refined products, or petrochemicals. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: crude_oil|natural_gas|ngl|lpg|lng|refined_products|petrochemicals|multiple — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  conditions_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of key conditions, stipulations, and special requirements attached to the operating license by the issuing authority (e.g., environmental mitigation measures, production rate limits, HSE reporting obligations, decommissioning bonds). Supports compliance obligation management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this operating license record was first created in the enterprise data platform. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED for this MASTER_AGREEMENT. Supports SOX audit trail and data lineage requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this license record (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, SAR). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-national operations.']
  decommissioning_obligation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this operating license carries a decommissioning obligation requiring the licensee to plug and abandon (P&A) wells and remove facilities upon license expiry or cessation of production. Triggers asset retirement obligation (ARO) accounting under IFRS/GAAP.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the operating license becomes legally binding and operations may commence under its authority. EFFECTIVE_FROM for this MASTER_AGREEMENT. Aligns with the commencement date recorded in the licensing authority\'s register.']
  environmental_bond_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of the environmental surety bond or financial assurance required by the issuing authority as a condition of the operating license. Covers potential decommissioning, well P&A (Plug and Abandonment), and environmental remediation costs.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the operating license ceases to be valid unless renewed or extended. EFFECTIVE_UNTIL for this MASTER_AGREEMENT. Nullable for perpetual or open-ended licenses. Critical for compliance calendar management and renewal obligation tracking.']
  ghg_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the operating license requires mandatory Greenhouse Gas (GHG) emissions reporting to the EPA or other environmental authority under the license conditions. Supports ESG reporting and EPA Mandatory Reporting Rule compliance.']
  hse_classification varchar [note: 'Health Safety and Environment (HSE) risk classification assigned to the operating license based on the nature and scale of authorized operations. High-risk licenses (e.g., deepwater drilling, H2S-bearing formations, NORM-handling) require enhanced HSE compliance monitoring in Enablon.']
  joa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the Joint Operating Agreement (JOA) associated with this operating license when the license is held jointly with co-venturers. Links to the governing JOA document for joint interest billing and partner obligations under SAP Joint Venture Accounting.']
  jurisdiction_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction under which the license is issued and enforced (e.g., USA, GBR, NOR, SAU). Supports multi-national regulatory compliance management.']
  jurisdiction_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or sub-national jurisdiction in which the license is issued and operative (e.g., Texas, Louisiana, Alberta, North Sea OCS). Required for state-level regulatory compliance tracking and reporting to state oil and gas commissions.']
  last_renewal_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent renewal of this operating license was granted by the issuing authority. Establishes the current license term start for renewal cycle tracking.']
  ldar_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the operating license mandates a Leak Detection and Repair (LDAR) program as a condition of authorization. LDAR programs are required under EPA regulations for facilities emitting volatile organic compounds (VOCs) and hazardous air pollutants.']
  license_category varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the license by the level of government authority that issued it. Federal licenses are issued by agencies such as BSEE, BOEM, FERC, or EPA. State licenses are issued by state oil and gas commissions. International licenses are issued by foreign national authorities or NOCs.']
  license_class varchar [note: 'Operational environment classification of the license. Onshore covers land-based E&P operations. Offshore covers continental shelf operations. Deepwater and ultra-deepwater apply to BSEE/BOEM regulated OCS operations. Pipeline covers FERC/PHMSA regulated transportation. Facility covers refineries and processing plants.']
  license_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name or title of the operating license as assigned by the licensing authority or internally designated (e.g., \'Gulf of Mexico OCS Lease G-12345\', \'Texas Railroad Commission Operator Permit\'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this agreement.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, officially-assigned license or authorization number issued by the licensing authority (e.g., BSEE lease number, EPA permit ID, FERC certificate number, BOEM OCS lease number). This is the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER used in all regulatory correspondence and submissions.']
  license_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the operating license. LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_AGREEMENT. Active indicates the license is in force; suspended indicates temporary halt by authority; expired indicates past effective-until date without renewal; pending indicates awaiting authority approval; revoked indicates permanently cancelled; under_renewal indicates renewal application submitted.']
  license_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the operating license by the category of operations it authorizes. CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this MASTER_AGREEMENT. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: exploration_license|production_license|pipeline_operating_certificate|refinery_operating_license|lng_facility_license|environmental_permit|drilling_permit|storage_license|terminal_operating_license|ngl_processing_license — promote to reference product]']
  licensed_area_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the geographic area, block, tract, or facility covered by the operating license (e.g., OCS Block designation, lease tract description, pipeline corridor, refinery site address). Defines the spatial scope of authorized operations.']
  net_revenue_interest_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Net Revenue Interest (NRI) percentage representing the company\'s share of production revenue after deducting royalties and overriding royalty interests (ORRI). Expressed as a decimal percentage. Used for revenue allocation and SEC reserves disclosure.']
  production_capacity_boe decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum authorized production or throughput capacity expressed in Barrels of Oil Equivalent (BOE) per day as stipulated in the license conditions. Represents the principal quantitative constraint of the license. Used for OPEC quota compliance and SEC reserves disclosure.']
  psa_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this operating license is governed by a Production Sharing Agreement (PSA) with a host government or National Oil Company (NOC). When True, PSA fiscal terms and cost recovery provisions apply to production accounting and revenue recognition.']
  regulatory_reference_number varchar [note: 'Additional regulatory docket, case, or reference number assigned by the issuing authority for tracking the license application and correspondence (e.g., FERC docket number, EPA permit tracking number, BSEE incident of non-compliance number). Supplements the primary license_number.']
  renewal_application_date date [note: 'Date on which the renewal application was submitted to the issuing authority. Used to confirm timely submission relative to the renewal lead time requirement and to track the renewal process lifecycle.']
  renewal_lead_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days prior to the expiry date by which the renewal application must be submitted to the issuing authority to maintain continuous operational authorization. Drives compliance calendar alerts and workflow triggers in Enablon HSE Management.']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this operating license requires periodic renewal before the expiry date. When True, renewal obligations must be tracked in the compliance calendar and renewal applications submitted to the issuing authority within the required lead time.']
  royalty_rate_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Royalty rate percentage payable to the mineral rights owner or government authority as a condition of the operating license. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 12.5000 for 12.5%). Impacts LOE (Lease Operating Expense) and net revenue calculations.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this license record originates (e.g., Enablon HSE Management permit register, Quorum Land System lease record, SAP S/4HANA compliance module). Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.']
  suspension_date date [note: 'Date on which the operating license was suspended by the issuing authority. Populated only when license_status is \'suspended\'. Triggers operational halt and compliance remediation workflow.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Textual description of the reason for license suspension as communicated by the issuing authority. Documents the compliance violation, safety incident, or administrative cause triggering the suspension. Supports HSE incident tracking in Enablon.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this operating license record was most recently modified in the enterprise data platform. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and SOX internal controls. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  working_interest_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Company\'s Working Interest (WI) percentage in the licensed area, representing the proportionate share of costs and production entitlement. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 65.0000 for 65%). Critical for Joint Interest Billing (JIB) and COPAS accounting.']

  Note: 'Master record of all operating licenses and authorizations required to conduct oil and gas operations including exploration licenses, production licenses, refinery operating licenses, pipeline operating certificates, and LNG facility licenses. Tracks license number, licensing authority, jurisdiction, scope of operations authorized, conditions, and renewal obligations.'
}

Table compliance.obligation {
  obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each regulatory compliance obligation record in the master catalog.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.contract. Business justification: Compliance obligations frequently arise from contractual commitments in PSAs, JOAs, and service agreements (e.g., HSE reporting, local content, decommissioning). Linking obligation to contract enables']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Compliance obligations have estimated_compliance_cost tracked by cost center for budget planning and variance analysis. Reusing existing sap_cost_center column (unlinked) would be ideal, but it\'s a pl']
  petroleum_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.petroleum_product. Business justification: Product-specific compliance obligations include RFS2 renewable fuel mandates, state fuel specification requirements (CARB diesel, RFG programs), product registration renewals, and fuel quality monitor']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Compliance obligations are tied to specific operating segments/profit centers for management accountability, segment reporting, and estimated compliance cost allocation. Essential for segment P&L fore']
  regulatory_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_authority. Business justification: A compliance obligation is mandated by a specific regulatory authority (e.g., EPA, BSEE, FERC, PHMSA). The obligation table has regulatory_body as a STRING which is denormalized. Adding regulatory_aut']
  advance_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before the due date that an internal compliance alert or reminder should be triggered to the obligation owner and responsible department. Supports proactive compliance management.']
  compliance_frequency varchar [note: 'Recurrence frequency at which the compliance action must be performed: one_time, daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, semi_annual, annual, biennial, triennial, or event_driven (triggered by a specific operational event). Drives the compliance calendar. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: one_time|daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|semi_annual|annual|biennial|triennial|event_driven — promote to reference product]']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance lifecycle state of the obligation: compliant (action completed on time), non_compliant (action missed or deficient), pending (action in progress), overdue (past due date without completion), waived (formally exempted by regulator), or under_review (regulator or internal review in progress).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country in which this obligation applies (e.g., USA, GBR, NOR, SAU). Supports international operations and multi-country compliance tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this obligation record was first created in the compliance master catalog. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  data_domain varchar [note: 'Primary data domain to which this obligation belongs, aligning with the enterprise data governance taxonomy. Supports domain-based compliance reporting and ownership assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: exploration|drilling|production|reservoir|refining|petrochemicals|supply_chain|marketing_sales|land_lease|joint_venture|hse|workforce|finance|asset_management|contracts_procurement|regulatory_compliance|transportation_logistics — promote to reference product]']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date on which this regulatory obligation became or becomes binding on the company. Marks the start of the compliance obligation period.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'Date on which this regulatory obligation expires or is no longer binding. Null for open-ended or perpetual obligations. Supports permit expiry tracking and renewal workflows.']
  enablon_obligation_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of this obligation record in the Enablon HSE Management system, which is the operational system of record for HSE compliance, permit-to-work, and environmental compliance tracking.']
  esg_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this obligation contributes to Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) reporting disclosures (True), including GHG emissions, LDAR programs, NORM management, or social compliance.']
  estimated_compliance_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated annual OPEX cost in the penalty currency to fulfill this obligation, including labor, monitoring, reporting, and third-party services. Used for compliance budget planning and CAPEX/OPEX allocation.']
  ghg_emission_scope varchar [note: 'For GHG and environmental reporting obligations, the GHG Protocol emission scope: scope_1 (direct emissions from owned/controlled sources), scope_2 (indirect from purchased energy), scope_3 (value chain indirect), or not_applicable for non-GHG obligations.']
  is_material boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this obligation is considered material for SEC financial disclosure, SOX internal controls, or ESG reporting purposes (True) or is non-material (False).']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this obligation recurs on a defined schedule (True) or is a one-time requirement (False). Drives automated compliance calendar generation.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic or legal jurisdiction in which this obligation applies (e.g., \'Federal\', \'Texas\', \'Gulf of Mexico OCS\', \'Alaska North Slope\', \'International\'). Supports multi-jurisdictional compliance management.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent internal or external audit that reviewed compliance with this obligation. Supports audit trail requirements and SOX 404 documentation.']
  last_audit_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent audit of this obligation: satisfactory (fully compliant), minor_finding (low-risk gap), major_finding (significant gap requiring corrective action), critical_finding (immediate regulatory risk), or not_audited.']
  last_completed_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent compliance action for this obligation was completed and submitted. Used to calculate compliance history and next due date for recurring obligations.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this obligation record was most recently modified in the compliance master catalog. Supports change management, audit trail, and SOX internal controls documentation.']
  max_penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum statutory monetary penalty per violation or per day of non-compliance as defined by the applicable regulation (in USD). Used for risk quantification and CAPEX/OPEX planning for compliance investments.']
  next_due_date date [note: 'The next calendar date by which the compliance action must be completed or submitted to the regulatory authority. Drives the compliance calendar and alert notifications.']
  obligation_category varchar [note: 'High-level business category grouping the obligation: environmental (EPA/GHG), safety (OSHA/BSEE/HSE), financial (SEC/SOX/COPAS), operational (FERC/PHMSA), land_lease (BOEM/Quorum), export_import, labor, tax, or esg (ESG reporting). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: environmental|safety|financial|operational|land_lease|export_import|labor|tax|esg — promote to reference product]']
  obligation_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the obligation across regulatory submissions, permit registers, and compliance calendars (e.g., EPA-00012345, BSEE-20240001).']
  obligation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the full scope of the regulatory compliance obligation, including what action must be taken, by whom, and under what conditions.']
  obligation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the obligation by its nature: reporting (e.g., SEC reserves disclosure), permit (e.g., BOEM offshore permit), license (e.g., FERC gas pipeline license), inspection (e.g., BSEE facility inspection), certification (e.g., OSHA safety certification), consent_order, tariff_filing, audit, training, or monitoring. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reporting|permit|license|inspection|certification|consent_order|tariff_filing|audit|training|monitoring — promote to reference product]']
  opec_quota_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this obligation is related to OPEC production quota compliance (True), requiring production volume monitoring and reporting to the national oil company or government authority.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the maximum penalty amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency international operations.']
  penalty_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk level of non-compliance based on potential regulatory penalties, operational impact, and reputational consequences: critical (facility shutdown, criminal liability), high (major fines, license revocation), medium (civil penalties, corrective action orders), or low (minor administrative penalties).']
  prms_reserves_category varchar [note: 'For SEC reserves disclosure obligations, the Petroleum Resources Management System (PRMS) reserves category to which this obligation applies: 1P (Proved), 2P (Proved plus Probable), 3P (Proved plus Probable plus Possible), PDP (Proved Developed Producing), PUD (Proved Undeveloped), or PDNP (Proved Developed Non-Producing). Null for non-reserves obligations.']
  regulation_citation varchar [note: 'Specific legal or regulatory citation that creates this obligation (e.g., \'40 CFR Part 98 Subpart W\', \'30 CFR Part 250.1920\', \'49 CFR Part 195.452\', \'SEC Rule 4-10(a)\'). Used for audit trail and legal defensibility.']
  reporting_form_number varchar [note: 'Official form number or submission template identifier required by the regulatory authority for this obligation (e.g., \'EPA Form 7540-1\', \'BSEE Form MMS-133\', \'SEC Form 10-K\', \'FERC Form 2\'). Supports submission workflow automation.']
  responsible_business_unit varchar [note: 'Name of the internal business unit or division accountable for fulfilling this obligation (e.g., \'Upstream E&P\', \'Refining Operations\', \'HSE Compliance\', \'Finance and Reporting\', \'Supply Chain\'). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA organizational hierarchy.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Specific department within the responsible business unit that owns day-to-day compliance actions for this obligation (e.g., \'Environmental Affairs\', \'Drilling Engineering\', \'Regulatory Affairs\', \'Legal\').']
  source_instrument_number varchar [note: 'Official number or identifier of the permit, license, consent order, or regulation that is the source of this obligation (e.g., permit number, docket number, consent decree case number). Links to the permit register.']
  source_instrument_type varchar [note: 'Type of legal instrument from which this obligation is derived: federal_regulation, state_regulation, permit, license, consent_order, settlement_agreement, executive_order, or tariff. Determines the legal authority and enforcement mechanism. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: federal_regulation|state_regulation|permit|license|consent_order|settlement_agreement|executive_order|tariff — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sox_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this obligation is linked to a Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) internal control (True) and must be included in the annual SOX 302/404 certification process.']
  submission_deadline_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days after the trigger event or period end by which the compliance submission must be filed with the regulatory authority (e.g., 30 days after fiscal year end for SEC annual reserves disclosure).']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'Required method for submitting the compliance deliverable to the regulatory authority: electronic (e.g., EPA CDX, BSEE TIMS), paper, online_portal, email, or certified_mail.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short, human-readable title of the regulatory obligation as referenced in the permit, regulation, or consent order (e.g., \'Annual GHG Emissions Report\', \'BSEE SEMS Audit\', \'PHMSA Pipeline Integrity Report\').']
  violation_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of documented regulatory violations or notices of non-compliance issued against this obligation. Supports violation tracking, penalty risk assessment, and regulatory relationship management.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all regulatory compliance obligations applicable to the company derived from federal and state regulations, permits, licenses, consent orders, and settlement agreements. Each obligation defines the regulatory requirement, applicable regulation citation, responsible business unit, frequency of compliance action, due date logic, consequence of non-compliance, and current compliance status. Serves as the authoritative register of what the company must do to remain compliant and the central hub linking permits, filings, violations, and calendar events.'
}

Table compliance.calendar {
  calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each scheduled compliance calendar event. Primary key for the compliance calendar data product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Compliance calendar events require cost center assignment for departmental accountability, budget tracking, and resource allocation planning. Real business process: compliance workload planning and de']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent regulatory compliance obligation that generated this calendar event. Links the scheduled action back to its governing obligation record.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the permit or license record associated with this calendar event, applicable for permit renewal deadlines, inspection due dates, and certification expirations.']
  petroleum_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.petroleum_product. Business justification: Compliance calendars track product-specific deadlines: quarterly fuel quality reports by product grade, annual EPA product registrations, state fuel specification certifications, and seasonal fuel tra']
  regulatory_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_authority. Business justification: A compliance calendar event is associated with a specific regulatory authority (e.g., EPA filing deadline, FERC tariff submission, BSEE inspection schedule). The calendar table has regulatory_authorit']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'Name of the internal business unit or operating division responsible for fulfilling this compliance obligation (e.g., Upstream E&P, Refining, Petrochemicals, Midstream, Corporate). Supports organizational reporting and accountability assignment.']
  certification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance event requires an officer-level certification or attestation (e.g., SOX Section 302/404 CEO/CFO certification, SEC reserves certification by qualified petroleum engineer, BSEE SEMS audit certification).']
  certifying_officer varchar [note: 'Name or title of the officer or qualified professional responsible for certifying or attesting to the accuracy of the regulatory submission (e.g., CEO, CFO, Qualified Petroleum Engineer for SEC reserves). Applicable when certification_required is True.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the compliance action was completed, filed, or submitted. Null if not yet completed. Used to calculate on-time performance and support audit trail requirements.']
  compliance_domain varchar [note: 'High-level business domain classification for this compliance event, enabling domain-based filtering and reporting across the compliance calendar. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: environmental|safety|financial|reserves|pipeline|offshore|export_control|labor|tax|hse|esg — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance calendar event record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX internal controls and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  days_overdue varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days by which this compliance event has exceeded its regulatory due date without completion. Populated only when overdue_flag is True. Used for regulatory risk scoring and enforcement exposure assessment.']
  due_date date [note: 'The hard regulatory deadline by which the compliance action must be completed or submitted to the regulatory authority. May differ from scheduled_date when internal preparation time is factored in.']
  escalation_contact varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the manager or senior officer to be notified when this compliance event becomes overdue or at risk. Supports the escalation threshold workflow for overdue items.']
  escalation_threshold_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before the due date at which an escalation notification is triggered if the compliance event has not been completed. Enables proactive overdue management and prevents regulatory violations.']
  esg_category varchar [note: 'ESG classification for this compliance event, indicating whether it relates to environmental (GHG emissions, LDAR, spill prevention), social (OSHA worker safety, SIMOPS), or governance (SOX, SEC disclosure, COPAS) obligations. Supports ESG reporting frameworks.']
  event_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to this compliance calendar event, used in regulatory submissions and internal tracking systems such as Enablon HSE Management.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the compliance calendar event, tracking progression from scheduled through completion or escalation. Drives compliance dashboard reporting and overdue escalation workflows.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the compliance calendar event indicating the nature of the required action. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: filing_deadline|permit_renewal|inspection_due|certification_expiration|reporting_window|audit_scheduled|quota_submission|tariff_filing — promote to reference product]']
  extended_due_date date [note: 'Revised regulatory deadline date granted by the regulatory authority when an extension has been approved. Null when extension_granted is False. Supersedes due_date for compliance tracking purposes when populated.']
  extension_granted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the regulatory authority has granted a formal extension of the filing or compliance deadline for this event. When True, the extended_due_date field captures the revised deadline.']
  form_type varchar [note: 'Official name or code of the regulatory form, report, or filing required for this compliance event (e.g., SEC Form 10-K, SEC Form 10-Q, EPA Form GHG, BSEE Form MMS-133, FERC Form 2, PHMSA Form 7100.1-1).']
  ghg_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance event is related to greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions reporting, monitoring, or reduction obligations under EPA 40 CFR Part 98 or equivalent international regulations. Supports ESG and climate disclosure tracking.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic or legal jurisdiction governing this compliance obligation, expressed as a 2-3 character ISO country or US state code (e.g., US, UK, NG, SA, TX, LA). Determines applicable regulatory framework and submission authority.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance officers to record contextual information, regulatory correspondence references, internal review comments, or special instructions related to this compliance calendar event.']
  overdue_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance calendar event has passed its due date without completion. True when current date exceeds due_date and event_status is not completed, waived, or cancelled. Drives compliance risk dashboards.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Name of the department or functional group to which the assigned compliance owner belongs (e.g., HSE, Legal, Finance, Regulatory Affairs, Operations). Supports departmental compliance reporting.']
  penalty_exposure_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated maximum financial penalty in US dollars that could be assessed by the regulatory authority for non-compliance or late filing of this obligation. Used for compliance risk prioritization and CAPEX/OPEX planning.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business-assigned priority level for this compliance event, reflecting regulatory risk, penalty exposure, and operational impact. Critical items include SEC reserves disclosures, BSEE offshore safety reports, and EPA major source permits.']
  recurrence_end_date date [note: 'Date after which no further recurring compliance calendar events will be generated for this obligation. Null for open-ended recurring obligations. Used by the compliance calendar scheduling engine.']
  recurrence_pattern varchar [note: 'Frequency pattern defining how often this compliance obligation recurs and generates new calendar events (e.g., annual SEC 10-K filing, quarterly EPA emissions reports, monthly BSEE production reports, one-time permit application).']
  regulatory_program varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory program or rule set under which this compliance event is required (e.g., SEC Reserves Disclosure, EPA GHG Reporting, BSEE SEMS, PHMSA Integrity Management, FERC Gas Tariff, OPEC Quota Compliance, SOX Section 302/404).']
  reporting_period_end date [note: 'End date of the regulatory reporting period covered by this compliance event. Together with reporting_period_start, defines the data window for which the regulatory submission or action applies.']
  reporting_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the regulatory reporting period covered by this compliance event (e.g., beginning of the fiscal quarter for SEC filings, start of the calendar year for EPA GHG reports, beginning of the OPEC quota period).']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The principal calendar date on which this compliance action is scheduled to occur or by which it must be completed. Serves as the primary deadline date for compliance tracking and escalation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this compliance calendar event was originated or ingested (e.g., Enablon HSE Management for HSE compliance events, SAP S/4HANA for financial regulatory filings, Quorum for land and lease permit renewals).']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the regulatory filing or compliance action is submitted to the regulatory authority (e.g., electronic via SEC EDGAR, EPA CDX, BSEE eWell, FERC eFiling, or paper submission).']
  submission_reference varchar [note: 'Confirmation number, accession number, or tracking reference assigned by the regulatory authority upon receipt of the submission (e.g., SEC EDGAR accession number, EPA CDX submission ID, BSEE confirmation number).']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the regulatory filing, report, or submission was transmitted to the regulatory authority. Provides exact proof of timely submission for audit and enforcement purposes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this compliance calendar event record. Tracks changes to scheduled dates, status updates, owner reassignments, and extension grants for audit trail purposes.']

  Note: 'Scheduled compliance events and regulatory deadlines derived from compliance obligations. Tracks each scheduled compliance action including filing deadlines, permit renewal dates, inspection due dates, certification expirations, and reporting windows. Includes assigned owner, regulatory authority, associated obligation, scheduled date, completion date, and escalation thresholds for overdue items.'
}

Table compliance.violation {
  violation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each regulatory violation, notice of violation (NOV), or non-compliance finding record. Primary key for the violation data product.']
  asset_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility, asset, or operational site where the violation occurred or was identified (e.g., refinery, offshore platform, pipeline segment, well pad).']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific physical asset (equipment, pipeline, well, vessel) directly associated with the violation, as tracked in Maximo Asset Management.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Violations require cost center allocation for departmental accountability, budget impact tracking, and corrective action cost management beyond GL account. Essential for management reporting and depar']
  equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment. Business justification: Violations are issued against specific equipment (e.g., PRV failure NOV, compressor emission violation, pressure vessel overpressure). violation links to asset_facility but not equipment; equipment-le']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Violations trigger penalty accruals and reserves requiring GL account mapping for financial statement preparation, SEC disclosure, and SOX controls. Essential for posting penalty_amount to proper liab']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: A regulatory violation is a breach of a specific compliance obligation. Linking violation to obligation enables traceability from the violation back to the underlying regulatory requirement that was b']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: A violation is often tied to a specific permit condition (e.g., exceeding emission limits on an air permit, violating BOEM drilling permit conditions). The violation table has permit_number as a STRIN']
  petroleum_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.petroleum_product. Business justification: Violations frequently cite specific petroleum products: off-specification fuel sales, unauthorized product marketing, mislabeled products, or contaminated product releases. Product identification is e']
  pipeline_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.pipeline_segment. Business justification: PHMSA NOVs and state pipeline safety violations are issued against specific pipeline segments. violation links to asset_facility but not pipeline_segment; segment-level violation tracking is required ']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Violations impact specific operating segments and must be allocated to profit centers for segment performance measurement, P&L impact analysis, and management accountability. Essential for accurate se']
  regulatory_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_authority. Business justification: A violation is issued by a specific regulatory authority (e.g., EPA issues NOVs, BSEE issues offshore violations). The violation table has issuing_authority as a STRING which is denormalized. Adding r']
  well_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.well_asset. Business justification: Violations (e.g., well integrity failures, H2S releases, spills) are issued against specific wells. violation links to asset_facility and asset but not well_asset; well-level violation tracking is req']
  appeal_filed_date date [note: 'Date the formal appeal was filed with the regulatory authority or administrative review body. Null if no appeal has been filed. Used to track appeal deadlines and statute of limitations.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Status of any formal appeal filed against the violation finding or assessed penalty: not_appealed, appeal_filed (submitted to authority), appeal_pending (under review), appeal_granted (violation or penalty reduced/dismissed), appeal_denied, or appeal_withdrawn.']
  capa_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date the CAPA plan was fully implemented and all corrective actions were completed. Null if CAPA is still in progress. Compared against capa_target_date to assess timeliness of corrective response.']
  capa_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) plan developed to address the root cause of the violation and prevent recurrence. Includes specific corrective steps, process changes, and preventive measures.']
  capa_effectiveness_review varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the implemented CAPA has been effective in preventing recurrence of the violation: not_started, in_progress (review underway), effective (no recurrence observed), partially_effective (improvement noted but not fully resolved), or ineffective (recurrence observed, additional action required).']
  capa_responsible_party varchar [note: 'Name or role of the individual or organizational unit accountable for implementing the CAPA plan (e.g., Operations Manager, HSE Director, Facility Superintendent). Supports accountability tracking and SOX control ownership.']
  capa_target_date date [note: 'Planned completion date for full implementation of the CAPA plan. Used to monitor corrective action progress against regulatory response deadlines and internal compliance milestones.']
  capa_verification_method varchar [note: 'Description of the method used to verify that the CAPA has been effectively implemented (e.g., regulatory inspection, internal audit, third-party verification, monitoring data review, management walkthrough). Required for regulatory closure documentation.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date the violation was formally closed by the regulatory authority or verified as resolved through internal compliance review. Null if the violation remains open. Used for regulatory closure documentation and ESG reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the violation record was first created in the compliance management system (Enablon HSE Management or SAP S/4HANA). Supports audit trail, SOX internal controls, and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  discovery_date date [note: 'Date the violation was first identified, whether through regulatory inspection, internal audit, self-disclosure, or monitoring system alert. Distinct from the date the NOV was formally issued. Critical for calculating response timelines and regulatory reporting deadlines.']
  discovery_source varchar [note: 'Source through which the violation was identified: regulatory_inspection (government inspector), internal_audit (company HSE or compliance audit), self_disclosure (voluntary company disclosure to regulator), monitoring_system (SCADA/PI System automated alert), third_party_audit (independent auditor), or employee_report (internal report or near-miss notification).']
  end_date date [note: 'Date the non-compliant condition or activity ceased, either through corrective action or regulatory determination. Null if the violation is ongoing. Used with violation_start_date to calculate total duration of non-compliance.']
  ghg_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the violation is related to Greenhouse Gas (GHG) emissions reporting, monitoring, or control requirements. True if GHG-related; False otherwise. Supports ESG reporting, EPA GHG Reporting Program compliance, and carbon disclosure obligations.']
  internal_reference_number varchar [note: 'Company-assigned internal tracking number for the violation record, used for cross-referencing with SAP S/4HANA compliance modules and Enablon HSE Management case management.']
  issuing_authority_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the issuing authority jurisdiction: federal (EPA, BSEE, OSHA, PHMSA, FERC, BOEM, SEC), state (state environmental or safety agency), local (municipal authority), international (OPEC, foreign regulator), or internal_audit (company internal compliance or HSE audit finding).']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic or regulatory jurisdiction in which the violation occurred and is being adjudicated (e.g., US state code such as TX, LA, AK, or country code such as USA, GBR, NGA). Supports multi-jurisdictional compliance reporting and OPEC quota compliance tracking.']
  nov_issue_date date [note: 'Date the Notice of Violation (NOV) or formal non-compliance finding was officially issued by the regulatory authority or internal audit function. Used to calculate regulatory response deadlines and statute of limitations.']
  nov_number varchar [note: 'Externally assigned Notice of Violation (NOV) number or citation reference issued by the regulatory authority. Serves as the official government-assigned identifier for the violation. Used for regulatory correspondence and closure documentation.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary penalty assessed by the regulatory authority for the violation, in the currency specified by penalty_currency. Null if no monetary penalty has been assessed. Used for financial exposure tracking, CAPEX/OPEX impact analysis, and SEC disclosure assessment.']
  penalty_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the assessed penalty amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations for international regulatory jurisdictions.']
  penalty_paid_date date [note: 'Date the monetary penalty was paid in full or the final installment was remitted to the regulatory authority. Null if payment has not been completed. Used for financial reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA FI module.']
  penalty_payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status of the assessed monetary penalty: not_assessed (no penalty levied), assessed_unpaid (penalty issued but not yet paid), partially_paid, paid_in_full, waived (penalty formally waived by authority), or under_appeal (payment suspended pending appeal outcome).']
  regulation_cited varchar [note: 'Specific regulation, rule, statute, or standard cited in the violation (e.g., 40 CFR Part 60 Subpart OOOO, 30 CFR Part 250, 29 CFR 1910.119, 49 CFR Part 195, FERC Order 636, SOX Section 302/404). Enables regulatory citation tracking and trend analysis by rule.']
  regulatory_program varchar [note: 'Name of the specific regulatory program or framework under which the violation was cited (e.g., Clean Air Act Title V, NPDES Stormwater, BSEE SEMS, OSHA PSM, PHMSA Integrity Management, SEC Regulation S-K, FERC Gas Tariff, BOEM OCSLA). Enables program-specific compliance trend analysis.']
  repeat_violation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this violation is a repeat occurrence of a previously cited violation at the same facility or for the same regulation within the lookback period defined by the issuing authority. True if repeat; False if first occurrence. Repeat violations typically attract enhanced penalties under EPA, OSHA, and BSEE enforcement policies.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the violation record: open (newly identified, no action taken), in_progress (corrective action underway), closed (fully resolved and verified), appealed (formal appeal filed with authority), contested (company disputes the finding), pending_verification (corrective action completed, awaiting regulatory confirmation). Drives compliance calendar and SOX control reporting.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which the company must submit a formal response, corrective action plan, or abatement plan to the issuing authority. Drives compliance calendar alerts and SOX control monitoring.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Identified root cause of the violation as determined through investigation (e.g., equipment failure, procedural non-compliance, human error, inadequate training, design deficiency, management system gap). Supports corrective action planning and recurrence prevention.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Standardized category of the root cause for trend analysis and systemic issue identification: equipment_failure, human_error, procedural (non-compliance with procedure), management_system (gap in management system), design_deficiency, or external_factor (weather, third-party action).']
  sec_disclosure_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the violation meets the materiality threshold requiring disclosure in SEC filings (10-K, 10-Q, 8-K) under SEC Regulation S-K Item 103 (legal proceedings) or other applicable disclosure requirements. True if disclosure is required; False otherwise.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the violation indicating its potential impact on operations, environment, safety, or regulatory standing. Critical: immediate threat to life, environment, or operations; Major: significant regulatory exposure; Moderate: notable non-compliance; Minor: administrative or procedural; Informational: observation only.']
  sox_control_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SOX internal control or control objective that was impacted or tested as a result of this violation. Used for SOX Section 302/404 compliance documentation and internal control deficiency reporting.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date the non-compliant condition or activity began, as determined by investigation or regulatory finding. Used to calculate duration of non-compliance and assess penalty exposure under per-day penalty frameworks (e.g., EPA Clean Air Act, PHMSA).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the violation record in the compliance management system. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  violation_category varchar [note: 'High-level domain category of the violation: environmental (emissions, spills, waste), safety (HSE/OSHA), financial (SEC/SOX/COPAS), operational (production, process), reporting (regulatory filings), or permitting (permit/license compliance). Supports ESG reporting and compliance dashboard segmentation.']
  violation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the non-compliant condition, activity, or omission as stated in the NOV or internal audit finding. Includes the specific regulatory requirement that was not met and the observed deviation.']
  violation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the violation by its nature: regulatory citation (specific rule breach), permit condition (permit term exceeded), license condition, reporting failure (missed or inaccurate filing), operational exceedance (production/emission limit exceeded), safety violation, environmental exceedance, or financial reporting non-compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regulatory_citation|permit_condition|license_condition|reporting_failure|operational_exceedance|safety_violation|environmental_exceedance|financial_reporting — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each regulatory violation, notice of violation (NOV), or non-compliance finding issued by a regulatory authority or identified through internal audit. Tracks violation type, issuing authority, regulation cited, facility or asset involved, discovery date, severity classification, corrective action plan (CAPA details including responsible party, target date, completion date, verification method, effectiveness review), monetary penalties assessed (amount, payment status, appeal status), and resolution status. Supports SOX internal controls, ESG reporting, and regulatory closure documentation.'
}

Table compliance.compliance_corrective_action {
  compliance_corrective_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the corrective and preventive action (CAPA) record. Primary key for the corrective_action data product. [Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER]']
  asset_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility, installation, or asset location where the violation or compliance gap occurred and where the corrective action is being implemented (e.g., offshore platform, refinery, pipeline segment, processing plant).']

  finance_afe_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_afe. Business justification: Major corrective actions (facility modifications, equipment upgrades) are capitalized under AFEs for asset tracking and depreciation. Links actual_cost_usd to AFE budget and enables capital vs. expens']
  inspection_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.inspection_event. Linking CAPA to triggering inspection event enable']

Links CAPA to specific material when equip']

  action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the specific corrective or preventive action to be taken, including the scope of work, methodology, and expected outcome. Supports regulatory closure documentation and audit evidence packages.']
  action_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the corrective action record. Drives compliance calendar tracking and regulatory closure documentation. Transitions: open → in_progress → pending_verification → closed. Overdue is a system-flagged state when target completion date is exceeded.']
  action_title varchar [note: 'Short, descriptive title summarizing the corrective action. Used as the primary display label in compliance dashboards, regulatory submissions, and management reports.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the action as corrective (addressing a known violation or nonconformance), preventive (proactively eliminating potential causes), immediate (emergency interim measure), systemic (root-cause-driven process change), or regulatory_directed (mandated by a regulatory authority such as BSEE, EPA, or PHMSA).']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the corrective action was fully implemented and all required activities were completed. Compared against target_completion_date to assess timeliness and regulatory compliance. Null until the action is completed.']
  actual_cost_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual total cost in US dollars incurred to implement the corrective action upon completion. Compared against estimated_cost_usd for budget variance analysis and financial reporting. Null until the action is completed and costs are finalized.']
  capa_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable reference number assigned to the corrective and preventive action record. Used in regulatory submissions, audit correspondence, and government reporting. Format: CAPA-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, management commentary, or contextual information related to the corrective action that does not fit into structured fields. Supports audit trail documentation and regulatory correspondence.']
  compliance_domain varchar [note: 'Business compliance domain to which this corrective action belongs, enabling cross-domain analytics and portfolio management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hse|environmental|financial|operational|safety|pipeline|offshore|reserves|process_safety|esg — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this corrective action record was first created in the data system. Distinct from initiated_timestamp which reflects the business event time. Supports SOX audit trail requirements.']
  effectiveness_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the effectiveness review was conducted to assess whether the corrective action successfully prevented recurrence of the violation or compliance gap. Typically performed 30-90 days after action closure.']
  effectiveness_review_notes varchar [note: 'Narrative documentation of the effectiveness review findings, including evidence of recurrence prevention, residual risks identified, and any follow-up actions recommended. Supports regulatory audit evidence packages.']
  effectiveness_review_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the post-implementation effectiveness review assessing whether the corrective action successfully eliminated the root cause and prevented recurrence. Supports continuous improvement tracking and regulatory audit readiness.']
  esg_category varchar [note: 'ESG reporting category to which this corrective action contributes, enabling aggregation for ESG disclosures, sustainability reports, and investor reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: environmental|social|governance|safety|ghg_emissions|spill|waste|biodiversity|human_rights — promote to reference product]']
  estimated_cost_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost in US dollars to implement the corrective action, including labor, materials, contractor services, and equipment. Used for CAPEX/OPEX budgeting, AFE preparation, and financial reporting under SOX and IFRS/GAAP.']
  initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the corrective action was formally initiated and entered into the compliance management system. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this transaction. Used to calculate response time against regulatory deadlines.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording the most recent modification to this corrective action record. Supports change tracking, SOX internal controls, and regulatory audit trail documentation.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the corrective action based on regulatory risk, safety impact, and potential penalty exposure. Critical actions typically involve imminent regulatory deadlines or safety-critical systems (e.g., BOP integrity, H2S exposure controls).']
  recurrence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this corrective action addresses a recurring violation or compliance gap that has previously been subject to a corrective action. Recurrence triggers escalated regulatory scrutiny and may indicate systemic management system failure under BSEE SEMS II.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'The regulatory body or governing authority whose requirement, inspection finding, or directive triggered or is associated with this corrective action (e.g., BSEE for offshore safety, EPA for environmental violations, PHMSA for pipeline safety, OSHA for workplace safety). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BSEE|EPA|PHMSA|OSHA|FERC|BOEM|SEC|OPEC|API|STATE|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory code, rule, or standard citation that was violated or is being addressed by this corrective action (e.g., \'30 CFR 250.1920\', \'40 CFR 60.18\', \'49 CFR 195.452\'). Used in regulatory correspondence and closure documentation.']
  regulatory_closure_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority formally acknowledged and accepted the closure of the corrective action, confirming that all regulatory requirements have been satisfied. Null until regulatory closure is confirmed.']
  regulatory_closure_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal regulatory closure documentation must be submitted to a regulatory authority (e.g., BSEE, EPA, PHMSA) upon completion of this corrective action. Drives the compliance calendar and submission workflow.']
  regulatory_deadline_date date [note: 'Externally mandated deadline imposed by a regulatory authority (e.g., BSEE, EPA, PHMSA) for completion of the corrective action. Distinct from the internal target_completion_date. Breach of this date may result in penalties, fines, or escalated enforcement action.']
  regulatory_submission_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number or tracking identifier assigned by the regulatory authority upon submission of the corrective action plan or closure documentation (e.g., BSEE incident number, EPA enforcement case number, PHMSA corrective action order number).']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or business unit accountable for executing the corrective action (e.g., HSE, Drilling, Production, Refining, Regulatory Affairs). Used for workload distribution reporting and compliance performance analytics.']
  responsible_party_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual assigned as the primary owner accountable for executing and completing the corrective action. Retained directly on the record for regulatory reporting and audit documentation where the workforce system may not be accessible.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Standardized classification of the root cause type to enable trend analysis and systemic improvement tracking across the compliance portfolio. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: human_factor|process_failure|equipment_failure|management_system|regulatory_gap|environmental|design_deficiency|contractor_performance — promote to reference product if additional categories are needed]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the identified root cause(s) of the violation, nonconformance, or compliance gap that necessitated this corrective action. Derived from root cause analysis (RCA) methodologies such as 5-Why or fishbone analysis.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the corrective action must be fully implemented and verified. May be set by internal management or mandated by a regulatory authority as part of a Notice of Violation (NOV) or Consent Order response deadline.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the verification activity was performed to confirm effective implementation of the corrective action. Required for regulatory closure packages submitted to BSEE, EPA, PHMSA, or other authorities.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify that the corrective action has been effectively implemented. Options include physical inspection, internal audit, functional test, document review, field observation, or independent third-party audit as required by regulatory authorities.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of corrective and preventive actions (CAPAs) initiated in response to regulatory violations, audit findings, or compliance gaps. Tracks action description, responsible party, target completion date, actual completion date, verification method, effectiveness review, and linkage to the originating violation or audit finding. Supports regulatory closure documentation.'
}

  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center.   petroleum_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.petroleum_product.
