  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the regulatory audit record, from scheduling through final closure. Drives compliance calendar management and escalation workflows.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit or inspection by the type of regulatory authority or internal program conducting it. Drives applicable regulatory framework, citation standards, and reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BSEE_INSPECTION|EPA_AUDIT|OSHA_INSPECTION|PHMSA_INSPECTION|FERC_AUDIT|BOEM_INSPECTION|INTERNAL_SOX|INTERNAL_HSE — promote to reference product]']
  cited_regulation varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory citation, code section, or standard violated as identified in the finding (e.g., \'30 CFR 250.440(a)\', \'40 CFR 60.18(c)\', \'29 CFR 1910.119(e)(1)\', \'API RP 14C Section 4.2\'). Provides the legal basis for each individual finding and drives citation-level compliance tracking.']
  corrective_action_completed_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the corrective action was completed and verified. Compared against corrective_action_due_date to assess timeliness of remediation. Required for regulatory closure submissions and SOX control effectiveness documentation.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the corrective action for the finding must be completed, as stipulated by the regulatory agency or internal compliance policy. Drives compliance calendar management and escalation triggers for overdue remediation items.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current resolution status of the corrective action for the finding. OVERDUE status triggers escalation to compliance management and potential regulatory notification. Supports compliance dashboard reporting and audit closure tracking.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction where the audited facility or operation is located (e.g., USA, GBR, NOR, SAU). Supports multinational compliance reporting, ESG disclosures, and country-level regulatory obligation tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the regulatory audit record was first created in the compliance management system. Supports audit trail integrity, SOX internal control documentation, and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  critical_finding_count varchar [note: 'Number of findings classified as critical or high-risk severity within this audit. Critical findings typically require immediate corrective action and executive notification. Supports risk-based compliance prioritization and SEC/BSEE mandatory disclosure assessments.']
  esg_category varchar [note: 'ESG pillar classification of the audit for sustainability and ESG reporting purposes. Environmental audits cover GHG emissions, LDAR, spill prevention. Social audits cover OSHA safety, workforce. Governance audits cover SOX, FCPA, financial controls. Supports ESG disclosure obligations under SEC climate rules, GRI, and SASB Oil & Gas Standard.']
  facility_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the facility, asset, or operational unit subject to the audit (e.g., \'Thunder Horse PDQ Platform\', \'Baytown Refinery\', \'Gulf Coast Pipeline Segment 7\'). Retained for reporting and audit trail clarity independent of the facility master record.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audited facility or asset by operational category. Determines applicable regulatory standards and inspection protocols. FPSO = Floating Production Storage and Offloading. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OFFSHORE_PLATFORM|ONSHORE_WELL|REFINERY|PIPELINE|PROCESSING_PLANT|TERMINAL|FPSO|PETROCHEMICAL_PLANT — promote to reference product]']
  finding_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual findings, deficiencies, or observations identified during the audit. Provides a summary metric for audit severity assessment and compliance trend analysis without requiring aggregation of the findings detail records.']
  finding_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the specific deficiency, non-conformance, or observation identified during the audit. Includes the nature of the violation, the condition observed, and the regulatory requirement that was not met. Core field for corrective action planning and regulatory response.']
  finding_risk_classification varchar [note: 'Risk severity level assigned to the individual finding based on potential safety, environmental, financial, or regulatory impact. CRITICAL findings may require immediate shutdown or regulatory notification. Drives corrective action prioritization and escalation protocols per HSE and SOX frameworks.']
  ghg_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the audit involves greenhouse gas emissions compliance, including methane emissions monitoring, GHG reporting under EPA 40 CFR Part 98, or carbon capture and storage (CCS) program compliance. Supports ESG reporting and SEC climate disclosure obligations.']
  h2s_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the audit involves H2S (Hydrogen Sulfide) safety compliance, including detection systems, emergency response plans, and worker protection measures. H2S is a critical safety hazard in oil and gas operations subject to OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 and API RP 55.']
  is_repeat_finding boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the finding is a recurrence of a previously identified deficiency at the same facility or for the same regulatory requirement. Repeat findings typically attract enhanced regulatory scrutiny, higher penalties, and mandatory escalation under BSEE, EPA, and OSHA enforcement policies.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the regulatory audit record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, SOX internal control documentation, and incremental data processing in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse architecture.']
  lead_auditor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary auditor or inspector responsible for conducting the audit. For government inspections, this is the agency inspector of record. For internal audits, this is the lead internal auditor. Required for audit trail integrity and regulatory correspondence.']
  norm_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the audit involves NORM (Naturally Occurring Radioactive Material) compliance, which is a specific regulatory concern in oil and gas production operations. NORM findings require specialized handling, disposal, and regulatory reporting under EPA and state radiation control programs.']
  overall_outcome varchar [note: 'The aggregate result or conclusion of the audit as determined by the auditing authority. Drives escalation, enforcement action, and executive compliance reporting. A VIOLATION_ISSUED outcome triggers mandatory corrective action tracking and potential regulatory penalty assessment.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty or fine assessed by the regulatory agency as a result of the audit findings, expressed in the reporting currency. Nullable when no financial penalty is assessed. Supports financial liability tracking, CAPEX/OPEX impact analysis, and SEC disclosure obligations for material regulatory penalties.']
  penalty_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the penalty amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for multinational operations where penalties may be assessed in local currency. Supports financial consolidation and IFRS/GAAP reporting.']
  penalty_payment_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the assessed regulatory penalty must be paid to the governing agency. Drives accounts payable scheduling and financial liability management. Nullable when no penalty is assessed.']
  prior_audit_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the prior audit in which the same or related finding was previously identified. Used to establish repeat-finding linkage for regulatory enforcement escalation and trend analysis. Nullable for first-occurrence findings.']
  recommended_corrective_action varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the corrective action recommended by the auditor to remediate the identified finding. May be prescribed by the regulatory agency or proposed by the internal compliance team. Forms the basis for the corrective action plan (CAP) and regulatory response submissions.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic or legal jurisdiction under which the audit is conducted (e.g., \'Federal — Gulf of Mexico OCS\', \'State — Texas Railroad Commission\', \'Federal — EPA Region 6\', \'International — North Sea UKCS\'). Determines applicable regulatory framework and reporting obligations for multinational operations.']
  regulatory_program varchar [note: 'The specific regulatory program or rule set under which the audit is conducted (e.g., \'SEMS — Safety and Environmental Management System\', \'LDAR — Leak Detection and Repair\', \'Pipeline Integrity Management\', \'SEC Reserves Disclosure\', \'OPEC Quota Compliance\', \'ESG Reporting\'). Maps the audit to the governing compliance framework.']
  report_issued_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal audit report was officially issued by the auditing authority or internal compliance team. Triggers response deadlines and corrective action plan submission timelines under applicable regulatory frameworks.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the company must submit a formal written response or corrective action plan to the auditing authority. Calculated from report_issued_date per applicable regulatory requirements. Drives compliance calendar management and escalation workflows.']
  response_submitted_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the company submitted its formal written response or corrective action plan to the auditing authority. Compared against response_due_date to assess regulatory response timeliness and compliance with submission deadlines.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual, team, or organizational unit accountable for implementing the corrective action for the finding. Typically the operations manager, HSE manager, or process owner for the audited facility or function.']

  Note: 'Master record of regulatory audits and inspections conducted by government agencies or internal compliance teams, including individual findings. Tracks audit type (BSEE inspection, EPA audit, OSHA inspection, internal SOX audit), auditing authority, facility or asset audited, audit date range, lead auditor, scope, overall audit outcome, and detailed findings (specific deficiency, regulation cited, risk classification, recommended corrective action, responsible party, resolution status). Distinct from HSE incident records — this captures formal compliance audit events and their outcomes.'
}

Table compliance.consent_order {
  consent_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the consent order record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key. Entity role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — a binding regulatory instrument with its own lifecycle, effective dates, and compliance obligations.']
  afe_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.afe_budget. Business justification: Consent orders requiring remediation, facility upgrades, or environmental restoration are funded through dedicated AFE budgets. Linking consent order to AFE enables tracking remediation costs, managin']
  asset_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary facility, plant, refinery, offshore platform, pipeline segment, or operational asset subject to the consent order. Links to the asset management domain for facility-level compliance tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Consent order fulfillment costs (SEP projects, monitoring, reporting) are allocated to cost centers for compliance budget tracking and variance analysis. Essential for tracking milestone completion co']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_counterparty. Business justification: Consent orders target counterparties as responsible parties for enforcement. Real process: tracking which counterparty is subject to enforcement actions for joint venture liability allocation, operato']
  drilling_well_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to drilling.drilling_well. Business justification: Consent orders are frequently issued against specific wells for well integrity violations, improper P&A, or environmental releases. Linking consent_order to drilling_well enables well-level legal obli']
  petroleum_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.petroleum_product. Business justification: Consent orders may mandate product-specific actions: reformulation requirements, specification changes, product discontinuation, or enhanced testing protocols. Links legal settlement terms to affected']
  pipeline_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.pipeline_segment. Business justification: PHMSA consent orders and state pipeline safety consent agreements target specific pipeline segments (e.g., pressure reduction orders, repair mandates). Segment-level consent order tracking is required']
  production_facility_id varchar [note: 'EPA Facility Registry Service (FRS) unique identifier for the regulated facility associated with this consent order. Used for cross-referencing EPA enforcement databases (ECHO, ICIS) and regulatory submissions.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Consent orders relate to specific facilities/operations within profit centers; remediation costs, penalties, and ongoing compliance costs must be tracked at profit center level for segment P&L and per']
  regulatory_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_authority. Business justification: A consent order is issued by a specific regulatory authority (e.g., EPA consent decrees, state environmental agency consent orders). The consent_order table has issuing_agency as a STRING which is den']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Consent orders are submitted to and approved by regulatory authorities, making them regulatory filings. Adding regulatory_filing_id FK links the consent order to its filing record, enabling tracking o']
  remediation_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Consent order fulfillment often requires vendor services (environmental remediation contractors, monitoring equipment suppliers). Real business process: consent order compliance execution via procurem']
  subject_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Consent orders are issued against legal entities (accounts) for enforcement actions. Real process: tracking which account is subject to each consent order for compliance monitoring, financial accruals']
  violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.violation. Business justification: A consent order is entered into to resolve a specific regulatory violation or enforcement action. Linking consent_order to the primary violation it resolves provides the enforcement chain from violati']
  well_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.well_asset. Business justification: Consent orders are issued against specific wells (e.g., well integrity consent orders, plugging orders, spill remediation orders). consent_order links to asset_facility but not well_asset; well-level ']
  agency_case_officer varchar [note: 'Name of the primary regulatory agency enforcement officer or case manager responsible for overseeing compliance with this consent order. Used for correspondence, milestone reporting submissions, and audit trail.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the consent order has legal effect (e.g., USA, GBR, NOR). Supports multinational compliance tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this consent order record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this consent order record (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multinational financial reporting under IFRS/GAAP.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the consent order becomes legally binding and enforceable, as stipulated in the instrument. Marks the start of the compliance obligation period and triggers milestone scheduling.']
  enablon_case_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the corresponding enforcement case or compliance obligation record in the Enablon HSE Management system. Enables traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date on which the consent order was formally signed or entered by all parties (company and regulatory agency), which may differ from the effective date. Represents the principal real-world business event timestamp for this agreement.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the consent order expires or all obligations are scheduled to be fully discharged, as specified in the instrument. Null for open-ended orders with no fixed termination. Used for compliance calendar management.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational fulfillment status of the consent order\'s compliance obligations: on_track (milestones being met), at_risk (potential delay identified), overdue (missed milestone or report), fulfilled (all obligations satisfied), or partially_fulfilled (some milestones complete). Distinct from order_status which reflects the legal lifecycle.']
  ghg_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consent order relates to greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions violations, including methane, CO2, or other regulated GHG emissions under EPA Clean Air Act or state equivalents. Supports GHG compliance and ESG reporting.']
  hse_classification varchar [note: 'HSE domain classification of the consent order: environmental (spills, emissions, waste disposal), safety (worker safety, OSHA), health (occupational health), process_safety (PSM, BSEE), or combined (multiple HSE domains). Aligns with Enablon HSE Management categorization.']
  is_esg_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this consent order must be disclosed in the company\'s ESG or sustainability report. True for orders involving GHG emissions, environmental spills, HSE violations, or other ESG-material events.']
  is_sec_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this consent order meets the materiality threshold requiring disclosure in SEC filings under Regulation S-K Item 103 (Legal Proceedings). True if the penalty exceeds $300,000 or is otherwise material to investors.']
  jurisdiction_level varchar [note: 'Level of governmental jurisdiction under which the consent order was issued: federal (EPA, BSEE, OSHA, PHMSA, FERC, BOEM), state (state environmental or safety agency), local (county or municipal authority), or international (foreign regulatory body). Determines reporting chain and legal venue.']
  jurisdiction_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter U.S. state code (FIPS/USPS standard) for the state in which the consent order has legal effect, or the state agency that issued it. Null for purely federal or international orders.']
  last_report_submitted_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent mandatory compliance progress report was submitted to the issuing regulatory agency. Used to verify reporting cadence adherence and identify overdue submissions.']
  legal_counsel_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal or external legal counsel representing the company in negotiations and ongoing compliance management for this consent order. Supports attorney-client privilege tracking and legal matter management.']
  milestone_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct compliance milestones or deliverables stipulated in the consent order (e.g., equipment installation deadlines, audit submissions, monitoring report due dates). Used for progress tracking and compliance calendar population.']
  milestones_completed_count varchar [note: 'Number of compliance milestones that have been fulfilled and accepted by the regulatory agency to date. Compared against milestone_count to assess overall fulfillment progress.']
  next_milestone_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the next outstanding compliance milestone or deliverable must be completed and submitted to the regulatory agency. Drives compliance calendar alerts and escalation workflows in Enablon HSE Management.']
  next_report_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the next mandatory progress or compliance status report must be submitted to the issuing regulatory agency under the consent order\'s reporting obligations.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known docket or case number assigned by the issuing regulatory agency to uniquely identify this consent order, consent decree, or settlement agreement (e.g., EPA Docket No. CAA-06-2023-0042). Used for all regulatory correspondence and submissions.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the consent order: active (in force and obligations ongoing), fulfilled (all milestones and penalties satisfied), terminated (voided or withdrawn), superseded (replaced by a subsequent order), or under_negotiation (draft stage prior to execution). Drives compliance calendar and SOX disclosure.']
  order_title varchar [note: 'Official short title or caption of the consent order as designated by the issuing agency, used for identification in regulatory filings, internal compliance registers, and SEC disclosure documents.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory instrument: consent_order (administrative), consent_decree (judicial/court-entered), settlement_agreement (negotiated resolution), administrative_order (unilateral agency directive), or compliance_order (corrective mandate). Determines legal enforceability and reporting treatment.']
  regulation_citation varchar [note: 'Specific statutory or regulatory citation(s) that form the legal basis for the consent order (e.g., Clean Air Act § 113(d), 40 CFR Part 60, 30 CFR § 250.401). Supports regulatory compliance mapping and legal review.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which progress reports must be submitted to the issuing regulatory agency under the terms of the consent order: monthly, quarterly, semi_annual, annual, one_time, or as_required. Populates the compliance obligation calendar.']
  responsible_business_unit varchar [note: 'Name of the internal business unit, division, or operating company accountable for managing compliance with this consent order (e.g., Upstream E&P, Refining and Marketing, Midstream Pipeline Operations). Aligns with SAP CO cost center hierarchy.']
  sap_legal_case_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the corresponding legal case or provision record in SAP S/4HANA (FI/Legal module) used for financial provisioning, penalty accruals, and CAPEX/OPEX cost tracking associated with this consent order.']
  sep_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar value of penalty mitigation credit granted for Supplemental Environmental Projects (SEPs) agreed to as part of the consent order settlement. SEPs are environmentally beneficial projects that reduce the net cash penalty obligation. Specific to EPA enforcement settlements.']
  stipulated_penalty_per_day decimal(18,2) [note: 'Daily stipulated penalty amount (in the designated currency) that accrues for each day of non-compliance with a milestone or reporting obligation under the consent order. Critical for financial risk quantification and CAPEX/OPEX planning.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this consent order record was most recently modified in the lakehouse silver layer, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, audit trail, and SOX internal controls.']

  Note: 'Master record of consent orders, consent decrees, and settlement agreements entered into with regulatory agencies including EPA, BSEE, OSHA, and state regulators. Tracks issuing agency, violation basis, agreed remediation actions, penalty amounts, compliance milestones, reporting obligations imposed by the order, and current fulfillment status.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_penalty {
  regulatory_penalty_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each regulatory penalty record in the Oil Gas compliance system. Primary key for the regulatory_penalty data product.']
  asset_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility, plant, terminal, or operational site where the violation occurred that resulted in the penalty (e.g., refinery, processing plant, offshore platform, pipeline segment).']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Penalties require cost center allocation for budget tracking, variance analysis, and departmental accountability. Real business process: penalty accrual, payment tracking, and budget impact analysis i']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Penalties require GL account mapping for accrual posting, payment clearing, and financial statement preparation. Links assessed_amount, settled_amount, and paid_amount to proper liability and expense ']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the compliance obligation that was violated, linking the penalty to the specific regulatory requirement that triggered it.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the permit associated with the violation, applicable when the penalty arises from a permit condition breach (e.g., emission limit exceedance under an EPA air permit).']
  petroleum_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.petroleum_product. Business justification: Penalties assessed for product-specific violations: off-spec fuel sales, contaminated product releases, mislabeled products, or unauthorized product marketing. Product identification enables penalty c']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Penalties impact segment profitability and must be allocated to profit centers for accurate segment P&L, performance measurement, and financial statement segment reporting. Essential for management ac']
  regulatory_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_authority. Business justification: A regulatory penalty is assessed by a specific regulatory authority (e.g., EPA assesses Clean Air Act penalties, BSEE assesses offshore safety penalties). The regulatory_penalty table has assessing_au']
  well_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.well_asset. Business justification: Penalties are assessed against specific wells (e.g., well integrity violations, spill penalties, UIC violations). regulatory_penalty has well_api_number as a denormalized text field; replacing with we']
  accrual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount accrued in the financial statements for this penalty under IFRS/GAAP contingent liability provisions. May differ from assessed or settled amounts based on probability-weighted estimates. Supports SOX financial controls and SEC disclosure.']
  appeal_decision_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority or administrative tribunal issued its final decision on the appeal. Triggers update to settled_amount and penalty_status.']
  appeal_filed_date date [note: 'Date on which the company formally filed an appeal or contest against the penalty assessment with the regulatory authority or administrative tribunal.']
  appeal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any formal appeal or contest filed against the penalty assessment. Determines whether the settled_amount may change and affects financial accrual estimates under IFRS IAS 37 / ASC 450.']
  assessed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary penalty amount originally assessed by the regulatory authority before any reductions, waivers, or negotiated settlements. Used as the basis for financial accrual and liability disclosure.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority formally assessed and issued the penalty notice or order. Serves as the principal business event date for the penalty lifecycle and triggers payment due date calculation.']
  authority_case_number varchar [note: 'Internal case or docket number assigned by the assessing regulatory authority for tracking the enforcement action in their system (distinct from the company-assigned penalty_number).']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the mandated corrective action plan must be completed and demonstrated to the regulatory authority. Applicable when corrective_action_required is true.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the regulatory authority has mandated a corrective action plan (CAP) or supplemental environmental project (SEP) as a condition of the penalty settlement or consent order.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the jurisdiction where the violation occurred (e.g., USA, GBR, NGA). Supports multinational regulatory liability reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory penalty record was first created in the compliance management system. Supports audit trail and SOX internal controls.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this penalty record (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multinational financial reporting and currency conversion.']
  enablon_incident_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier from the Enablon HSE Management system for the incident or non-compliance event that triggered this penalty. Enables cross-system traceability between HSE incident management and regulatory enforcement.']
  facility_name varchar [note: 'Name of the facility, plant, or operational site where the violation occurred. Retained as a denormalized field for reporting and regulatory submissions without requiring a join.']
  ghg_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the penalty is related to a Greenhouse Gas (GHG) emission violation (e.g., methane flaring exceedance, GHG reporting failure). Supports ESG reporting and carbon liability disclosure.']
  hse_classification varchar [note: 'Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) classification of the penalty, indicating whether it relates to an environmental, occupational safety, health, process safety, or security violation. Supports ESG reporting and HSE performance metrics.']
  is_sec_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this penalty meets the materiality threshold requiring disclosure in SEC filings (10-K, 10-Q) under SEC Regulation S-K Item 103 (Legal Proceedings). Supports SOX compliance and investor relations.']
  jurisdiction_level varchar [note: 'Level of government jurisdiction under which the penalty was assessed. Determines applicable legal framework, appeal venue, and disclosure requirements.']
  paid_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual monetary amount paid to the regulatory authority to date. May be less than settled_amount if payment is in installments or partially paid. Supports cash flow reporting and accounts payable reconciliation.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the penalty payment was remitted to the regulatory authority. Used to confirm timely payment and close the penalty record.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the penalty payment must be remitted to the regulatory authority to avoid additional interest, late fees, or escalated enforcement. Drives accounts payable scheduling.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status of the penalty amount. Drives accounts payable follow-up, interest accrual, and regulatory relationship management.']
  penalty_number varchar [note: 'Externally assigned penalty or civil penalty case number issued by the regulatory authority (e.g., EPA enforcement case number, BSEE incident of non-compliance number, PHMSA penalty order number). Used for correspondence and regulatory tracking.']
  penalty_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory penalty. Drives financial accrual decisions and regulatory liability disclosure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: assessed|contested|settled|paid|waived|appealed|closed — promote to reference product]']
  penalty_title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title of the penalty or enforcement action as designated by the assessing regulatory authority (e.g., \'Notice of Violation - Flaring Exceedance\', \'Civil Penalty Order - Pipeline Integrity\').']
  regulation_citation varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory citation, statute, or code section under which the penalty was assessed (e.g., \'Clean Air Act Section 113(b)\', \'30 CFR Part 250\', \'49 CFR Part 195\'). Supports legal defense and compliance gap analysis.']
  regulation_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the regulation, rule, or statute under which the penalty was assessed (e.g., \'Clean Air Act\', \'Clean Water Act\', \'Pipeline Safety Improvement Act\', \'Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act\').']
  repeat_violation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this penalty arises from a repeat violation of the same regulation or permit condition within a defined lookback period. Repeat violations typically attract higher penalty multipliers and heightened regulatory scrutiny.']
  responsible_business_unit varchar [note: 'Name of the business unit or division within Oil Gas that is accountable for the violation and responsible for penalty payment and corrective action (e.g., Upstream E&P, Refining, Midstream, Petrochemicals).']
  sap_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA FI module document number for the financial posting (accrual or payment) associated with this penalty. Enables reconciliation between the compliance register and the general ledger.']
  settled_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final negotiated or adjudicated penalty amount agreed upon after appeal, mitigation, or settlement proceedings. May differ from assessed_amount due to reductions granted by the authority. Used for final financial accrual and SEC disclosure.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code for the jurisdiction where the violation occurred and penalty was assessed. Applicable for state-level enforcement actions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory penalty record was last modified in the compliance management system. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and SOX internal controls.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of monetary penalties, fines, and civil penalties assessed by regulatory authorities for compliance violations. Tracks penalty amount, assessing authority, violation basis, assessment date, payment due date, payment status, appeal status, and final settled amount. Supports financial accrual reporting and regulatory liability disclosure.'
}

Real process: entity certification tracking for vendor qualification, operator certification requirements, and determi']
  certifying_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Certification bodies (ISO registrars, API auditors) are vendors providing certification audit services. Real business process: certification services procurement. Replaces text certifying_body with FK']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Certification fees and audit costs are allocated to facility or compliance cost centers for operational budgeting. Links certification_fee_amount to cost objects for variance analysis and joint ventur']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_counterparty. Business justification: Counterparties hold certifications as operators or service providers. Real process: tracking counterparty certifications for operator qualification, JV partner vetting, and verifying that counterparti']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Certifications are often required to fulfill specific regulatory compliance obligations (e.g., ISO 14001 for environmental compliance, API certifications for safety). Adding obligation_id FK links the']
  operating_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.operating_license. Business justification: A compliance certification may be required as a condition of an operating license (e.g., an offshore operating license requiring SEMS certification, a pipeline operating license requiring operator qua']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: A compliance certification may be required as a condition of a specific permit (e.g., an air permit requiring ISO 14001 certification, a BOEM permit requiring SEMS certification). Linking compliance_c']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Certifications (ISO, SEMS, API) are obtained for specific operating segments/profit centers; certification costs and maintenance expenses must be allocated to profit centers for accurate segment P&L a']
  regulatory_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_authority. Business justification: A compliance certification is issued or recognized by a specific regulatory authority or governing body (e.g., ISO certifications recognized by EPA, SEMS certifications recognized by BSEE). The compli']
  well_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.well_asset. Business justification: Well certifications (mechanical integrity certifications, well control certifications, BSEE SEMS well certifications) are issued for specific wells. compliance_certification links to asset_facility bu']
  audit_cycle_years varchar [note: 'The standard recertification or surveillance audit cycle in years as defined by the certifying body (e.g., 3 years for ISO management system certifications with annual surveillance audits).']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'The organizational business unit or division responsible for maintaining and renewing this certification (e.g., \'Upstream Gulf of Mexico\', \'Downstream Refining\', \'Midstream Pipeline Operations\', \'Petrochemicals\').']
  certificate_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference path, document management system ID, or URL to the scanned or digital copy of the official certificate document issued by the certifying body. Supports audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned by the certifying body or internal compliance system to uniquely reference this certification (e.g., API-2024-00123, ISO14001-CERT-0045, BSEE-SEMS-2024-001).']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the certification. \'active\' indicates the certification is valid and in force; \'expired\' indicates the certification has passed its expiration date without renewal; \'suspended\' indicates temporary withdrawal by the certifying body; \'revoked\' indicates permanent cancellation; \'pending_renewal\' indicates renewal is in progress; \'withdrawn\' indicates voluntary surrender.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the certification by its governing domain (e.g., management_system for ISO certifications, regulatory_attestation for BSEE/BOEM, financial_attestation for SOX CEO/CFO, operator_qualification for pipeline operator qualifications). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: management_system|operator_qualification|regulatory_attestation|financial_attestation|api_standard|pipeline_safety|environmental|hse|other — promote to reference product]']
  certifying_auditor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the lead auditor or assessor from the certifying body who conducted the certification audit or assessment.']
  certifying_body_accreditation varchar [note: 'Accreditation number or reference of the certifying body issued by an accreditation authority (e.g., ANAB, UKAS, DAkkS accreditation number), confirming the certifying body is authorized to issue the certification standard.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction in which the certification is applicable or was issued (e.g., USA, GBR, NLD, SAU, NGA).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this certification record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the certification fee amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, SAR). Supports multi-currency financial reporting for multinational operations.']
  enablon_cert_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for this certification record in the Enablon HSE Management system, enabling traceability between the enterprise data platform and the operational HSE system of record.']
  esg_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification is referenced in or supports the company\'s Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) reporting disclosures (e.g., ISO 14001 certification cited in ESG report, GHG verification certifications).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the certification expires and is no longer valid unless renewed. Null for certifications with no defined expiry (e.g., certain SOX attestations). Used to drive renewal workflows and compliance calendar alerts.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The fee paid to the certifying body for the initial certification or most recent renewal audit, expressed in the currency defined by currency_code. Used for OPEX budgeting and cost tracking.']
  ghg_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification is related to Greenhouse Gas (GHG) emissions verification, carbon accounting, or climate-related compliance (e.g., ISO 14064 GHG verification, EPA GHG Reporting Program certifications).']
  hse_classification varchar [note: 'Classifies the certification by its Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) domain. \'environmental\' for ISO 14001 and EPA-related certifications; \'occupational_health_safety\' for ISO 45001 and OSHA certifications; \'process_safety\' for API RP 750/OSHA PSM certifications; \'combined_hse\' for integrated management system certifications; \'not_applicable\' for financial or non-HSE certifications.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the certifying body formally issued or granted the certification. Represents the start of the certification\'s validity period.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent surveillance, recertification, or assessment audit conducted by the certifying body against this certification standard.']
  major_nonconformance_count varchar [note: 'Number of major nonconformances identified during the most recent certification audit. Major nonconformances typically require resolution before the certificate can be issued or maintained.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the next planned surveillance or recertification audit scheduled by the certifying body. Used to drive compliance calendar planning and resource allocation.']
  nonconformance_count varchar [note: 'Number of nonconformances or findings identified during the most recent certification audit. Major and minor nonconformances must be resolved before or after certification issuance per certifying body requirements.']
  offshore_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification applies to offshore operations (True) or onshore operations (False). Offshore certifications are subject to BSEE/BOEM jurisdiction and additional regulatory requirements.']
  operator_qualification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification is an Operator Qualification (OQ) certification for pipeline operations personnel, as required by PHMSA regulations for covered tasks on pipeline facilities.']
  regulatory_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the certification or evidence of certification was submitted to the relevant regulatory authority. Null if regulatory_submission_required is False or submission has not yet occurred.']
  regulatory_submission_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification or evidence of its maintenance must be formally submitted to a regulatory authority (e.g., BSEE SEMS certification submission, SEC SOX certifications filed with annual reports).']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the renewal application or re-certification audit must be initiated or completed to maintain continuous certification coverage. Typically set ahead of the expiration date based on certifying body requirements.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the renewal process for this certification. \'not_required\' for certifications that do not expire; \'pending\' when renewal has not yet been initiated; \'in_progress\' when the renewal audit or application is underway; \'submitted\' when the renewal application has been filed; \'approved\' when renewal is confirmed; \'rejected\' when renewal was denied.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the organizational scope, geographic boundaries, operational activities, and processes covered by the certification (e.g., \'Offshore drilling operations in Gulf of Mexico including platforms A, B, C\', \'Refinery crude distillation and hydrodesulfurization units at Baytown facility\').']
  sems_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification is associated with the BSEE Safety and Environmental Management System (SEMS) program applicable to offshore oil and gas operations on the U.S. Outer Continental Shelf.']
  sox_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification is directly associated with a Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) internal control requirement, such as CEO/CFO Section 302 or Section 906 certifications, or certifications supporting SOX financial reporting controls.']
  standard varchar [note: 'The specific standard, regulation, or framework against which the certification is issued (e.g., ISO 14001:2015, ISO 45001:2018, ISO 55001:2014, API RP 75, BSEE SEMS, SOX Section 302, PHMSA OQ, OSHA PSM 29 CFR 1910.119).']
  state_province_code varchar [note: 'State or province code where the certification applies, using ISO 3166-2 subdivision codes (e.g., US-TX for Texas, US-LA for Louisiana). Relevant for state-level regulatory certifications.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the certification (e.g., \'ISO 14001:2015 Environmental Management System Certification\', \'BSEE Safety and Environmental Management System Certification\', \'SOX CEO/CFO Section 302 Certification\').']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this certification record was most recently modified in the enterprise data platform. Supports change tracking and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Master record of compliance certifications, attestations, and declarations submitted to regulatory bodies or maintained internally. Includes API certifications, ISO 14001 and ISO 45001 certifications, BSEE SEMS certifications, pipeline operator qualifications, and SOX CEO/CFO certifications. Tracks certifying body, certification standard, scope, issue date, expiration date, and renewal status.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_authority {
  regulatory_authority_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for regulatory_authority']
  parent_regulatory_authority_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on regulatory_authority (parent_regulatory_authority_id)']
  abbreviation varchar [note: 'Common short form or acronym used for the authority.']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the authority.']
  address varchar [note: 'Physical mailing address of the authority\'s main office.']
  authority_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the authority by its governance level.']
  compliance_scope varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory domain(s) the authority oversees.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary public email address for contacting the authority.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the authority record was first created in the system.']
  data_submission_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for submitting data to the authority.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the authority became effective or was recognized.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO‑3 country code of the primary jurisdiction the authority covers.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the authority record.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Public telephone number for the authority.']
  regulatory_authority_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the authority\'s mandate and responsibilities.']
  regulatory_authority_name varchar [note: 'Official full name of the regulatory authority (e.g., Environmental Protection Agency).']
  regulatory_body_code varchar [note: 'Internal or industry code used to reference the authority.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Typical frequency at which the authority requires regulatory submissions.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the authority ceased operations, if applicable.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website address for the regulatory authority.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for regulatory_authority. Referenced by regulatory_authority_id.'
}

Table procurement.vendor {
  vendor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor record. Primary key.']
  active_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether the vendor is currently active and available for new procurement activities.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the vendor\'s registered business address, typically containing street number and street name.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the vendor\'s registered business address for additional address details such as suite, building, or floor number.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Vendor\'s bank account number for electronic funds transfer and payment processing.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution where the vendor maintains their primary business account for payment receipt.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'Bank routing number, SWIFT code, or IBAN used for international and domestic wire transfers to the vendor.']
  blacklist_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor was added to the blacklist.']
  blacklist_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether the vendor is blacklisted or prohibited from doing business due to compliance violations, safety incidents, or contractual breaches.']
  blacklist_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the vendor was blacklisted, including reference to specific incidents, violations, or regulatory actions.']
  business_category varchar [note: 'Detailed business category describing the vendor\'s primary goods or services: drilling services, oilfield equipment, refinery chemicals, catalysts, MRO (Maintenance Repair and Operations) materials, EPC services, FEED services, transportation, logistics, or other specialized categories. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: drilling_services|oilfield_equipment|refinery_chemicals|catalysts|mro_materials|epc_services|feed_services|transportation|logistics|consulting|inspection|testing|environmental_services — promote to reference product]']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the vendor\'s registered business address.']
  copas_classification varchar [note: 'COPAS-compliant vendor classification code used for joint interest billing and AFE (Authorization for Expenditure) cost allocation in joint ventures.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the vendor\'s registered business address.']
  country_of_incorporation varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the country where the vendor is legally incorporated or registered.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor record was first created in the procurement system.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit DUNS number assigned by Dun & Bradstreet for vendor identification and credit assessment.']
  hse_rating varchar [note: 'HSE (Health Safety and Environment) performance rating based on incident history, safety audits, and compliance with OSHA, EPA, and API standards.']
  insurance_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number for the vendor\'s liability insurance policy, required for contractor and service provider qualification.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the vendor\'s current liability insurance coverage.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor record was last updated or modified.']
  last_purchase_order_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent purchase order issued to this vendor, used for vendor activity tracking and performance analysis.']
  minority_owned_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether the vendor is certified as a minority-owned business enterprise for diversity and inclusion reporting.']
  payment_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code representing the preferred or default currency for payments to this vendor.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms negotiated with the vendor, such as Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30, or other custom payment schedules.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the vendor\'s registered business address.']
  preferred_vendor_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether the vendor has preferred status based on performance, pricing, or strategic partnership agreements.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact person for procurement communications, purchase order confirmations, and vendor correspondence.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact person at the vendor organization for procurement and operational communications.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the vendor contact, including country and area codes.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor was last qualified or approved for procurement activities.']
  qualification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor\'s current qualification status expires and requires renewal or reassessment.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current qualification status of the vendor based on HSE (Health Safety and Environment) compliance, financial stability, technical capability, and past performance assessments.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official business registration or incorporation number issued by the vendor\'s country of incorporation. Used for legal verification and compliance.']
  small_business_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether the vendor qualifies as a small business under applicable government or industry definitions.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region of the vendor\'s registered business address.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number or employer identification number (EIN) used for tax reporting and compliance in the vendor\'s jurisdiction.']
  vendor_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier or vendor number used in procurement systems and purchase orders. Maps to SAP vendor master number.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the vendor, supplier, or service provider as registered in their country of incorporation.']
  vendor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor based on their primary business relationship: contractor (drilling, construction), supplier (equipment, materials), service provider (maintenance, logistics), EPC (Engineering Procurement and Construction) firm, FEED (Front-End Engineering Design) contractor, or consultant.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Vendor\'s official website URL for reference and additional company information.']

  Note: 'Master record for all vendors, suppliers, and service providers in the oil and gas procurement ecosystem, including drilling contractors, oilfield equipment suppliers, EPC firms, FEED contractors, catalyst suppliers, and maintenance material vendors. Captures vendor legal name, registration number, country of incorporation, vendor type (contractor, supplier, service provider), business category (drilling services, oilfield equipment, refinery chemicals, MRO), tax identification, DUNS number, SAP vendor master number, payment terms, currency, bank details, preferred contact, qualification status, blacklist flag, COPAS-compliant vendor classification, and active/inactive status. Serves as the SSOT for vendor identity within the procurement domain.'
}

Table procurement.vendor_qualification {
  vendor_qualification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor qualification record. Primary key for the vendor qualification entity.']
  petroleum_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.petroleum_product. Business justification: Vendor qualification scoped to specific petroleum product categories (crude supply, refined products, petrochemicals). Ensures vendors meet quality, compliance, and technical requirements for specific']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor entity being qualified. Links to the vendor master record in the procurement system.']
  api_certification_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of American Petroleum Institute (API) certification numbers held by the vendor. API certifications validate compliance with industry standards for equipment, materials, and services used in oil and gas operations. Examples include API Spec Q1, API Spec 6A, API Spec 5CT.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name and title of the authorized individual who granted final approval for the vendor qualification. Typically a Procurement Manager, Supply Chain Director, or Vice President depending on qualification scope and value limit.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the approval authority officially approved the vendor qualification. May differ from qualification date if approval is backdated to evaluation completion.']
  approval_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or organizational scope of the vendor qualification. Global indicates qualification for all Oil Gas operations worldwide, regional indicates qualification for specific geographic regions (e.g., North America, Middle East), asset-specific indicates qualification for specific facilities or fields, and project-specific indicates qualification for a single Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) project.']
  approved_regions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of geographic regions or countries where the vendor is qualified to operate. Uses three-letter ISO country codes (e.g., USA, SAU, NOR, BRA). Applicable when approval scope is regional or asset-specific.']
  approved_value_limit_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum cumulative contract value in United States Dollars (USD) that the vendor is authorized to supply under this qualification. Used for Authorization for Expenditure (AFE) and Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) budget control. Null indicates no monetary limit.']
  avl_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the vendor on the Approved Vendor List (AVL) for the qualified commodity category. Active indicates vendor is eligible for Request for Quotation (RFQ) issuance and direct awards, inactive indicates qualification expired or vendor requested removal, suspended indicates temporary hold due to performance or compliance issues, and pending removal indicates disqualification process underway.']
  background_check_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether background checks (corporate due diligence, sanctions screening, anti-corruption checks, beneficial ownership verification) have been completed for the vendor. True indicates background check completed and cleared, False indicates background check not completed or issues identified.']
  commodity_category_code varchar [note: 'Standardized commodity classification code aligned with SAP Material Management (MM) material groups or UNSPSC codes. Defines the specific product or service category the vendor is qualified to supply.']
  commodity_category_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the commodity category corresponding to the commodity category code. Examples include Drilling Services, Wellhead Equipment, Refinery Catalysts, Piping and Valves, or Instrumentation and Controls.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor qualification record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  disqualification_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation for vendor disqualification or qualification rejection. May include technical deficiencies, financial instability, Health Safety and Environment (HSE) violations, ethical breaches, contract performance failures, or regulatory non-compliance. Null if qualification is approved.']
  evaluator_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the evaluator who conducted the vendor qualification assessment. Used for follow-up questions and audit trail.']
  evaluator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the procurement or technical specialist who conducted the vendor qualification evaluation. Provides accountability and contact for qualification questions.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the vendor qualification expires and requires renewal or re-qualification. Null indicates indefinite qualification subject to periodic review.']
  financial_evaluation_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score representing the vendor\'s financial stability and creditworthiness assessment. Typically scored on a 0-100 scale based on financial statements, credit rating, liquidity ratios, debt-to-equity ratio, and payment history. Ensures vendor can fulfill long-term contracts.']
  hse_evaluation_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score representing the vendor\'s Health Safety and Environment (HSE) performance and compliance assessment. Typically scored on a 0-100 scale based on Total Recordable Incident Rate (TRIR), Lost Time Injury Frequency (LTIF), environmental compliance record, HSE management systems, and safety culture. Critical for high-risk operations such as drilling and Simultaneous Operations (SIMOPS).']
  insurance_coverage_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor\'s insurance coverage (general liability, professional indemnity, workers compensation, pollution liability) has been verified and meets Oil Gas requirements. True indicates insurance verified and adequate, False indicates insurance not verified or inadequate.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the vendor\'s insurance coverage. Used to trigger insurance renewal verification and prevent engagement of vendors with lapsed coverage.']
  iso_14001_certification_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number for ISO 14001 Environmental Management System certification. Validates vendor\'s environmental management processes and compliance capability.']
  iso_45001_certification_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number for ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management System certification. Validates vendor\'s occupational health and safety management processes.']
  iso_9001_certification_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number for ISO 9001 Quality Management System certification. Validates vendor\'s quality management processes and continuous improvement capability.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the individual who last modified the vendor qualification record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor qualification record was last updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data freshness assessment.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review or re-qualification assessment of the vendor. Used to trigger re-qualification workflows and ensure ongoing vendor capability and compliance.']
  overall_qualification_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite weighted score combining technical, financial, and Health Safety and Environment (HSE) evaluation scores. Used for vendor ranking and Approved Vendor List (AVL) prioritization. Typically scored on a 0-100 scale.']
  preferred_vendor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has preferred vendor status for the qualified commodity category. Preferred vendors receive priority consideration in Request for Quotation (RFQ) processes and may have negotiated pricing agreements. True indicates preferred status, False indicates standard qualified vendor.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date on which the vendor qualification was officially approved and became effective. Represents the start of the qualification validity period.']
  qualification_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the qualification record, used for external communication and tracking. Format: VQ-YYYYNNNN where YYYY is year and NNNN is sequence.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor qualification. Pending indicates submission received, under review indicates active evaluation, approved indicates full qualification granted, conditionally approved requires specific conditions to be met, rejected indicates disqualification, expired indicates qualification period ended, suspended indicates temporary hold, and withdrawn indicates vendor or company cancelled the qualification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|under review|approved|conditionally approved|rejected|expired|suspended|withdrawn — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  qualification_type varchar [note: 'Type of qualification assessment being performed. Pre-qualification is initial screening, full qualification is comprehensive assessment, re-qualification is periodic renewal, conditional qualification requires remediation, and expedited qualification is fast-track for urgent needs.']
  reinstatement_date date [note: 'Date on which a previously suspended vendor qualification was reinstated to active status after remediation of suspension issues. Null if qualification has never been suspended or has not been reinstated.']
  sanctions_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions screening against Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC), United Nations (UN), European Union (EU), and other sanctions lists. Cleared indicates no matches found, flagged indicates potential match requiring investigation, pending indicates screening in progress, and not screened indicates screening not yet performed.']
  scope_of_supply varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the goods or services the vendor is qualified to provide. May include drilling services, Engineering Procurement and Construction (EPC), Front-End Engineering Design (FEED), catalyst supply, Maintenance Repair and Operations (MRO) materials, oilfield equipment, refinery equipment, petrochemical feedstocks, or specialized technical services.']
  sole_source_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is the sole qualified source for the specified commodity category, typically due to proprietary technology, intellectual property rights, or unique capability. Sole source vendors may be exempt from competitive bidding requirements. True indicates sole source, False indicates competitive market.']
  strategic_vendor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is designated as a strategic partner with long-term relationship agreements, joint development programs, or integrated supply chain arrangements. Strategic vendors typically support critical operations or provide unique capabilities. True indicates strategic status, False indicates transactional relationship.']
  suspension_date date [note: 'Date on which the vendor qualification was suspended. Null if qualification has never been suspended.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation for temporary suspension of vendor qualification. May include ongoing investigation of Health Safety and Environment (HSE) incident, quality non-conformance, contract dispute, financial concerns, or regulatory enforcement action. Null if qualification is not suspended.']
  technical_evaluation_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score representing the vendor\'s technical capability assessment. Typically scored on a 0-100 scale based on technical expertise, equipment capability, process maturity, quality systems, and past performance. Used to rank vendors for Request for Quotation (RFQ) issuance.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the individual who created the vendor qualification record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Qualification and pre-qualification assessment record for vendors seeking approval to supply goods or services to Oil Gas, including Approved Vendor List (AVL) status and commodity-level approval scope. Captures qualification reference number, vendor ID, qualification type (pre-qualification, full qualification, re-qualification), scope of supply (drilling services, EPC, catalyst supply, MRO), commodity category code, approved value limit (USD), qualification date, expiry date, qualification status (pending, approved, conditionally approved, rejected, expired, suspended), technical evaluation score, financial evaluation score, HSE evaluation score, QHSE certification references (ISO 9001, ISO 14001, ISO 45001), API certification numbers, evaluator name, approval authority, approval scope (global, regional, asset-specific), preferred vendor flag, strategic vendor flag, sole source flag, and disqualification reason. Supports COPAS and API vendor qualification standards and governs vendor selection eligibility for RFQ issuance and direct award decisions.'
}

Table procurement.material_master {
  material_master_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the material master record. Primary key for the material master catalog.']
  petroleum_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.petroleum_product. Business justification: Material master records for petroleum products enable procurement of crude grades, refined products, NGLs for operations or trading. Links commodity product specifications to procurement system for so']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'ABC inventory classification based on consumption value and criticality. A items are high-value or critical, B items are moderate, C items are low-value or non-critical. Drives inventory control policies and procurement strategies.']
  api_specification varchar [note: 'API specification number or standard that the material conforms to. Ensures materials meet industry technical requirements for oil and gas operations.']
  base_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Primary unit of measure for inventory management and procurement. Standard units include barrels for crude oil, gallons for refined products, pounds or kilograms for chemicals and catalysts, and each for equipment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|LB|KG|GAL|L|BBL|FT|M|TON|MT — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  batch_management_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material requires batch or lot number tracking for quality control, traceability, and recall management. Typically required for chemicals, catalysts, and consumables.']
  copas_material_classification varchar [note: 'COPAS standard material classification code used for joint venture accounting, authorization for expenditure tracking, and joint interest billing. Ensures consistent material categorization across joint operating agreements.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating where the material is manufactured or produced. Required for customs declarations, trade compliance, and tariff classification.']
  created_date date [note: 'Date when the material master record was first created in the system. Used for master data governance and audit trails.']
  critical_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is critical for operations with limited alternative sources or long lead times. Critical materials receive priority in procurement planning and inventory management.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the standard cost. Typically USD for U.S. operations but may vary for international subsidiaries.']
  h2s_service_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is rated for use in hydrogen sulfide service environments. Critical for equipment and materials used in sour gas production and processing.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is classified as hazardous under EPA, OSHA, or PHMSA regulations. Triggers special handling, storage, and transportation requirements.']
  height_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Height dimension of the material in meters. Used for storage space planning and transportation logistics.']
  hts_code varchar [note: 'Harmonized tariff schedule code for customs classification and duty calculation. Required for international procurement and import/export compliance.']
  inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether incoming materials must undergo quality inspection before being released to inventory. Ensures materials meet specifications before use in operations.']
  last_modified_date date [note: 'Date when the material master record was last updated. Used for master data governance, change tracking, and audit trails.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days from purchase order creation to material receipt. Used for materials requirements planning and inventory replenishment calculations.']
  length_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Length dimension of the material in meters. Used for storage space planning and transportation logistics for tubular goods, pipe, and long equipment.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the original equipment manufacturer or producer of the material. Used for vendor qualification, quality assurance, and sourcing decisions.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'Original equipment manufacturer part number for the material. Used for cross-referencing, warranty claims, and ensuring correct replacement parts are procured.']
  material_category varchar [note: 'High-level business category for the material aligned with oil and gas operational domains. Used for spend analytics, sourcing strategies, and procurement reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: drilling_services|oilfield_equipment|refinery_catalyst|maintenance_material|rig_consumable|safety_equipment|chemical_additive|tubular_goods — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the material including technical specifications, grade, and distinguishing characteristics. Used for procurement requisitions and purchase orders.']
  material_group varchar [note: 'Material group code used for grouping materials with similar characteristics for procurement analytics, spend analysis, and vendor sourcing strategies. Aligns with commodity codes.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique material number assigned by the enterprise resource planning system. This is the business identifier used across procurement, inventory, and supply chain processes.']
  material_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the material master record. Controls whether the material can be procured, issued, or used in operations.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the material by procurement and inventory management category. Determines procurement processes, valuation methods, and inventory control procedures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: raw_material|spare_part|chemical|catalyst|consumable|capital_equipment|service|trading_good — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered from vendors in the base unit of measure. Drives procurement planning and inventory optimization.']
  norm_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material contains naturally occurring radioactive material commonly found in oil and gas production equipment and waste streams. Requires special disposal and handling procedures.']
  plant_specific_status varchar [note: 'Material status at the plant or facility level, which may differ from the enterprise-wide status. Allows local control of material availability for specific locations.']
  procurement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the material is procured externally from vendors, manufactured in-house, or both. Drives procurement planning and sourcing strategies.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Code identifying the procurement team or buyer responsible for sourcing this material. Used for workload distribution and procurement accountability.']
  serial_number_profile varchar [note: 'Indicates whether serial number tracking is required, optional, or not applicable for this material. Mandatory for capital equipment and high-value assets requiring individual traceability.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days the material can be stored before it expires or degrades. Critical for chemicals, catalysts, and consumables with limited storage life.']
  standard_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard unit cost of the material in the base currency used for inventory valuation, cost accounting, and variance analysis. Updated periodically based on procurement history and market conditions.']
  storage_conditions varchar [note: 'Required environmental conditions for safe storage of the material including temperature range, humidity controls, ventilation requirements, and segregation rules.']
  unspsc_code varchar [note: 'United Nations standard product and service classification code. Used for spend analytics, supplier management, and procurement category management.']
  valuation_class varchar [note: 'Accounting valuation class that determines the general ledger accounts for inventory postings, cost of goods sold, and material consumption. Links material master to financial accounting.']
  weight_kg decimal(15,3) [note: 'Net weight of one base unit of the material in kilograms. Used for transportation planning, freight cost calculation, and logistics optimization.']
  width_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Width dimension of the material in meters. Used for storage space planning and transportation logistics.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all materials, equipment, spare parts, chemicals, and consumables procured by Oil Gas. Aligned with SAP MM Material Master. Captures material number, material description, material type (raw material, spare part, chemical, catalyst, consumable, capital equipment), material group, base unit of measure, procurement type (external, in-house), valuation class, hazardous material flag, NORM flag, H2S service flag, API material specification, manufacturer part number, shelf life, storage conditions, weight, dimensions, country of origin, and COPAS material classification code. Serves as the SSOT for material identity across procurement and supply chain.'
}
